If a change touches `SKILL.md`, templates, scripts, references, or package inputs, decide whether `dist/kami.zip` must be refreshed before handoff. Shipped behavior is not ready until the package contains the changed files.

---

## Step 2 · Pick the document type

| User says | Document | CN template | EN template |
|---|---|---|---|
| "one-pager / 方案 / 执行摘要 / exec summary" | One-Pager | `one-pager.html` | `one-pager-en.html` |
| "white paper / 白皮书 / 长文 / 年度总结 / technical report" | Long Doc | `long-doc.html` | `long-doc-en.html` |
| "formal letter / 信件 / 辞职信 / 推荐信 / memo" | Letter | `letter.html` | `letter-en.html` |
| "portfolio / 作品集 / case studies" | Portfolio | `portfolio.html` | `portfolio-en.html` |
| "resume / CV / 简历 / 履歴書" | Resume | `resume.html` | `resume-en.html` |
| "slides / PPT / deck / 演示" | Slides | `slides-weasy.html` | `slides-weasy-en.html` |
| "个股研报 / equity report / 估值分析 / investment memo / 股票分析" | Equity Report | `equity-report.html` | `equity-report-en.html` |
| "更新日志 / changelog / release notes / 版本记录" | Changelog | `changelog.html` | `changelog-en.html` |
| "landing page / 落地页 / 官网 / product page / 产品页" | Landing Page | `landing-page.html` | `landing-page-en.html` |

> **Changelog vs. release notes**: The changelog template above is for styled document output. GitHub release notes are a separate deliverable; use `/write` with Release Note Template Mode.

> **Landing Page**: Screen-first interactive template. No PDF output. Includes gallery carousel with auto-rotate, hero entrance animation, responsive breakpoints (880px / 480px), and prefers-reduced-motion support. Deploy as static HTML to Vercel / Netlify / any host. The agent fills {{PLACEHOLDER}} values and HTML comment blocks, then saves as a ready-to-serve `.html` file.

> **Landing Page companion files**: For a production multilingual deploy, copy the five `landing-page-*.example` files alongside the main HTML, remove the `.example` suffix, and fill the placeholders. They cover Vercel rewrites and headers, sitemap hreflang, robots AI allowlist, and llms.txt + llms-full.txt for AI assistants. The main HTML already ships matching hreflang and og:locale in `<head>`; an Accept-Language redirect at the end of `landing-page-en.html` is commented out for opt-in. `{{SITE_ORIGIN}}` is the scheme + host of your `{{CANONICAL_URL}}` (e.g. `https://example.com`). See `references/design.md` Section 11 «Companion assets».

> Slides: default to `slides-weasy.html` / `slides-weasy-en.html` (WeasyPrint HTML → PDF). Use `slides.py` / `slides-en.py` only when the user explicitly requires an editable PPTX file. Use `assets/templates/marp/slides-marp(.md|.css)` only when the user explicitly asks for Marp / markdown slides / a deck that lives in a `.md` file.

> Deck recipe: read design.md Section 8 before drafting slides. Marp-specific constraints live in design.md §8 «Marp variant».

### Decision tree (use before asking)

Walk this tree before reaching for a one-liner question. Ask only when two cells genuinely both fit.

| Signal | Document |
|---|---|
| Length target unknown | Ask "how many pages" before classifying |
| ≤ 1 page + investor / recruiter / exec summary audience | one-pager |
| ≤ 1 page + formal correspondence (sales, hiring, resignation, memo) | letter |
| 1.5-2 pages + career narrative + project bullets | resume |
| 3-6 pages + project showcase + visual heavy | portfolio |
| 6-15 pages + sustained argument + low visual density | long-doc |
| Presentation flow + speaker support + per-slide assertion | slides |
| Financial / metrics dashboard + thesis + price or risk view | equity-report |
| Version-by-version log + release facts | changelog |
| Product showcase + pricing + screenshots + FAQ for browser | landing-page |

Ambiguity examples that justify a one-liner:
- "1.5 page career story with heavy visuals" -> ask "resume or portfolio?"
- "2 page exec summary with metric tiles" -> ask "one-pager or equity-report?"
- "5 page argument with several charts" -> ask "long-doc or portfolio?"

Pick from the tree first. Ask only when the tree is genuinely silent.

### Diagrams (primitives, not a separate template type)

When the user asks for **a diagram inside** a long-doc / portfolio / slide (not a standalone document), route to `assets/diagrams/` rather than a template:

| User says | Diagram | Template |
|---|---|---|
| "架构图 / architecture / 系统图 / components diagram" | Architecture | `assets/diagrams/architecture.html` |
| "流程图 / flowchart / 决策流 / branching logic" | Flowchart | `assets/diagrams/flowchart.html` |
| "象限图 / quadrant / 优先级矩阵 / 2×2 matrix" | Quadrant | `assets/diagrams/quadrant.html` |
| "柱状图 / bar chart / 分类对比 / grouped bars" | Bar Chart | `assets/diagrams/bar-chart.html` |
| "折线图 / line chart / 趋势 / 股价 / time series" | Line Chart | `assets/diagrams/line-chart.html` |
| "环形图 / donut / pie / 占比 / 分布结构" | Donut Chart | `assets/diagrams/donut-chart.html` |
| "状态机 / state machine / 状态图 / lifecycle" | State Machine | `assets/diagrams/state-machine.html` |
| "时间线 / timeline / 里程碑 / milestones / roadmap" | Timeline | `assets/diagrams/timeline.html` |
| "泳道图 / swimlane / 跨角色流程 / cross-team flow" | Swimlane | `assets/diagrams/swimlane.html` |
| "树状图 / tree / hierarchy / 层级 / 组织架构" | Tree | `assets/diagrams/tree.html` |
| "分层图 / layer stack / 分层架构 / OSI / stack" | Layer Stack | `assets/diagrams/layer-stack.html` |
| "维恩图 / venn / 交集 / overlap / 集合关系" | Venn | `assets/diagrams/venn.html` |
| "K 线 / candlestick / OHLC / 股价走势 / price history" | Candlestick | `assets/diagrams/candlestick.html` |
| "瀑布图 / waterfall / 收入桥 / revenue bridge / decomposition" | Waterfall | `assets/diagrams/waterfall.html` |

Read `references/diagrams.md` before drawing - it has the selection guide, kami token map, and the AI-slop anti-pattern table. Extract the `<svg>` block from the template and drop it into a `<figure>` inside long-doc / portfolio.

Before drawing, always ask: **would a well-written paragraph teach the reader less than this diagram?** If no, don't draw.

**Auto-select charts from data.** When content contains numerical data, choose the chart type and embed it without waiting for the user to specify. Decision tree (first match wins):

| Data shape | Chart |
|---|---|
| Has open/high/low/close fields, or per-day price | Candlestick |
| Has + and - contributions that sum to a total (bridge, waterfall, P&L) | Waterfall |
| One series, values sum to ~100%, items ≤ 6 | Donut |
| One series, values sum to ~100%, items ≥ 7 | Horizontal bar |
| Two or more series across time (months, quarters, years) | Line |
| One series across time, large count changes dominate (not rate) | Bar |
| Multiple categories, same time snapshot, 2+ series | Grouped bar |
| 2×2 strategic or priority positioning | Quadrant |
| Hierarchical data with depth ≥ 2 | Tree |
| Process with decision branches | Flowchart |
| Cross-team or cross-role process with ≥ 3 actors | Swimlane |
| Set overlaps or shared attributes between 2-3 groups | Venn |
| Category comparison, single series, no time axis | Bar |

When data fits multiple types, prefer the one that shows variance most clearly. Always embed inside a `<figure>` with a caption that states the insight, not just the data range.

## Step 2.1 · Source and material pass

Run this before distilling or filling content when the document depends on facts or materials outside the user's draft. Skip it only for personal drafts where the user already supplied everything needed.

### Source check

Trigger when the document mentions a specific company, product, person, release date, version, funding round, metric, market fact, technical spec, or any current fact likely to change.

- Use primary sources before writing: user-provided material, official site, docs, filings, press release, app store page, or repo release
- Keep a short note of source names and dates for facts that drive the document
- If sources conflict or a fact cannot be checked quickly, ask the user instead of choosing silently
- Avoid current-sounding claims such as "latest", "recent", "new", version numbers, launch dates, or financial figures unless they are checked

### Material check

Trigger when the document is about a company, product, project, venue, or personal brand.

Confirm the materials that make the subject recognizable before layout:

| Need | Required when | Accept |
|---|---|---|
| Logo | Any branded document | User file or official SVG/PNG |
| Product image | Physical product / venue / object | Official image, user image, or marked gap |
| UI screenshot | App / SaaS / website / tool | Current screenshot, official product image, or user capture |
| Brand colors | Branded one-pager / portfolio / deck | Official value, extracted asset value, or keep kami ink-blue |
| Fonts | Only if brand typography matters | Official font, close system fallback, or kami default |

If a required item is missing, use a compact gap table and ask once. Do not replace missing material with generic imagery, approximate logo drawings, or invented values.

Logo fallback: when the request names no logo but the brand profile has a `logo` path, fill the commented `.brand-logo` slot in `one-pager` / `portfolio` / `slides-weasy` per `references/brand-profile.md` Layer C. Expand `~` to an absolute path, and if the file is missing or the template has no slot, leave it commented and render without a logo (never insert a broken image). An explicit logo in the current request always wins.

### Materials status block

After the material check, output a structured status block before continuing. This is a one-shot transparency display, not a question:

```
Materials status:
- Logo: OK assets/client-logo.svg
- Brand colors: OK #1B365D mapped to --brand
- Product screenshot: MISSING (proceeding with kami default placeholder)
- UI screenshot: not required for this doc type
```

Use `OK`, `MISSING`, or `not required`. If a required item is missing and no user input arrived, ask once with the gap table; otherwise continue silently.

## Step 2.5 · Distill raw content (if applicable)

**Auto-detect whether to distill.** Do not ask the user; judge from the input:

| Skip distill (fill directly) | Run distill |
|---|---|
| Content has explicit section labels matching template structure | Raw prose without section structure |
| Metrics already quantified with units in place | Numbers scattered or implied, not extracted |
| User wrote "use this as-is" / "直接用这个" / "原封不动" | User pasted multi-source dump (chat / email thread / multiple docs) |
| Content count matches template (e.g. 4 metrics for 4 metric cards) | Content count mismatches template (too many or too few items) |
| One coherent voice with consistent claims | Conflicting claims or duplicate facts across sources |

When in doubt, run distill. Distill is cheap; rebuilding a misaligned doc is not.

When the user hands over **raw material** (meeting notes, brain dump, existing doc in different format, chat transcript, scattered points):

1. **Extract**: pull out every factual claim, number, date, name, source, material reference, and action item
2. **Classify**: map each extract to the target template's sections (see `references/writing.md` for section structure per doc type)
3. **Gap-check**: list what the template needs but the raw content doesn't have - include missing facts, missing proof, and missing materials
4. **Ask once**: share the gap table with the user. Do not guess to fill gaps.

Example gap-check:

| Template needs | Found | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| 4 metric cards | "8 years", "50-person team" | 2 more quantifiable results |
| 3-5 core projects | 2 mentioned | at least 1 more with outcome |
| Materials | logo file provided | product screenshot source |

Then proceed to Step 2.6 (slides) or the layout note (all other doc types) with structured, distilled content.

## Step 2.6 · Deck pre-flight (slides only)

Skip this step for every doc type except slides.

### Path selection

Default to the WeasyPrint HTML path. Switch to pptx only if the user explicitly requires an editable PPTX file. Switch to Marp only when the user explicitly asks for Marp / markdown slides.

| Path | Template | When |
|---|---|---|
| WeasyPrint HTML → PDF (default) | `slides-weasy.html` / `slides-weasy-en.html` | All cases unless PPTX or Marp is required |
| python-pptx → PPTX (fallback) | `slides.py` / `slides-en.py` | User explicitly requires editable PPTX |
| Marp Markdown (variant) | `assets/templates/marp/slides-marp.md` (+ `slides-marp.css`) / `slides-marp-en.md` (+ `slides-marp-en.css`) | User explicitly asks for Marp, "markdown slides", or a `.md` deck. Shipped `.md` is a working demo of Kami Marp itself; copy it, swap content, keep the structure. Renders via local `marp` CLI; not bundled. |

### Page size

Default is `280mm 158mm`. Ask only if the user has mentioned length or density constraints.

| Size | When |
|---|---|
| `280mm 158mm` | Default; fits most decks |
| `297mm 167mm` | User wants a bit more room |
| `338mm 190mm` | Heavy content slide or many data points per page |

### Content pre-flight

Before drafting any slide, confirm these points with the user. Ask all at once, skip any already answered:

| # | Question |
|---|---|
| 1 | **Audience + venue** - who is in the room, and is it live keynote, investor 1:1, or async share link? |
| 2 | **Length target** - presentation time or slide count? (15 min: ~10 slides / 30 min: ~20 slides / 45 min: ~25-30 slides) |
| 3 | **Source material** - what content is already ready: outline, doc, notes, data? |
| 4 | **Images** - are screenshots, charts, logos, or product images available, or are gaps expected? |
| 5 | **Hard constraints** - brand colors, required logo, PPTX required, any slides that must exist? |
| 6 | **Format confirmation** - slides deck, or a one-pager that looks like a deck? |

### Content rules for slides

- No section divider slides: use `.eyebrow` for section numbering, not a dedicated blue-background page
- No CJK parentheses: replace `（...）` with `·` or `,`
- Each bullet fits one line: trim until it does
- 2×2 layouts: use `table.t2x2`, not CSS Grid
- Pinned conclusions: use `.co` at `position: absolute; bottom: 12mm`

These rules apply identically to Marp decks. Marp-specific syntax: see `references/design.md` §8 «Marp variant».

## Step 2.7 · Layout note (transparent, non-blocking)

Before loading specs and filling the template, write a short editor-style note stating the layout intent: template choice, length target, narrative arc, embedded diagrams, material status, and output formats. Match the document's language. Keep it under 80 words, written as prose, not a status panel. Continue immediately after; do not wait.

Example (CN):

> 排版意图：Equity Report 中文版，2 页 A4。先立论与目标价，进入估值 (DCF 与可比公司)，落于催化剂与风险。中段嵌一张营收趋势折线和 FY26 收入桥瀑布。Logo 已就位，产品图暂缺，header 改走纯文字。输出 HTML 与 PDF。

Example (EN):

> Layout intent: Equity Report (EN), two pages A4. Open with thesis and price target, run through valuation (DCF and comparables), close on catalysts and risks. A revenue line chart and an FY26 waterfall sit mid-doc. Logo is in hand; product image is absent, so the header stays text-only. Output: HTML and PDF.

The note is for transparency, not approval. If the user pushes back, adjust; otherwise proceed to Step 3.

## Step 3 · Load the right amount of spec

Pick the tier that matches the task. Default to the lowest tier that covers the work.

| Tier | When | Read |
|---|---|---|
| **Content-only** | Updating text, swapping bullets, translating an existing doc. CSS stays untouched. | `CHEATSHEET.md` only |
| **Layout tweak** | Adjusting spacing, moving sections, changing font size within spec. CSS touched. | `CHEATSHEET.md` + template (tokens already inline) |
| **New document** | Building from scratch or from raw content. | Full design spec + writing spec + template |
| **Resume content** | Resume-specific bullet structure, project framing, scope-result-outcome rules. | `resume-writing.md` + template |
| **Sources / materials** | Company, product, market, launch, funding, specs, or branded subject. | `writing.md` source rules + user/source material |
| **Deck (>20 slides)** | Long presentation needing Part Divider, Code Cards, section headers. | Full design spec + Deck Recipe (design.md section 8) |
| **Troubleshoot** | Rendering bug, font issue, page overflow. | `production.md` (+ design spec if CSS is the cause) |
| **Anti-patterns** | Reviewing AI-generated drafts before shipping. | `anti-patterns.md` (six-category checklist) |
| **Diagram** | Embedding SVG in a doc. | `diagrams.md` only (has its own token map) |

You can always escalate mid-task if the work turns out to need more than the initial tier.

The full spec files for reference:
- Design: `references/design.md`
- Writing (general): `references/writing.md`
- Writing (resume-specific): `references/resume-writing.md`
- Production: `references/production.md`
- Diagrams: `references/diagrams.md`
- Anti-patterns: `references/anti-patterns.md`

## Step 4 · Fill content into the template

- Copy the template into your working directory; don't write HTML from scratch
- **CSS stays untouched**, only edit the body
- Content follows `writing.md`: data over adjectives, distinctive phrasing over industry clichés
- Avoid patterns listed in `references/anti-patterns.md`: emptiness, fabrication, mimicry, excess, source gaps, tone contamination
- **Before filling, read the quality bar for your document type** in `writing.md` section "Quality bars by document type". Structure is necessary but not sufficient: a resume bullet needs Action + Scope + Result + Business Outcome; an equity report needs variant perception + quantified catalysts; slides need assertion-evidence titles. Meeting the quality bar is as important as filling every placeholder.

### Do not generate

These are the most common AI document failures. Cross-reference `references/anti-patterns.md` for the full list.

- Do not leave placeholder text in the final document ("Lorem ipsum", "[Insert here]", "TBD")
- Do not invent metrics, financial data, or statistics; mark gaps with `[DATA NEEDED: description]`
- Do not use stock-image descriptions as image placeholders ("A diverse team collaborating in a modern office")
- Do not pad content to fill template slots (a resume with 3 real projects does not need 5 fabricated ones)
- Do not write a paragraph that merely restates its own heading in sentence form

### Fill PDF metadata (WeasyPrint reads these into the PDF)

Every template has meta placeholders in `<head>`. Fill all four before building:

| Placeholder (CN) | Placeholder (EN) | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| `{{作者}}` | `{{AUTHOR}}` | Resume/letter/portfolio: use the person's name from the doc. All others: leave as-is (build script infers from git config or env) |
| `{{摘要}}` | `{{DESCRIPTION}}` | Extract one sentence (≤150 chars) from the first 2 paragraphs |
| `{{关键词}}` | `{{KEYWORDS}}` | 3-5 keywords from the title + section headings, comma-separated |
| `{{文档标题}}` / `{{信件主题}}` etc. | `{{DOC_TITLE}}` / `{{LETTER_SUBJECT}}` etc. | Infer from the H1 or `.header .title` text |

`<meta name="generator" content="Kami">` is already fixed in the template; do not change it.

**Author inference**: `build.py` automatically sets PDF `/Author` metadata from:
1. `git config user.name` (primary)
2. `KAMI_AUTHOR` environment variable (fallback)
3. `"Kami"` (final fallback)

For personal documents (resume/letter/portfolio), the HTML `<meta name="author">` should match the person's name in the content. For non-personal documents (one-pager/long-doc), leave the placeholder as-is and let the build script infer it.

## Step 4.1 · Per-page density target (multi-page templates only)

适用：slides-weasy / long-doc / portfolio / equity-report / changelog。不适用 resume / one-pager / letter（这些有独立的长度合约）。

正文页填充率目标 60-80%。封面 / 目录 / 末尾署名页豁免。这条规则解决的是 AI 生成多页文档时最常见的 draft 缺陷：把内容拆得太散，结果几页都填不满。

### Items-per-page contract

| Template | Typical body page | Hard floor (merge if below) |
|---|---|---|
| slides-weasy | 1 assertion title + 3-5 supporting items, or 1 chart + 2-3 callouts | <3 items and no chart → merge into adjacent slide |
| long-doc | 1 chapter heading + 2-4 paragraphs + at most 1 figure | Chapter renders to <40% page → merge into neighbor chapter |
| portfolio | 1 project header + 1 hero image + 3-5 outcome bullets | No image and <3 outcomes → merge with adjacent project |
| equity-report | 1 section + 1 table/chart + supporting prose | Only a 2-row table on the page → combine sections |
| changelog | 1 version block + 4-8 entries | Version has <4 entries → place on the same page as the prior version |

### Sparse-page merge rule

Before finalizing, scan the draft. Any body page that would render under 50% full → apply one of, in order:

1. Merge upward into the previous section.
2. Merge downward into the next section.
3. Promote a list to a small diagram or table that earns the space.
4. Pin a `.co` callout to bottom (slides-weasy only). Whitespace above a pinned callout is intentional, not sparse.

Forbidden ways to "fill" a sparse page: padding with filler prose, repeating the heading as a sentence, inventing statistics, restating the prior page in different words. If the merge options don't apply, the page itself shouldn't exist.

### Last-page exemption

The last body page is allowed to run 40-60% fill. Forcing balance on the last page usually means padding. The colophon / closing slide may have any fill level.

### Verify after build

```bash
python3 scripts/build.py --check-density   # flags >25% (WARN) / >50% (SPARSE) trailing whitespace
```

If a body page (not cover, not last page) gets a SPARSE warning, treat it as a draft defect and re-author with the merge rule.

## Step 4.5 · Auto-select output format

Do not ask the user which format to export. Decide from context:

| Signal | Output | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Any document request | HTML + PDF | PDF is the default deliverable, HTML is the source |
| Slides / PPT / deck | HTML + PDF + PPTX | Presentations need a projectable format |
| "分享" / "发朋友圈" / "share" / "post" / "preview" | + PNG | Social platforms and messaging need images |
| "嵌入" / "插图" / "embed in another doc" | PNG only | Used as material inside other documents |
| User explicitly says a format | Follow the user | Explicit request overrides auto-selection |

PDF always ships for document templates. Landing pages ship as a ready-to-serve static HTML file. PPTX follows slides. PNG follows sharing context. The user should never need to think about formats.

## Step 5 · Build & verify

```bash
python3 scripts/build.py --verify           # build all templates + page count + font check + slides
python3 scripts/build.py --verify resume-en # single target full verification
python3 scripts/build.py landing-page        # screen-first static HTML template check
python3 scripts/build.py --verify slides    # single slide deck verification
python3 scripts/build.py --check-placeholders path/to/filled.html
python3 scripts/build.py --check-density              # page whitespace scanner (skips cover)
python3 scripts/build.py --check            # CSS rule violations only (fast, no build)
```

Source templates intentionally keep `{{...}}` fields. Run placeholder checks on completed documents, not on the template library.

Visual anomalies (tag double rectangle, font fallback, page break issues) -> `production.md` Part 4.

## Fonts

**Chinese**
- Main serif: Source Han Serif SC (思源宋体) — `NotoSerifSC-Regular.otf` (400 weight) + `NotoSerifSC-Medium.otf` (500 weight, real bold). Noto Serif SC is Google's twin of Adobe Source Han Serif; the `@font-face` family `"Source Han Serif SC"` maps to these bundled OTFs, and the OFL license allows commercial use
- Templates use dual @font-face declarations: Regular for body text, Medium for headings
- Bundled OTFs are git-tracked for local preview and CDN fallback, but excluded from Claude Desktop skill ZIPs (run `bash scripts/ensure-fonts.sh` to recover them when missing)
- Fallback chain baked into templates: Source Han Serif SC -> Noto Serif SC -> Noto Serif CJK SC -> Songti SC -> STSong -> TsangerJinKai02 (deeper fallback, license required for commercial use) -> Georgia

**Japanese (best-effort)**
- Uses CJK template path, no dedicated `-ja` templates yet
- JP Mincho-first stack: YuMincho -> Hiragino Mincho ProN -> Noto Serif CJK JP -> Source Han Serif JP -> TsangerJinKai02 -> serif
- Visually verify line breaks, punctuation rhythm, and emphasis weight before shipping

**English**
- Single serif: Charter (system-bundled, macOS/iOS), used for both headlines and body
- No separate sans: `--sans: var(--serif)`, one font per page
- Fallback: Georgia (cross-platform) / Palatino / Times New Roman

Font files next to HTML with relative `@font-face` paths is the most stable setup. `scripts/package-skill.sh` excludes the bundled font binaries (NotoSerifSC OTFs, TsangerJinKai TTFs, Source Han Serif K OTFs) from the Claude Desktop ZIP, so the uploaded package stays ~4.3MB. Always upload that `package-skill.sh` output, never a hand-zipped checkout (the tracked font files make it much larger and Claude Desktop rejects the upload).

**Font auto-recovery (Claude Desktop)**

Before building Chinese documents, ensure fonts are present. The script tries multiple CDN sources with retry and size validation:

```bash
bash scripts/ensure-fonts.sh
```

It downloads to the XDG user font dir (`${XDG_DATA_HOME:-~/.local/share}/fonts/kami`, override with `KAMI_FONT_DIR`), **not** into the skill's `assets/fonts` -- that keeps the installed skill small so Claude Desktop never trips its size limit. fontconfig scans that dir by default, so WeasyPrint finds `TsangerJinKai02` there; online renders fall back to the jsDelivr `@font-face` URL. Run once before building. If all sources fail, the script suggests installing Source Han Serif SC as fallback.

## Feedback protocol

When the user gives **vague visual feedback** ("looks off", "太挤了", "not elegant"), do not guess. Ask back with current values:

| User says | Ask about |
|---|---|
| "太挤了" / "too cramped" | Which element? Line-height (current: X)? Padding (current: Y)? Page margin? |
| "太松了" / "too loose" | Same direction, reversed |
| "颜色不对" / "color feels wrong" | Which element? Brand blue overused? A gray reading too cool? |
| "不够好看" / "not polished" | Font rendering? Alignment? Whitespace distribution? Hierarchy unclear? |
| "看着不专业" / "unprofessional" | Content wording? Or layout (alignment, consistency)? |

Template response: "X is currently set to Y. Would you like (a) [specific alternative within spec] or (b) [another option]?"

Never say "I'll adjust the spacing" without naming the exact property and its new value.

---

## When not to use this skill

- User explicitly wants Material / Fluent / Tailwind default - different design language
- Need dark / cyberpunk / futurist aesthetic (this is deliberately anti-future)
- Need saturated multi-color (this has one accent)
- Need cartoon / animation / illustration style (this is editorial)
- Web dynamic app UI (this is for print / static documents)

---

Next: **apply Step 3's tier table to decide what to read**, then copy the matching template and start filling.

name: lint-python
description: Use when the user wants to systematically add type hints or fix ruff/ty lints across a Python project, in small batches per round, committing after each. Triggers include "fix python lints", "add type hints", "fix ruff warnings", "clean up type annotations", "run ruff fix", "fix ty errors", "batch fix python lint", "lint the python code", or any cron job for Python lint maintenance. Trigger even if ruff/ty aren't named explicitly.
body:
# Python Type Hint & Ruff/Ty Lint Fix

You are a batch Python lint fixer. Your job is to add type hints and fix ruff/ty lints across a Python
project, processing a small batch of files per round, committing and pushing after each round.

## Prerequisites

Before starting, verify the project is Python-based:

1. Check for `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, or `setup.cfg` in the working directory.
2. Check that `ruff` is available: `ruff --version`.
3. Optionally check for `ty`: `ty --version`.
4. Check for type checkers: `mypy --version` or `pyright --version`.
5. Check for test command: look at `justfile` for `just test`, or fall back to `uv run pytest` / `pytest`.

If the project is not Python, tell the user this skill is for Python projects.

## Ensure test files are excluded from linting

Before scanning, check `pyproject.toml` for `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]`. If the section is missing
or does not already exclude test files, add/update it so test code is never linted:

```toml
[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"tests/**" = ["ALL"]
"**/tests/**" = ["ALL"]
"**/test_*.py" = ["ALL"]
"e2e/**" = ["ALL"]
"**/e2e/**" = ["ALL"]
```

If the section already exists, merge these entries without removing existing per-file-ignores.
Also skip any file matching these patterns during the scan and fix phases — never process test files.

## Load the prompt template

Read the prompt template at `/Users/junix/projects/crates/prompt-alias-rs/templates/type-hint-ruff.md`.
This is the authoritative instruction set — follow it as the execution rules.

## Workflow Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Scan: ruff check + ty check │
│  Identify files with lints   │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Pick ≤5 files (≤20 changes │
│  per file) — priority order │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Fix type hints & lints     │
│  (ANN/F/B/ARG/UP/ty)        │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Verify: ruff + ty + test   │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Pass? → commit & push      │
│  Fail? → git restore, skip  │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              ▼
         All done?
         ├─ Yes → output "Stop this cron job"
         └─ No  → next round
```

## Quick Reference: Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Batch size | ≤5 files per round |
| Per-file limit | ≤20 changes per file |
| API safety | Never change public function signatures, `__all__`, dataclass fields, Protocol, TypedDict, re-exports |
| Type syntax | Use `X \| None` (not `Optional[X]`), `X \| Y` (not `Union`) — consistent with UP rules |
| Lint scope | ANN, F, B, ARG, UP (+ ty if configured) |
| Skip tests/e2e | Never lint test or e2e files — ensure `per-file-ignores` excludes `tests/**`, `**/tests/**`, `**/test_*.py`, `e2e/**`, `**/e2e/**` |
| Skip style | No E501, W-series, or formatting-only fixes |
| Test failure | `git restore` changed files, record reason, skip to next |
| Blocked after 2 fails | Mark file as blocked, move on |
| Commit format | `chore: add type hints & fix ruff lints in <module>` |
| Cron stop | `ruff check . --select ANN,F,B,ARG` exit code 0; or 2 consecutive no-op rounds |

## Output Format

After each round, report:

```
## Round <N> Summary
- **Files processed**: <list>
- **Type hints added**: <brief summary>
- **Lints fixed**: <ruff codes or ty errors>
- **Skipped**: <files and reasons>
- **Verification**: ruff ✓/✗ | ty ✓/✗ | tests ✓/✗
- **Commit**: <message>
- **Remaining**: ~<N> files with lint issues
- **Cron**: continue / cancel
```

name: lint-rust
description: Use when the user wants to systematically fix Rust clippy warnings across a project, in small batches per round, committing and pushing after each. Triggers include "fix clippy warnings", "fix rust lints", "clean up clippy", "resolve clippy", "lint the rust code", "batch fix rust lint", or any cron job for Rust lint maintenance. Trigger even if clippy isn't named explicitly.
body:
# Rust Clippy Lint Fix

You are a batch Rust clippy lint fixer. Your job is to resolve clippy warnings across a Rust project,
processing a small batch of files per round, committing and pushing after each round.

## Theoretical grounding

Clippy is not a refactoring catalog — it is a **mechanical enforcer of micro-level invariants**. Most of its lints map to small, well-known code smells; a few map to micro-refactorings from Fowler:

| Clippy category | Smell or refactoring it enforces |
|---|---|
| `correctness` | Logic bugs — not a smell, an actual defect |
| `suspicious` | **Inappropriate intimacy** / **mysterious name** — code that *might* work but reads wrong |
| `complexity` | **Long method**, **dead code**, **speculative generality**, **needless complexity** |
| `perf` | Micro-level **performance smell** (needless allocation, redundant clone) |
| `style` (skipped) | Aesthetic preference, not behavior-preserving in the Fowler sense |

The "API safety" rule (never change `pub fn` signatures, `pub struct` fields, etc.) is the **Open-Closed Principle** in disguise: this skill is closed for modification of the public surface, open only for internal cleanup. That's why a behavior-preserving lint fix is still safe even without downstream test coverage — the contract didn't move.

What this skill explicitly does **not** do:
- It does not introduce new abstractions (that's `run-crap` territory).
- It does not move code across files (that's `split-rust-module`).
- It does not extend or shrink the public API (that's `subtract-rust` for removal, or a higher-risk skill for additions).

If a clippy fix would require any of the above to compile cleanly, **skip it**, mark it blocked, and route up.

## Handoff invariants

**Preconditions** (from Phase 0 `audit-rust-architecture` or the user):
- `.refactor-baseline/build.txt`, `.refactor-baseline/test.txt`, `.refactor-baseline/public-api.txt`, `.refactor-baseline/features.txt` exist (or equivalent facts are supplied).
- `subtract-rust` has run (or is irrelevant): dead-code warnings won't outnumber clippy warnings, so the lint signal stays clear.
- The user has agreed on the lint scope (correctness / suspicious / complexity / perf — skip style unless requested).
- The audit's role classification has been read: items in **domain** modules get tighter API-stability scrutiny than items in adapter/infrastructure modules.

**Postconditions:**
- No `correctness`, `suspicious`, `complexity`, or `perf` warnings remain — except items behind `#[allow(clippy::...)]` with a written justification.
- `cargo build && cargo test` is still green under every feature combination in `features.txt`.
- Public API (`pub fn` signatures, `pub struct` fields, `pub enum` variants, `pub trait` methods, `pub use` re-exports) is unchanged. Verified by `cargo public-api --simplified` diff against `.refactor-baseline/public-api.txt` when the crate is a library.

## Prerequisites

Before starting, verify the project is Rust-based:

1. Check for `Cargo.toml` in the working directory or parent directories.
2. Check that `cargo clippy` is available: `cargo clippy --version`.
3. Check test command: `cargo test` (or look at `justfile` for `just test`).

If the project is not Rust, tell the user this skill is for Rust projects.

## Load the prompt template

Read the prompt template at `/Users/junix/projects/crates/prompt-alias-rs/templates/rust-clippy-fix.md`.
This is the authoritative instruction set — follow it as the execution rules.

## Workflow Overview

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Scan: cargo clippy -- -W all    │
│  Identify files with warnings    │
└──────────────┬───────────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pick ≤5 files (≤20 changes     │
│  per file) — priority order      │
└──────────────┬───────────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Fix clippy lints                │
│  (correctness/suspicious/        │
│   complexity/perf)               │
└──────────────┬───────────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Verify: clippy + cargo test     │
└──────────────┬───────────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pass? → commit & push           │
│  Fail? → git restore, skip       │
└──────────────┬───────────────────┘
               ▼
          All done?
          ├─ Yes → output "Stop this cron job"
          └─ No  → next round
```

## Quick Reference: Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Batch size | ≤5 files per round |
| Per-file limit | ≤20 changes per file |
| API safety | Never change `pub fn` signatures, `pub struct` fields, `pub enum` variants, `pub trait` methods, `pub type` aliases, `pub const`/`pub static`, `pub use` re-exports |
| Lint scope | correctness, suspicious, complexity, perf |
| Explicitly skip | `needless_pass_by_value`, `must_use_candidate`, style lints, `help: consider` suggestions |
| Cross-file lints | If a fix requires coordinated edits across files to keep compilation green, handle them together in one round |
| Test failure | `git restore` changed files, record reason, skip to next |
| Blocked after 2 fails | `#[allow(clippy::...)]` with comment, mark as blocked |
| Commit format | `fix(clippy): <brief description>` |
| Cron stop | `cargo clippy -- -W clippy::all 2>&1 \| grep -E "^warning\|^error"` no matches; or 2 consecutive no-op rounds |

## Architectural stop conditions (route up)

Clippy fixes are local. If the same clippy fix recurs across many files, or a clippy suggestion would require touching cross-module structure, the cause is **architectural**, not lint-level:

- The same `needless_collect` / `needless_borrow` / `redundant_clone` fix appears in 5+ files with the same shape → there's a shared abstraction missing. Route to `run-crap` to find the dispatch site.
- A `large_enum_variant` warning where the variant carries layered data → the type wants splitting. Route to `split-rust-module`.
- A `too_many_arguments` warning that can't be fixed without introducing a struct that spans modules → route to `run-crap` for Context-struct extraction.
- A `suspicious` lint that exposes an inconsistency between two modules' contracts → route to `audit-rust-architecture` (when available) or pause for the user.

When this happens, **stop the lint round**, record the routing reason in the round summary, and let the user decide whether to switch skills.

## API Escape Hatch

When a clippy lint would require an API change to fix:

```rust
//#[allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_value)]  // API: changing &X to &X would break downstream caller at crate::foo::bar
```

Always include a comment explaining *why* the lint was suppressed — this keeps the audit trail clear for the next person (or the next cron round).

## Output Format

After each round, report:

```
## Round <N> Summary
- **Files processed**: <list>
- **Lints fixed**: <lint names and locations>
- **Skipped**: <lints/files and reasons — API, blocked, etc.>
- **Verification**: clippy ✓/✗ | tests ✓/✗
- **Commit**: <message>
- **Remaining**: ~<N> lints
- **Cron**: continue / cancel
```

name: llm-bill-query
description: Use when the user wants to query historical LLM billing records under `/Users/junix/sync/llm-costs` via the `llm-bill` Rust CLI — analyzing, inspecting, counting, grouping, filtering, exporting, or debugging bills with SQL or the `llm-bill query` command. Also when explaining bill paths (`LLMS_COST_DIR`), per-agent import state under `~/.session-imports` / `LLM_BILL_*_SESSION_STATE_DIR`, Codex per-session checkpoints (`last_billed_usage`, `last_billed_event_timestamp` under `~/.session-imports/codex`), or `llm-bill migrate --agent` in relation to queries. For modifying pricing tags or repairing past bills, use `llm-pricing-audit` instead.
body:
# LLM Bill Query

Use this skill to answer historical LLM billing questions with the local Rust
CLI at `/Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill`.

Default data source:

- Bills directory: `/Users/junix/sync/llm-costs`
- CLI checkout: `/Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill`
- SQL table name: `bills`

### Bills directory vs import state

`llm-bill query` only reads **bill JSON** under the cost directory. Imports also maintain **per-agent import state** (fingerprints / cursors) so repeated `import` runs do not duplicate historical rows. That state lives under `~/.session-imports/...` by default, not inside `llm-costs`.

**Bills (what `query` reads):** directory is `~/sync/llm-costs` unless overridden by `LLMS_COST_DIR`.

Per-agent **session / day state** directories (used by `import` and `migrate`):

| Agent | Env var | Default path |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CC (ccusage) | `LLM_BILL_CCUSAGE_DAY_STATE_DIR` | `~/.session-imports/cc` |
| Codex | `LLM_BILL_CODEX_SESSION_STATE_DIR` | `~/.session-imports/codex` |
| Copilot | `LLM_BILL_COPILOT_SESSION_STATE_DIR` | `~/.session-imports/copilot` |
| Cursor | `LLM_BILL_CURSOR_SESSION_STATE_DIR` | `~/.session-imports/cursor` |
| Gemini | `LLM_BILL_GEMINI_SESSION_STATE_DIR` | `~/.session-imports/gemini` |

**Migrate** seeds import state and the global tracker from **the same agent telemetry `import` uses** (and aligns dates via `merged_tracker_day_after_dates`) so a later matching `import` does not re-ingest already-imported history:

```bash
llm-bill migrate --agent codex
llm-bill migrate --agent cc --dryrun
llm-bill migrate --agent cursor --json
```

`--agent` values: `cc` (CLI aliases `claude-code`, `claude`), `codex`, `copilot`, `cursor`, `gemini`. Useful flags: `--dryrun`, `--test` (built-in sample data), `--verbose`, `--tracker` / `--root` (see `llm-bill migrate --help`). Codex session root: `CODEX_HOME` (default `~/.codex`).

**Codex per-session checkpoint (`~/.session-imports/codex/{session_id}.json`).** Import bills **increments** between consecutive `token_count` cumulative snapshots; each increment is attributed to the **local `(date, hour)` of the later event** whose snapshot completes that delta. The cursor is **not** “whole session on `started_at` day”. Persisted fields (conceptually): **`last_billed_usage`** — full cumulative `total_token_usage` through the last `token_count` line that was fully rolled into cost files; **`last_billed_event_timestamp`** — RFC 3339 timestamp of that same line (telemetry checkpoint). Older files may lack those keys; the importer falls back to legacy `last_total_tokens` / `last_last_activity_at` when reconstructing the baseline. **`migrate --agent codex`** seeds **`last_billed_usage`** / **`last_billed_event_timestamp`** and sets the global tracker using **local dates from token_count events** (merged with any existing tracker), not session `started_at`. Default import skips **today** unless `--include-today`, matching the “no cost attributed to the current local calendar day” rule.

If the user needs to **change model pricing or repair wrong costs**, use `llm-pricing-audit`, not migrate/query.

## Core Rule

Prefer real `llm-bill query` runs over reasoning from memory. The bill corpus is
local, mutable, and date-sensitive.

```bash
llm-bill query 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
```

Use the checkout form only when the installed `llm-bill` binary is missing,
stale, or you are specifically validating uninstalled local edits:

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill
cargo run -- query 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
```

If installed-binary behavior differs from the checkout, trust the checkout
command and report the mismatch.

## Table Schema

The `bills` SQL table is flattened from `cost-*.json` files:

| Column | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `file` | Source JSON file name |
| `date` | Bill date from filename, `YYYY-MM-DD` |
| `hour` | Hour bucket, `0..23` |
| `model` | Persisted model key |
| `count` | Call count |
| `prompt_tokens` | Input token total |
| `completion_tokens` | Output token total |
| `total_tokens` | Prompt + completion tokens |
| `price` | Persisted row cost |
| `currency` | Persisted currency, often `CNY` or empty for old rows |

Quote `date` in SQL when in doubt, because it can collide with SQL date syntax:

```sql
select "date", sum(price) from bills group by "date";
```

## Common Queries

Total rows:

```bash
llm-bill query 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
```

Top models by spend:

```bash
llm-bill query \
  'select model, sum(count) as calls, sum(price) as cost
   from bills
   group by model
   order by cost desc
   limit 20'
```

Daily spend for a date range:

```bash
llm-bill query --from 2026-03-01 --to 2026-03-31 \
  'select "date", sum(price) as cost
   from bills
   group by "date"
   order by "date"'
```

Model family search:

```bash
llm-bill query \
  "select \"date\", model, sum(price) as cost, sum(count) as calls
   from bills
   where lower(model) like '%glm%'
   group by \"date\", model
   order by \"date\", cost desc"
```

Zero-cost token-bearing rows:

```bash
llm-bill query \
  'select file, "date", hour, model, count, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
   from bills
   where price = 0 and total_tokens > 0
   order by "date", hour, model'
```

Old empty rows:

```bash
llm-bill query \
  'select file, "date", hour, model, count
   from bills
   where price = 0 and total_tokens = 0
   order by "date", hour, model'
```

## Output Formats

Default output is a table:

```bash
llm-bill query 'select model, sum(price) as cost from bills group by model limit 5'
```

Use JSON lines for scripts:

```bash
llm-bill query --format json 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
```

Use CSV for spreadsheet or shell pipelines:

```bash
llm-bill query --format csv \
  'select model, sum(price) as cost from bills group by model order by cost desc'
```

## Date Filtering

Prefer `--from` and `--to` for broad date windows. They filter files before SQL
execution and are inclusive:

```bash
llm-bill query --from 2026-05-01 --to 2026-05-07 \
  'select count(*) as rows, sum(price) as cost from bills'
```

Use SQL `where` clauses for more complex filters:

```bash
llm-bill query \
  "select model, sum(price) as cost
   from bills
   where \"date\" >= '2026-05-01' and model like '%gpt%'
   group by model"
```

When composing shell commands, quote carefully. Use double quotes around SQL
when the SQL contains string literals such as `'%glm%'`, and escape identifier
quotes such as `\"date\"`.

## Safety

- `llm-bill query` is read-only. Do not edit bill JSON files unless the user
  explicitly asks for repair or cleanup.
- Do not use SQL as a mutation interface; it only registers an in-memory
  DataFusion table.
- When the user asks for exact counts or costs, run the query live and include
  the concrete output.
- If query results look empty unexpectedly, check date filters first:

  ```bash
  llm-bill query 'select min("date") as min_date, max("date") as max_date, count(*) as rows from bills'
  ```

## Verification For Code Changes

If changing `llm-bill` query implementation, run:

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill
cargo fmt --check
cargo test
llm-bill query 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
llm-bill query --from 2026-03-01 --to 2026-03-31 \
  'select count(*) as rows, min("date") as min_date, max("date") as max_date from bills'
llm-bill query --format json 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
llm-bill query --format csv 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
```

If the edited checkout has not been installed yet, run the same query checks as
`cargo run -- query ...` from `/Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill`.

Also inspect:

```bash
llm-bill query --help
git diff --check
```

## Reporting

For analysis tasks, answer with the direct numeric or tabular result first, then
briefly mention:

- SQL query used.
- Date range and bill directory.
- Any skipped or suspicious data, if the command reports warnings.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/README.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/llm-bill-query/SKILL.md` |

name: llm-pricing-audit
description: Use when the user wants to update LLM model pricing, fix incorrect lcost amounts, audit provider/model cost tags, add pricing aliases, or repair historical `llm_costs` bills after a price change. Touches the local `model-info` registry, alias table, and persisted billing records. 当用户要求更新模型定价、修正 lcost 成本、审计提供商/模型成本、添加定价别名，或在定价变更后修复历史账单时触发。
body:
# LLM 定价审计

使用本技能在**不改动无关模型**的前提下，更新本地 LLM 定价元数据并修复持久化的 `lcost` 账单。

## 范围

默认本地路径：

- 定价注册表：`/Users/junix/.config/model-info/model-info.json`
- 历史/仅账单用定价注册表：`/Users/junix/.config/model-info/old_model_pricing.json`
- `lcost` 仓库：`/Users/junix/projects/python/llm-costs`
- Rust 账单查询/修复 CLI：`/Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill`
- 历史账单：`/Users/junix/sync/llm-costs`

若上述路径不存在，先用 `which llm-bill`、`which lcost`、`rg --files`，以及 `rg "model-info|LLM_COSTS_DIR|sync/llm-costs"` 定位真实 CLI 与仓库。

## 工作流

1. **确认目标模型族**
   - 从用户输出中提取精确模型名，例如 `glm-5.1`、`kimi-for-coding`、`MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`。
   - 优先用 `llm-bill query` 在历史账单中检索所有拼写与提供商前缀，并锚定具体账单文件：
     ```bash
     llm-bill query \
       "select model, count(*) as rows, sum(count) as calls,
               sum(prompt_tokens) as prompt, sum(completion_tokens) as completion,
               sum(price) as cost
        from bills
        where lower(model) like '%glm%'
        group by model
        order by model"
     ```
   - 查找可修复的 0 成本 token 行时，必须输出 `file`、`date`、`hour`：
     ```bash
     llm-bill query \
       "select file, \"date\", hour, model, count, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, price
        from bills
        where lower(model) like '%glm%'
          and price = 0
          and total_tokens > 0
        order by \"date\", hour, model"
     ```
   - 如果已安装的 `llm-bill` 没有 `query`/`fix` 子命令，用 checkout 版本：
     ```bash
     cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/llm-bill
     cargo run -- query 'select count(*) as rows from bills'
     ```
   - 仅在 `llm-bill` 不可用或需要与 Python 输出对齐时，用 Python 路径检索：
     ```bash
     cd /Users/junix/projects/python/llm-costs
     uv run python - <<'PY'
     from pathlib import Path
     from collections import defaultdict
     from llm_costs._types import DailyCosts
     needle = "minimax"  # change this
     agg = defaultdict(lambda: [0, 0.0, 0, 0, 0])
     for path in Path("/Users/junix/sync/llm-costs").glob("cost-*.json"):
         try:
             daily = DailyCosts.model_validate_json(path.read_text())
         except Exception:
             continue
         for hourly in daily.costs_by_hour.values():
             for model, cost in hourly.costs_by_model.items():
                 if needle.lower() in model.lower():
                     row = agg[model]
                     row[0] += 1
                     row[1] += cost.price
                     row[2] += cost.count
                     row[3] += cost.prompt_tokens
                     row[4] += cost.completion_tokens
     for model, row in sorted(agg.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[0].lower()):
         print(model, "rows=", row[0], "cost=", round(row[1], 2), "calls=", row[2], "prompt=", row[3], "completion=", row[4])
     PY
     ```

2. **检索当前官方定价**
   - 先浏览提供商官方定价/文档页。**不要**凭缓存记忆定价。
   - 优先官方页面，而非博客、封装层、LiteLLM 表或第三方看板。
   - 记录来源 URL、若可见则记录发布/生效日期、币种、计价单位（`per 1K`、`per 1M`、按次等），以及价格是否依赖缓存命中、上下文档位、思考/推理 token 或套餐类型。
   - 若账单只存 prompt/completion token 而无缓存命中拆分，在修复前说明假设；通常采用 cache-miss 输入价格。

3. **更新定价注册表**
   - 当前线上在跑模型用活跃 `model-info.json`。
   - 已下线模型、端点 ID、滚动别名快照、订阅/代理标签，以及**不应暗示仍可用**的仅账单别名，用 `old_model_pricing.json`。
   - **只**编辑目标模型族的条目。
   - 将定价规范为注册表字段：
     - `input_per_1k_tokens = input_per_1m / 1000`
     - `output_per_1k_tokens = output_per_1m / 1000`
     - `currency` 与来源完全一致。
   - 为账单中出现的每种历史拼写添加别名。若为纯历史别名，放在 `old_model_pricing.json`：
     - 裸模型名，例如 `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`
     - 带提供商前缀名，例如 `cc/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`
     - 路由别名，例如 `coding/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`
     - 历史上出现过的全小写与斜杠变体
   - 除非用户明确要求改路由，否则保留现有默认路由/提供商。**不要**仅为修成本而把已下线/仅账单别名挂到活跃线上路由上。
   - 编辑后校验 JSON：
     ```bash
     python3 -m json.tool /Users/junix/.config/model-info/model-info.json >/tmp/model-info_check.json
     python3 -m json.tool /Users/junix/.config/model-info/old_model_pricing.json >/tmp/old_model_pricing_check.json
     ```

4. **历史修复 dry-run**
   - 写入前**务必**对精确目标模型 dry-run。先用 `llm-bill query` 锚定具体 `file/date/hour/model`，再用 `llm-bill fix --day <DATE> --model <MODEL> --dry-run` 做最窄 dry-run：
     ```bash
     llm-bill fix --day 2026-05-06 --model 'glm-5.1' --dry-run
     ```
   - `llm-bill fix` 语义：
     - 默认只修 `price = 0` 且 token 非空的行。
     - `--model MODEL` 会按精确模型名匹配，并会重算该模型的非零价格行。
     - `--all-models` 会重算所有有注册表价格的行，风险更大，除非明确需要不要用。
     - token 全为 0 的行不会被修，因为没有计价依据；这种行只能按数据清理另行处理。
   - 日常审计必须用 `--day` 或 `--from/--to` 限定范围；不带日期范围的 `--model` 会扫描整个历史，只在用户要做全历史重算时使用：
     ```bash
     llm-bill fix --from 2026-05-01 --to 2026-05-06 --model 'MODEL' --dry-run
     llm-bill fix --model 'MODEL' --dry-run
     ```
   - 若需要用 Python `lcost` 路径做兼容修复，也先 dry-run：
     ```bash
     cd /Users/junix/projects/python/llm-costs
     uv run -m llm_costs fix --day yesterday --model 'MODEL' --dry-run
     uv run -m llm_costs fix --from 2026-05-01 --to 2026-05-06 --model 'MODEL' --dry-run
     uv run -m llm_costs fix --model 'MODEL' --dry-run
     ```
   - 做整族审计时，对每个发现的模型拼写分别执行。除非用户要求全局修复，否则避免 `--all-models`。
   - 若出现 `Skipped ... without local registry pricing`，视为阻塞：先在活跃注册表或 `old_model_pricing.json` 中补全/修正条目。

5. **执行修复**
   - dry-run 输出合理后，优先用 `llm-bill fix` 对已锚定的日期和模型执行修复：
     ```bash
     llm-bill fix --day 2026-05-06 --model 'glm-5.1'
     ```
   - 若使用 Python 路径，再对精确模型执行修复：
     ```bash
     uv run -m llm_costs fix --model 'MODEL'
     ```
   - 仅当用户要求按日限定时使用 `--day <date|yesterday|today>`。
   - 若多个别名指向同一批行，后续命令出现 `fixed 0 rows` 亦可接受。

6. **验证**
   - 用 `llm-bill query` 验证目标 0 成本 token 行已消失：
     ```bash
     llm-bill query --format csv \
       "select count(*) as bad_rows
        from bills
        where model = 'glm-5.1'
          and price = 0
          and total_tokens > 0"
     ```
   - 重新查询被修复的具体 `file/date/hour/model`，确认 `price` 和 `currency`：
     ```bash
     llm-bill query \
       "select file, \"date\", hour, model, count, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, price, currency
        from bills
        where model = 'glm-5.1' and \"date\" = '2026-05-06'
        order by hour, file"
     ```
   - 再次对目标模型（或该族各拼写）执行 dry-run，对已检查模型要求 `fixed 0 rows`。
   - 重跑用户原命令，保持其日期表达完全一致：
     ```bash
     uv run -m llm_costs --day yesterday
     ```
   - 用步骤 1 的审计脚本汇总修复后的模型族合计。
   - 若改动过代码，运行针对性测试：
     ```bash
     uv run pytest tests/test_pricing.py tests/test_view.py tests/test_main.py
     ```

## 汇报内容

回复中应包含：

- 官方定价来源 URL
- 所用价格假设与计价单位
- 新增或修正的注册表别名
- 修复的行数/文件数及修复前后成本差
- 用户原始 `lcost` 命令输出或相关变更行
- 仍无官方定价或 token 拆分不足、暂未解决的模型

## 护栏

- 定价随时间变化：务必浏览**当前**官方定价。
- 不要在说明中混用币种而不声明。说明存储成本是 USD、CNY 还是提供商套餐单位。
- 不要重写无关提供商或借机规范化整个注册表。
- 不要回滚无关仓库改动或历史账单文件。
- 除非历史记录里包含所需的按次/包量计数，否则不要用按次/包价去修按 token 计费的历史。

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/llm-bill/README.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/llm_costs/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/llm-pricing-audit/SKILL.md` |

name: locate-anything
description: Use when the user wants to locate, detect, or find objects/text/GUI elements in a local image — "find the cat in this photo", "where is the button", "detect all text", "point to the traffic light", "ground this phrase in the image", "GUI element grounding", "object detection on image", "visual grounding", "locate X in screenshot", "find bounding box". Uses the local `locate` CLI backed by nvidia/LocateAnything-3B.
body:
# locate-anything

CLI: `locate` — installed at `~/sync/bin/locate`.
Model: `~/models/LocateAnything-3B-4bit` (MLX 4-bit, Apple Silicon).
Source: `~/projects/models/locate-anything`. Rebuild with `just install`.

## Subcommands

| Subcommand | Task | Key arg |
|---|---|---|
| `detect` | Open-set object detection by category | `CATEGORY [CATEGORY ...]` |
| `ground` | Phrase grounding (natural language → boxes) | `"phrase"` |
| `text` | Scene text / OCR detection | — |
| `point` | Point-based localization | `"phrase"` |
| `gui` | GUI element grounding | `"phrase" [--output-type box\|point]` |

## Common usage

```bash
# Detect objects by name
locate detect photo.jpg person car bicycle --draw out.jpg

# Phrase grounding (all instances)
locate ground photo.jpg 'people wearing red shirts'

# Single best match
locate ground photo.jpg 'the black TV' --single

# Scene text detection
locate text document.jpg --json | jq '.boxes | length'

# Point at a target
locate point photo.jpg 'the traffic light' --json

# GUI grounding — box or point
locate gui screenshot.png 'search box' --output-type point --draw out.png
```

## Flags (all subcommands)

| Flag | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--model PATH` | `~/models/LocateAnything-3B-4bit` | or set `LOCATE_MODEL` env |
| `--backend auto\|mlx\|torch` | `auto` | mlx = Apple Silicon, torch = CUDA/MPS/CPU |
| `--json` | off | Structured output: `{ok, task, image, model, backend, raw, boxes, points}` |
| `-q / --quiet` | off | Suppress non-error output |
| `--draw OUT` | off | Annotate image and save to OUT |
| `--mode fast\|slow\|hybrid` | `hybrid` | torch backend only |

## JSON output shape

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "task": "detect",
  "image": "/abs/path/to/image.jpg",
  "image_size": {"w": 640, "h": 480},
  "model": "/Users/junix/models/LocateAnything-3B-4bit",
  "backend": "mlx",
  "raw": "<ref>cat</ref><box><9><112><495><990></box>",
  "boxes": [{"x1": 5.8, "y1": 53.8, "x2": 316.8, "y2": 475.2}],
  "points": []
}
```

Error envelope (exit code 3 for bad input, 1 for runtime):
```json
{"ok": false, "error": {"code": "IMAGE_NOT_FOUND", "message": "...", "fix": "..."}}
```

## Notes

- Auto-resizes images >1024px before MLX inference (prevents Metal OOM)
- `LOCATE_MODEL` env overrides `--model` for all subcommands
- Boxes are pixel coordinates in original image space (before any resize)
- Coordinate format in raw output: `<box><x1><y1><x2><y2></box>` normalized to [0,1000]
- For point tasks the raw format is `<box><x><y></box>` (2-coord)

name: low-risk-local-refactoring
description: Use when the user wants a low-risk, behavior-preserving refactor that fits inside one file with clear semantic boundaries — no public API or dependency changes, narrow existing verification. Sibling of `medium-risk-structural-refactoring` (multi-file, module-local) and `high-risk-refactor-planning` (architectural). For routing by risk, use `refactoring-router`.
body:
# Low Risk Local Refactoring

This skill is for direct execution of small, local refactors. It is intentionally strict.

## Use When

Use this skill only when all of the following are true:

- The task is a local cleanup, readability improvement, or tiny behavior-preserving simplification.
- The work can stay inside one production file.
- No public API, dependency, import boundary, or cross-file calling boundary changes are needed.
- The change can be verified with the narrowest existing test target that exercises the modified behavior.

If any of these are false, stop and route upward.

## Do

Allowed patterns are the **low-risk** entries in `refactoring-router/references/named-refactorings.md`. Use Fowler names when describing the work. The patterns below are the everyday subset:

- rename a function-local variable for clarity (Rename Variable)
- remove obviously unused local code
- simplify one conditional expression (Decompose Conditional, local)
- reduce one function's nesting with early returns when semantics stay unchanged (Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses)
- extract a tiny same-file helper that serves one local behavior slice (Extract Method, single-caller)
- inline a trivial same-file helper used once when it clearly improves readability (Inline Method)
- split a long expression into clearer intermediate variables (Extract Variable)
- replace a magic literal with a function-local or file-private constant when the meaning is obvious (Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant)

If the named transformation appears under medium- or high-risk in the catalog, stop and route upward — even if the diff would have been small.

A helper extraction is low-risk only when all of the following are true:

- it serves one original function
- it captures one local behavior slice inside that function
- it is not reused by other functions
- it does not introduce new branching semantics or a shared local abstraction

## Do Not

- do not change public APIs, exported symbols, or shared contracts
- do not move code across files, classes, or modules
- do not add or remove dependencies
- do not manually add, remove, or reorder imports for this skill
- do not rename externally referenced symbols
- do not change signatures, default values, return shapes, error types, log timing, or configuration behavior
- do not touch concurrency, caching, I/O, security, transactions, serialization, or framework registration logic
- do not rely on language-level privacy alone as evidence of safety
- do not touch symbols that may be discovered through reflection, conventions, registration, templates, routing, serialization, or generated code
- do not mix unrelated cleanup

## Pre-flight Checklist

All items must pass before editing:

1. The change is confined to one production file and one local behavior slice.
2. You can describe the change in one short sentence.
3. Only local symbols inside the target function or extracted helper may be renamed.
4. No dependency changes are needed, and no import editing is required.
5. The change does not require file-external business reasoning to understand safely.
6. The narrowest existing test target covering the behavior can be identified confidently.
7. Only one production file will be modified, with at most one existing related test file updated.
8. No symbol involved in the change is discovered indirectly by reflection, conventions, registration, templates, routing, serialization, or generated code.

## Workflow

1. If the user did not specify a file, inspect at most 5 production files or about 300 lines total to find one acceptable candidate.
2. Stop scanning as soon as you find the first candidate that clearly passes the checklist.
3. Before editing, state the target file, the exact local cleanup, and why this is low-risk.
4. Edit only the chosen production file and at most one existing related test file.
5. Run the narrowest existing test target that exercises the modified behavior.
6. If no file-level target exists, use the smallest existing package-level or module-level target that still covers the change.
7. If no suitable existing target can be identified confidently, stop.
8. **Verification means reading the failure count.** Exit code 0 alone is not verification. Read the runner output, locate the pass/fail summary, and confirm the failure count is exactly zero. If the runner output is in a file, read the entire file. Do not infer "passed" from "tests ran" or "no errors printed."
9. If verification passes, create a focused commit for this completed task and push it to the current branch.

## Common Pitfalls

- A small diff can still be high-risk if it changes boundaries.
- A "tiny helper extraction" stops being low-risk when the helper starts serving multiple unrelated call sites.
- A helper that introduces a new local abstraction or carries multiple branches is no longer low-risk.
- Import churn is often a sign that the task is no longer purely local.
- Logging and error-path edits often change observable behavior even when they look cosmetic.
- Language-level private does not prove the code is safe to reshape.

## Stop Conditions

Stop and output `STOP_LOW_RISK_REFACTOR` on the first line, followed by one short reason, when:

- no candidate passes the checklist within the scan budget
- the task requires more than one production file
- any public, dependency, or boundary change is needed
- no suitable existing verification target can be identified confidently
- the available tests mainly assert implementation details that this refactor would change
- the task is actually medium-risk or high-risk

## Verification

- Use the narrowest existing target that exercises the behavior.
- Do not broaden verification casually just to make the task seem allowed.
- If the existing tests mainly lock private implementation details rather than behavior, treat verification as insufficient.
- If only broad verification exists and the task still looks structurally larger than local cleanup, stop and route upward.

## Required Justification

Report:

- what changed
- why this is low-risk rather than medium-risk
- which allowed pattern was used
- files changed
- tests run
- test result
- commit message
- pushed branch

name: macos-app-build-lessons
body:
# 创建 macOS App 的经验总结(签名 · 权限 · 打包 · 调试)

来源:`~/projects/models/clipstack`(SwiftPM 编出单二进制 → 既是菜单栏 agent
又是 CLI,需要辅助功能权限发 ⌘V)。下面是真正绊住人的几类问题,新做 App 时直接抄。

---

## 1. 代码签名:ad-hoc 会让权限每次重装都失效

**症状**:给 App 授了"辅助功能"(或屏幕录制等任何 TCC 权限),系统设置里那条
还显示"已勾选",但 App 实际拿不到权限;每次 `codesign --sign -` 重装后又得重授。

**根因**:macOS 的隐私权限(TCC)是按 App 的**指定要求(designated requirement, DR)**
匹配的。**ad-hoc 签名(`codesign --sign -`)没有稳定身份,DR 退化成绑定二进制的
cdhash**;而 cdhash **每次重新编译都会变** → 新二进制对系统就是"另一个 App",
旧授权(绑在旧 cdhash 上)对不上,但列表里的旧条目会**残留**,看起来像已授权。

```sh
# 看签名是不是 ad-hoc:
codesign -dv --verbose=4 YourApp.app 2>&1 | grep -E 'Signature|flags|TeamIdentifier'
#   Signature=adhoc / flags=0x2(adhoc) / TeamIdentifier=not set  → 就是它

# 看 DR 是 cdhash 还是证书绑定:
codesign -d --requirements - YourApp.app 2>&1 | grep designated
#   ...and certificate leaf = H"..."   ← 证书绑定(稳定,好)
#   ...and cdhash H"..."               ← cdhash 绑定(每次构建都变,坏)
```

**根治**:用一个**稳定的本机自签名代码签名证书**签名。这样 DR 变成
`certificate leaf = H"<固定哈希>"`,跨重新构建不变 → 授权一次,永久保留
(只要还用同一张证书签)。无需 Apple 开发者账号。

```sh
# 一次性创建本机自签名 code-signing 证书(注意 openssl3 的 PKCS12 需 legacy 算法,
# 否则 macOS `security import` 会报 "MAC verification failed"):
DIR=$(mktemp -d)
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -days 3650 \
  -keyout "$DIR/k.pem" -out "$DIR/c.pem" -subj "/CN=MyApp Local Signing" \
  -addext "basicConstraints=critical,CA:false" \
  -addext "keyUsage=critical,digitalSignature" \
  -addext "extendedKeyUsage=critical,codeSigning"
openssl pkcs12 -export -inkey "$DIR/k.pem" -in "$DIR/c.pem" -name "MyApp Local Signing" \
  -passout pass:tmp -certpbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -keypbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -macalg sha1 -out "$DIR/id.p12"
security import "$DIR/id.p12" -k ~/Library/Keychains/login.keychain-db -P tmp -T /usr/bin/codesign
rm -rf "$DIR"

# 之后签名(证书自签未受信任也能签;签名不要求信任,只要私钥可用):
codesign --force --sign "MyApp Local Signing" --timestamp=none YourApp.app
```

要点:
- 自签证书 `find-identity -v -p codesigning` 会显示 `CSSMERR_TP_NOT_TRUSTED`,
  **不影响 codesign 使用**(信任只影响验证,不影响签名,也不影响 TCC 按 leaf hash 匹配)。
- 第一次用该 key 签名,钥匙串可能弹一次"codesign 想使用密钥",点**始终允许**即可
  (import 时 `-T /usr/bin/codesign` 已尽量避免)。需要彻底免提示用
  `security set-key-partition-list`(要钥匙串密码,让用户自己 `! ...` 跑)。
- TCC 授权是**每台机器各自**的,证书也不随 `~/sync` 同步 → 每台机器各建一次证书、
  各授权一次。
- 把 build 脚本写成"有该证书就用、没有就退回 ad-hoc",便于在没配证书的机器上也能构建。

### 1b. 同步卷上**原地覆盖**已签名二进制 → 运行即 `Killed: 9`

**症状**:`just install` 把新构建的二进制 `cp` 到 `~/sync/bin_<arch>/foo`(覆盖同名旧文件),
随后**任何**运行(连 `foo --version` 都不行)立刻 `Killed: 9`(exit 137)。`codesign -v` 说
"valid on disk",`spctl` 只是 reject(自签未受信,正常),都骗你以为没问题。最迷惑的是:
**字节完全相同**的同一文件,`cp` 成**另一个文件名**就能跑,原路径却必被杀。

**根因**:`cp` 到已存在文件是**原地 truncate+写同一 inode**。在 `~/sync` 这类同步卷
(iCloud Drive / Syncthing 等)上,原地覆盖一个代码签名 Mach-O,会让该 inode 的页与新签名
**不一致**;内核在 page-in 校验代码页时发现 hash 对不上 → 直接 `SIGKILL`。换文件名 = 新 inode,
绕开了脏页/签名错配。

**根治**:安装时**先 `rm` 再 `cp`**,让每次落到**新 inode**:

```sh
rm -f "$INSTALL_DIR/foo"          # 关键:别原地覆盖
cp .build/release/foo "$INSTALL_DIR/foo"
```

排查确认:`cp .build/release/foo /tmp/x && /tmp/x --version`(/tmp 非同步卷)能跑、
`~/sync/.../foo` 被杀,且两者 `cmp` 字节相同 → 就是这个坑。同理别用 `install -C`/rsync 原地更新。

---

## 2. TCC / 辅助功能权限:几个致命陷阱

### 2a. "责任进程"陷阱 —— 从终端直接起进程会测出假阳性

`AXIsProcessTrusted()` 返回的是**当前责任进程**的授权状态。如果你**从终端直接执行
二进制**(`./MyApp.app/Contents/MacOS/MyApp &`),TCC 可能把责任算到**终端**头上;
终端通常已被授予辅助功能 → `AXIsProcessTrusted()` 返回 `true`,**假阳性**,把你
误导成"权限没问题"。

**正确测法**:用 LaunchServices 启动(`open YourApp.app`),让 App 成为自己的责任
进程,再看权限。但这样 stderr 没法直接捕获 → 让 App **把诊断写进文件**(而不是只
`NSLog`,见 §4),再读文件。

```swift
import ApplicationServices
// 真实判断(在 open 启动的 App 内部打到文件):
let trusted = AXIsProcessTrusted()
```

### 2b. 主动申请权限 + 别静默失败

权限缺失时,合成的 ⌘V / 事件会被**静默丢弃**,UI 上"像没反应"。要主动弹系统框、
把自己注册进辅助功能列表,而不是默默失败:

```swift
private static var didPrompt = false
func requestAccessibilityOnce() {
    guard !didPrompt else { return }; didPrompt = true
    let key = kAXTrustedCheckOptionPrompt.takeUnretainedValue() as String
    _ = AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions([key: true] as CFDictionary)  // 弹框 + 入列表
}
```

### 2c. 清理被搞乱的授权状态

反复换签名/重装后,列表里可能有**多条互相打架的残留**(旧 cdhash 的、新证书的)。
用 `tccutil` 按 bundle id 清空,再让 App 重新申请一次干净的:

```sh
tccutil reset Accessibility com.you.myapp   # 清掉该 App 全部辅助功能授权
# 然后 open 启动 → 触发 requestAccessibilityOnce → 用户重新勾选(这次绑稳定证书,持久)
```

### 2d. 给用户的"最后一次授权"步骤

签名换成稳定证书后,旧授权对不上,需用户**再授权一次**(此后持久):
系统设置 → 隐私与安全性 → 辅助功能 → 删掉旧的 App 条目(−)→ 重新添加
`~/Applications/YourApp.app` 并打开开关。强调"删旧条目"——只开关旧条目仍绑旧身份。

---

## 3. 把 SwiftPM 二进制打成 .app(菜单栏 agent)

SwiftPM 只产二进制;要成为带图标、可授权、无 Dock 图标的菜单栏 App,需手工组 bundle:

```
YourApp.app/Contents/
  MacOS/YourApp            # 拷贝 .build/release/二进制
  Resources/AppIcon.icns
  Info.plist
```

`Info.plist` 关键键:
- `CFBundleIdentifier`(如 `com.you.myapp`)—— **TCC 按它认 App**,必须稳定。
- `CFBundleExecutable` = MacOS 下的可执行名。
- `LSUIElement` = `true` —— 菜单栏 agent:无 Dock 图标、无主菜单。
  (代码里也 `NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)` 双保险,裸二进制启动时也对。)
- `CFBundleIconFile` / `CFBundleIconName` = `AppIcon`。
- `LSMinimumSystemVersion`、`NSPrincipalClass=NSApplication`、`NSHighResolutionCapable`。

组完 `codesign --force --sign "<身份>" YourApp.app`(§1)。本地构建的 App **不带
quarantine 属性**,Gatekeeper 不拦自签/ad-hoc,可直接 `open`。

---

## 4. 调试:日志与"无头驱动 GUI"

### 4a. NSLog 不一定能被 `log show` 捞到 —— 直接重定向 stderr

`log show --predicate 'eventMessage CONTAINS ...'` 经常**捞不到** App 的 `NSLog`
(过滤/分级问题,排查半天)。**最可靠**:直接执行二进制并把 stderr 重定向到文件。
但注意这会触发 §2a 的责任进程陷阱(影响 TCC 判断),所以:**测权限用 `open` +
文件日志,测纯逻辑可用 stderr 重定向**。

```sh
"$HOME/Applications/YourApp.app/Contents/MacOS/YourApp" >/tmp/app.out 2>/tmp/app.err &
grep MARKER /tmp/app.err
```

### 4b. 非激活面板(.nonactivatingPanel)收不到合成按键

测一个无边框、`.nonactivatingPanel` 的搜索弹窗时:
- System Events 的 `keystroke` / `key code` 按"**最前台 App**"投递,而非激活面板
  **拿不到前台** → 合成按键到不了它。
- 合成的全局热键(`key code ... using {command down}`)**触发不了 Carbon
  `RegisterEventHotKey`**(真人按键才行)。
- App 级 `addLocalMonitorForEvents` 对这种面板的按键**根本不触发**。

可用的办法:
- 用**菜单栏状态项的菜单**(AX: `click menu item ... of menu 1 of menu bar item 1
  of menu bar 1`)来打开面板,而不是热键。
- 让面板**真正激活后**,再用 `do shell script` 在同一个 osascript 里发 HID 级
  `CGEvent`,或干脆用真实信号**间接验证**(粘贴结果、`count of windows`)。
- **键盘导航别依赖 NSEvent 监听**:把 `NSTextField` 的 field editor 命令选择器
  (`control(_:textView:doCommandBy:)` 里的 `moveUp:`/`moveDown:`/`insertNewline:`/
  `cancelOperation:`)拦下来 —— 这是这种面板里唯一稳定收到方向键/回车/Esc 的地方。
  SwiftUI `TextField` 会把 ↑/↓ 吞掉,`.onMoveCommand` 也收不到。
- `NSViewRepresentable` 里抢焦点要在 **`viewDidMoveToWindow`**(子类化 NSTextField),
  因为 `updateNSView` 常在还没 window 时跑、有了 window 后不再跑。

---

## 5. 同一个二进制既当 GUI 又当 CLI

- `main.swift` 先判调用方式再分流:按 `argv[0]` 的 basename(如别名 `cs`)或识别到
  子命令就走 CLI 并 `exit`,否则起 `NSApplication`。裸别名(无子命令)应打印 usage
  而**不要**误启 GUI。
- CLI 安装为指向二进制的**符号链接**(`ln -sf binary cs`);通过符号链接调用时
  `argv[0]` 仍是别名名,basename 判断成立。
- 编译型二进制装到 `~/sync/bin_<arch>/`,解释型脚本装到 `~/sync/bin/`(见根 CLAUDE.md)。

---

## 速查清单(新 macOS App 起步)

1. [ ] 需要 TCC 权限?→ **第一天就配稳定自签证书**,别用 ad-hoc(§1)。
1b.[ ] 装到 `~/sync` 等同步卷的已签名二进制,`install` 必须**先 `rm` 再 `cp`**(新 inode),
   否则原地覆盖会让它运行即 `Killed: 9`(§1b)。
2. [ ] `Info.plist` 里 `CFBundleIdentifier` 固定;菜单栏 agent 加 `LSUIElement`(§3)。
3. [ ] 权限缺失要**主动弹框**、别静默失败(§2b)。
4. [ ] 测权限**必须 `open` 启动 + 文件日志**,别从终端直起(§2a)。
5. [ ] 换签名后提醒用户**删旧条目再重授**一次(§2d)。
6. [ ] GUI 自动化测试:走菜单/间接信号,键盘导航走 field editor 选择器(§4b)。

## 相关
- 配套工具/同步驱动 GUI:[[use-macbot]]。
- 项目骨架与安装路径约定:[[create-skeleton-project]]。

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/clipstack` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/clipstack/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/clipstack/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/clipstack/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/macos-app-build-lessons/SKILL.md` |

name: manage-obsidian-notes
description: Use when the user mentions Obsidian, an Obsidian vault, wikilinks, or backlinks, or asks to find, create, link, or organize notes inside their AI Research Obsidian vault.
body:
# Obsidian Vault

## Vault location

`/mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/`

Mostly flat at root level.

## Naming conventions

- **Index notes**: aggregate related topics (e.g., `Ralph Wiggum Index.md`, `Skills Index.md`, `RAG Index.md`)
- **Title case** for all note names
- No folders for organization - use links and index notes instead

## Linking

- Use Obsidian `[[wikilinks]]` syntax: `[[Note Title]]`
- Notes link to dependencies/related notes at the bottom
- Index notes are just lists of `[[wikilinks]]`

## Workflows

### Search for notes

```bash
# Search by filename
find "/mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/" -name "*.md" | grep -i "keyword"

# Search by content
grep -rl "keyword" "/mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/" --include="*.md"
```

Or use Grep/Glob tools directly on the vault path.

### Create a new note

1. Use **Title Case** for filename
2. Write content as a unit of learning (per vault rules)
3. Add `[[wikilinks]]` to related notes at the bottom
4. If part of a numbered sequence, use the hierarchical numbering scheme

### Find related notes

Search for `[[Note Title]]` across the vault to find backlinks:

```bash
grep -rl "\\[\\[Note Title\\]\\]" "/mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/"
```

### Find index notes

```bash
find "/mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/" -name "*Index*"
```

name: medium-risk-structural-refactoring
description: Use when the user wants a behavior-preserving structural refactor across a small number of related files inside one module — no public API or architectural-boundary changes, requires a boundary-and-invariants plan before editing. Sibling of `low-risk-local-refactoring` (single file) and `high-risk-refactor-planning` (architectural). For routing by risk, use `refactoring-router`.
body:
# Medium Risk Structural Refactoring

This skill is for local structural cleanup, not architectural redesign.

For this skill, `module` means one observable ownership unit: the same package/namespace/module tree, with the same primary verification target, and without crossing an exported surface.

## Use When

Use this skill only when all of the following are true:

- The goal is still behavior-preserving.
- The work stays within one module or a very small cluster of related files.
- Only private or module-local structure is changing.
- The task needs more than one local edit or more than one private call-site update.
- Existing module-level or equivalent verification is available.

If the task changes responsibilities, public interfaces, dependency direction, or ownership boundaries, route to `high-risk-refactor-planning` instead.
If correctness still depends on a broader migration sequence, route to `high-risk-refactor-planning` instead.

## Do

Allowed patterns are the **medium-risk** entries in `refactoring-router/references/named-refactorings.md`. Use Fowler names when describing the work. The patterns below are the everyday subset:

- extract a private helper reused by multiple functions in the same file or module (Extract Method, cross-function)
- split an overlong function into a few private helpers (Extract Method)
- rename a private function or method and update its local call sites (Rename Method, private)
- merge two highly similar private helpers when their differences are explicit and local (Inline Method + re-Extract, or Consolidate Conditional Expression)
- consolidate repeated private validation, formatting, or guard logic inside one module (Consolidate Conditional Expression)
- replace a small cluster of parallel locals with a file-private helper type or record when it does not escape the module (Replace Data Value with Object, private)
- move a method or field to the class whose data it uses, both classes inside the module (Move Method / Move Field, intra-module)
- split or merge two private classes inside the module (Extract Class / Inline Class)

If the named transformation appears under high-risk in the catalog — including any cross-module Move, any Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, any Branch by Abstraction or Parallel Change — stop and route to `high-risk-refactor-planning`.

## Do Not

- do not change public APIs, exported symbols, or externally consumed error contracts
- do not move logic across modules or services
- do not introduce new abstraction layers such as shared services, repositories, adapters, or base classes
- do not change dependency direction or ownership boundaries
- do not alter concurrency, caching, I/O, security, transaction, serialization, or lifecycle behavior
- do not touch framework registration or magic naming conventions unless the task is explicitly about that and routed higher
- do not rely on language-level private visibility as proof that a symbol is safe to reshape
- do not mix opportunistic cleanup outside the declared boundary

## Pre-flight Checklist

All items must pass before editing:

1. The work stays behavior-preserving and within one module or one tightly related directory.
2. No public API, exported symbol, or widely used signature change is needed.
3. The production scope is at most 3 related files.
4. You can list the affected private symbols and local call sites before editing.
5. You can list the key behaviors that must remain unchanged.
6. No dependency changes or new abstraction layers are needed.
7. Existing module-level or equivalent verification can be identified confidently.
8. No affected symbol is discovered indirectly by reflection, conventions, registration, templates, routing, serialization, or generated code.
9. Correctness does not depend on later migration phases or future boundary changes.

## Workflow

1. Inspect only the target module and immediate call sites needed for the refactor.
2. Before editing, state:
   - the exact boundary
   - affected files
   - affected private symbols or call sites
   - the behaviors that must not change
   - the verification target you will run
   - the bail signal: which test failure or compile error means abort, and the exact command that returns the tree to the last green state (e.g. `git restore .`, `git stash`, or `git revert <sha>` for the latest commit on this branch). If the work spans multiple commits within this skill, the bail signal must name a recovery point per commit.
3. Perform only the declared refactor.
4. Keep the implementation private or module-local.
5. Run the narrowest existing module-level or equivalent verification target that exercises the affected behavior.
6. If the work starts pulling in public contracts, cross-module changes, or migration concerns, stop and route upward.
