7. **Verification means reading the failure count.** Exit code 0 alone is not verification. Read the runner output, locate the pass/fail summary, and confirm the failure count is exactly zero. If the runner output is in a file, read the entire file. Do not infer "passed" from "tests ran" or "no errors printed."
8. If verification passes, create a focused commit for this completed task and push it to the current branch.

A task is not medium-risk merely because it was split into small edits.
If correctness still depends on a broader migration sequence or later boundary changes, route upward.

### No-coverage fallback (Feathers' characterization-test rule)

If the affected behavior has no behavior-oriented test today, do not relax the verification rule. Instead:

1. Make the **first slice** of this work "write a characterization test that pins the current externally observable behavior of the affected area." A characterization test asserts what the code currently does, even if you do not yet trust that behavior — its job is to make change visible, not to ratify the design.
2. Route that slice through the `tdd` skill (or whichever test-writing skill the project uses).
3. Land that slice green on its own commit.
4. Only then run this skill again on the structural refactor.

Do not interleave structural change with characterization-test writing in the same commit. The point of the test is to give the refactor a tripwire; if the test and the refactor land together, the tripwire is invisible.

## Common Pitfalls

- Two helpers that look duplicated may embody different business cases.
- Private symbol renames can still be dangerous when frameworks or reflection discover them indirectly.
- A "small" helper type can become a stealth public abstraction if it leaks into signatures.
- Repeated validation logic often differs in edge-case ordering or error semantics.
- Three related files can still be high-risk if they cross an exported surface or hidden framework boundary.

## Stop Conditions

Stop and output `STOP_MEDIUM_RISK_REFACTOR` on the first line, followed by one short reason, when:

- the scope expands beyond one module or 3 production files
- a public API or shared boundary would change
- the behaviors that must remain unchanged cannot be stated clearly
- no suitable existing module-level verification target can be identified confidently
- the available tests mainly assert implementation details that this refactor would change
- correctness depends on later migration phases or future boundary changes
- the task is actually high-risk

## Verification

- Run the narrowest existing module-level or equivalent target that covers the affected behavior.
- Prefer behavior-oriented tests over implementation-detail tests.
- If the available tests mostly lock private implementation details and this refactor changes those internals, treat verification as insufficient.
- If verification would need broad integration coverage to gain confidence, route upward instead of guessing.

## Required Justification

Report:

- what changed
- why this is medium-risk rather than low-risk or high-risk
- declared boundary
- unchanged behaviors
- files changed
- tests run
- test result
- commit message
- pushed branch

name: migrate-to-shoehorn
minimax-generate-video
description: Use when the user wants to generate a MiniMax / Hailuo video via the local `minimax-cli` — submitting a text-to-video or image-to-video task, polling task status, retrieving the download URL, or saving a generated MP4. Avoids duplicate quota spend by preferring the CLI over raw `curl`.
body:
# MiniMax Generate Video

Use this skill for MiniMax video generation while avoiding duplicate quota
spend. Prefer the local Rust CLI at `/Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli`.
Use direct `curl` only when the CLI is unavailable or the user asks for raw API
calls.

## Prerequisites

- API key is read from `MINIMAX_API_KEY`; never echo the key.
- Work from `/Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli` when using `cargo run`.
- Check current official docs before changing model/field assumptions:
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/video-generation-t2v`
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/video-generation-i2v`
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/video-generation-query`
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/file-management-retrieve`
- Video generation consumes scarce quota. Submit exactly one generation task
  unless the user explicitly asks for another attempt.

## Default Text-To-Video Workflow

Use `MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3`, 6 seconds, 768P by default:

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli
cargo run -- --compact video create \
  --model MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3 \
  --prompt 'A cinematic 6-second shot of a quiet rainy neon street at night, reflections on wet pavement, slow forward camera movement' \
  --duration 6 \
  --resolution 768P
```

Save the returned `task_id`. Do not submit the same prompt again while polling.

## Image-To-Video Workflow

Use image-to-video when the user provides a first-frame image URL. Models such
as `MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3-Fast`, `I2V-01`, `I2V-01-live`, and `I2V-01-Director`
require `--first-frame-image`.

```bash
cargo run -- --compact video create \
  --model MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3-Fast \
  --first-frame-image 'https://example.com/frame.jpg' \
  --prompt 'The subject slowly turns toward camera, cinematic lighting' \
  --duration 6 \
  --resolution 768P
```

For legacy `I2V-01*` models, use `--duration 6 --resolution 720P`.

## Poll Until Complete

Query the task after creation:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact video query --task-id <TASK_ID>
```

Common statuses:

- `Preparing`: accepted, not rendering yet.
- `Processing`: rendering.
- `Success`: finished; response includes `file_id`.
- Failure/error status: report the exact JSON and stop.

Polling discipline:

1. Query once immediately.
2. If not complete, wait 30-60 seconds before querying again.
3. Do not create a duplicate task while a task is `Preparing` or `Processing`.
4. Stop after a reasonable wait and report the `task_id` if still processing.

## Retrieve Download URL

When query returns `status: "Success"` and a `file_id`, retrieve the file:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact file retrieve --file-id <FILE_ID>
```

The response should contain `file.download_url`. Report the URL and metadata
such as `filename`, `file_id`, `video_width`, and `video_height` when available.

## Save MP4 Locally

Use `file retrieve --out` when the user wants a durable local file:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact file retrieve \
  --file-id <FILE_ID> \
  --out /path/to/output.mp4
```

After saving, verify:

```bash
test -s /path/to/output.mp4
```

Then report the saved path and file size.

## Model And Parameter Guardrails

- Default: `MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3`, `--duration 6`, `--resolution 768P`.
- 1080P is only valid for 6-second Hailuo-style videos.
- `I2V-01`, `I2V-01-live`, `I2V-01-Director`, `T2V-01`, and
  `T2V-01-Director` use `--duration 6 --resolution 720P`.
- `MiniMax-Hailuo-2.3-Fast` is treated as image-to-video and requires
  `--first-frame-image`.
- Keep prompts concise and visual. Describe camera motion, subject motion,
  lighting, style, and duration-relevant action.

## Failure Handling

- If MiniMax returns quota/limit errors, report the status message and reset
  time if present; do not retry in a loop.
- If `video create` succeeds but polling times out, give the `task_id` so the
  user can continue later.
- If `file retrieve` lacks `download_url`, report the full response shape.
- If a generated URL is returned, mention that signed URLs can expire; save
  with `--out` when the user needs a persistent artifact.

## Useful Local Checks

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli
cargo run -- video create --help
cargo run -- video query --help
cargo run -- file retrieve --help
cargo test
```

Use tests/help for skill or CLI edits. Do not run live video generation just to
validate a skill change.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/minimax-generate-video/SKILL.md` |

name: minimax-music-generation
description: Use when the user wants to generate music through MiniMax APIs via the local `minimax-cli` Rust tool — creating a song, generating lyrics-then-music, making instrumental music, producing a cover from a reference audio URL/file, or saving MiniMax music output. Falls back to direct `curl` only when the CLI is unavailable.
body:
# MiniMax Music Generation

Use this skill to generate music through MiniMax while preserving quota and
producing durable output. Prefer the local Rust CLI at
`/Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli`; fall back to direct `curl` only when
the CLI is unavailable or the user explicitly asks for raw API calls.

## Prerequisites

- API key is read from `MINIMAX_API_KEY`; never echo the key.
- Work from `/Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli` when using `cargo run`.
- For current endpoint details, verify against official docs before changing
  model/field assumptions:
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/music-generation`
  - `https://platform.minimaxi.com/docs/api-reference/lyrics-generation`
- Avoid live generation unless the user explicitly asks to generate or test.
  Music calls consume quota and may take 30-120 seconds.

## Default Workflow

1. Clarify the desired kind of output only if needed: vocal song, instrumental,
   or cover. If the user gives a topic/style, proceed without asking.
2. If lyrics are not provided and a vocal song is desired, generate lyrics first.
3. Generate music with `music-2.6` and `--output-format url` by default.
4. If the user wants a file, pass `--out <path>` and then confirm the file
   exists and has nonzero size.
5. Report the audio URL, duration, sample rate, bitrate, and saved path when
   available.

## Lyrics First

Generate lyrics when the user gives a theme but not actual lyrics:

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli
cargo run -- --compact lyrics \
  --prompt '一首关于清晨写代码的轻快流行歌'
```

Use the returned `lyrics` as the music input. The returned `style_tags` are a
good starting `--prompt` for music generation.

For random lyric generation, omit `--prompt`:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact lyrics
```

For editing existing lyrics, use `--mode edit` with both `--prompt` and
`--lyrics` or `--lyrics-file`.

## Vocal Song

Use `music-2.6`. Lyrics are required unless `--lyrics-optimizer` is used.

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli
cargo run -- --compact music \
  --model music-2.6 \
  --prompt 'acoustic pop, warm morning, optimistic, light percussion, gentle guitar' \
  --lyrics-file /path/to/lyrics.txt \
  --output-format url \
  --out /path/to/song.mp3
```

If lyrics are inline, preserve line breaks and structure tags such as `[Verse]`
and `[Chorus]`. For long lyrics, write a temp file and use `--lyrics-file`.

## Instrumental

For pure instrumental music, omit lyrics and pass `--instrumental`. A prompt is
required.

```bash
cargo run -- --compact music \
  --model music-2.6 \
  --prompt 'ambient piano, soft pads, calm study music, no vocals' \
  --instrumental \
  --output-format url \
  --out /path/to/instrumental.mp3
```

Do not combine `--instrumental` with `--lyrics`, `--lyrics-file`, or
`--lyrics-optimizer`.

## Auto-Lyrics Music

When the user wants MiniMax to generate lyrics and music in one call, use
`--lyrics-optimizer` with a strong prompt:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact music \
  --model music-2.6 \
  --prompt 'Mandopop, summer beach romance, bright chorus, female vocal' \
  --lyrics-optimizer \
  --output-format url
```

This is less controllable than the two-step lyrics-first workflow.

## Cover Music

Use `music-cover` when the user provides a reference audio URL or local audio
file. Provide exactly one reference source:

- `--audio-url <url>`
- `--audio-base64-file <path>`
- `--cover-feature-id <id>`

Basic cover from reference URL:

```bash
cargo run -- --compact music \
  --model music-cover \
  --prompt 'warm acoustic pop cover' \
  --audio-url 'https://example.com/reference.mp3' \
  --output-format url \
  --out /path/to/cover.mp3
```

If using `--cover-feature-id`, lyrics are required. The current CLI does not
yet implement the `music_cover_preprocess` command that creates
`cover_feature_id`; use direct API only if the user specifically needs that
two-step workflow.

## Validation And Failure Handling

- A successful response has `base_resp.status_code == 0`.
- `data.audio` is either a URL or hex audio data. With `--output-format url`,
  URLs expire, so save with `--out` when the user wants a durable file.
- `extra_info.music_duration` is milliseconds.
- If MiniMax returns quota/limit errors, report the exact reset time or status
  message and do not retry in a loop.
- If generation hangs, wait up to the CLI timeout. Do not start duplicate music
  generation requests unless the user confirms.

## Useful Local Checks

```bash
cd /Users/junix/projects/crates/minimax-cli
cargo run -- music --help
cargo run -- lyrics --help
cargo test
```

Use `cargo test` for CLI changes; do not run live generation just to validate a
skill edit.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/minimax-cli/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/minimax-music-generation/SKILL.md` |

name: monitor-claude-code
body:
# monitor-claude-code (ccmon)

Real-time monitor for Claude Code sessions. Two pieces:

- **`crates/ccmon-report`** (Rust) — a tiny, fail-silent reporter that Claude Code
  hooks invoke. Reads the hook JSON on stdin, distills a `ccmon.event.v1` frame,
  ships it over a Unix socket. Never blocks/breaks the agent (always exits 0).
- **`swift/ccmon`** → `CCMon.app` (Swift) — a `LSUIElement` menu-bar agent. Binds
  the socket, runs the session state machine, renders a top-center **Dynamic
  Island** (hover to expand) plus a right-side **`))) N` menu-bar icon** (fallback).

Architecture/decisions: `~/projects/docs/adr/827-ccmon-claude-code-monitor-dynamic-island.md`.

## Install

```bash
cd ~/projects/models/ccmon-report && just install   # reporter → ~/sync/bin_arm64/ccmon-report
cd ~/projects/models/ccmon         && just install    # CCMon.app → ~/Applications + ccmon CLI
just init   # (in crates/ccmon-report) scaffolds ~/.ccmon-report/{config.json, plugin/}
open ~/Applications/CCMon.app                        # menu-bar agent starts (no Dock icon)
```

It needs **no special macOS permission** — it only binds its own Unix socket.

## Enable monitoring (two ways)

**Preferred — static plugin, opt-in, doesn't touch global config:**

```bash
claude --plugin-dir ~/.ccmon-report/plugin           # or glmcc / arkcc / deepseekcc
# always-on: alias glmcc='command glmcc --plugin-dir ~/.ccmon-report/plugin'
```

**Alternative — global, automatic for every session (edits ~/.claude/settings.json):**

```bash
ccmon-report install          # add the 7 hooks   (just install-hooks)
ccmon-report uninstall        # remove them        (just uninstall-hooks)
```

Hooks registered (both routes): `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `PreToolUse`,
`PostToolUse`, `Notification`, `Stop`, `SessionEnd`.

## See the UI

`CCMon.app` must be running. Then run a session (`claude`/`glmcc` with the plugin).

- **Top-center** (under the notch): the island. Empty = a tiny dark pill; **hover**
  to expand the session list; **click** the pill to pin; click a row for detail
  (cwd, turns/tools, last message, Open / Copy id / Dismiss). It auto-expands ~2.5s
  on launch so you can spot it.
- **Menu bar (right):** `))) N` wave icon (N = active sessions). Click for the list +
  **Start at Login** + Quit.

Each row shows: status dot · project (cwd basename) · status · current tool · 🔧total · elapsed.
Status: working=blue(pulse) · waiting=orange · idle=gray · done=green · error=red · stale.
live-cc managed sessions get a purple **`live-cc`** badge.

## Config — `~/.ccmon-report/config.json`

Single source of truth, read by **both** the reporter and the app.

```json
{ "socket_path": "/Users/<you>/.claude-monitor/monitor.sock", "log": true }
```

Socket resolution (both halves): `CCMON_SOCKET` env → `config.json` `socket_path` → default.
`just init` only writes config.json when absent (won't clobber edits); the plugin is
always regenerated.

## Debug — `~/.ccmon-report/run.log`

The reporter appends one line per hook (bounded 512 KiB, auto-truncates). **First place
to look when nothing shows up.**

```
2026-05-30T21:13:56+08:00 PreToolUse session=<id> tool=Read -> sent (socket=…/monitor.sock)
2026-05-30T21:13:57+08:00 Stop       session=<id> tool=-    -> monitor-unreachable (… No such file …)
```

Statuses: `sent` (delivered), `monitor-unreachable` (app not running / wrong socket),
`send-failed`, `bad-input`, `too-large`, `read-failed`. Toggle with `"log": false` or
`CCMON_LOG=0`. `CCMON_DEBUG=1` also mirrors to stderr.

```bash
tail -f ~/.ccmon-report/run.log
ccmon-report socket-path     # resolved socket
ccmon socket-path            # app's view of the same (must match)
```

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Check |
|---|---|
| Island/menu-bar empty during a session | `run.log` — `sent` means delivered (app issue); `monitor-unreachable` means app not running or socket mismatch |
| `monitor-unreachable` | `open ~/Applications/CCMon.app`; confirm `ccmon-report socket-path` == `ccmon socket-path` |
| No `run.log` lines at all | hooks not loaded — pass `--plugin-dir ~/.ccmon-report/plugin` or run `ccmon-report install` |
| Session lingers / wrong state | idle/working go `stale` after 3 min; `done` rows drop after ~15s; or expand → row → Dismiss |
| Can't find the UI | it's a background agent (no Dock icon): look top-center (island) and right menu bar (`)))`) |

Tests: `just test` in each project (Rust + Swift). Stale socket from a crash is safe — the
app `unlink`s and rebinds on next launch.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/ccmon-report` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/ccmon-report/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/ccmon-report/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/ccmon-report/CLAUDE.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/ccmon` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/ccmon/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/ccmon/CLAUDE.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/monitor-claude-code/SKILL.md` |

name: operate-linux
body:
# 用命令行操作 Linux 系统设置

不打开图形设置也能通过 shell / agent 控制所有常见系统参数。Linux 命令随环境分叉（Wayland/X11、桌面环境、音频栈），**先做环境检测，再按需查阅对应参考文档**。

## 环境检测（先跑这个）

```bash
echo $XDG_SESSION_TYPE                 # wayland / x11 / tty（截图、窗口工具据此分流）
echo $XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP              # GNOME / KDE / sway / Hyprland …
pactl info | grep "Server Name"        # on PipeWire → 用 wpctl；否则 pactl
nmcli general status >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "NetworkManager"   # 网络管理器
systemd-detect-virt                    # none / kvm / docker / wsl（容器里大半设置不可用）
```

## 导航

| 类别 | 参考文档 | 关键工具 |
|------|---------|---------|
| 音量 / 静音 / TTS / 播放 | [references/audio.md](references/audio.md) | `wpctl`, `pactl`, `amixer`, `espeak-ng`, `paplay` |
| 音频设备切换 / 流路由 / 虚拟设备 | [references/audio-routing.md](references/audio-routing.md) | `wpctl`, `pactl`, `pavucontrol`, `qpwgraph` |
| 屏幕亮度 / 键盘背光 / 夜览 | [references/brightness.md](references/brightness.md) | `brightnessctl`, `ddcutil`, `wlsunset`, `gammastep` |
| 外观 / 暗黑模式 / 主题 / 壁纸 | [references/appearance.md](references/appearance.md) | `gsettings`, `lookandfeeltool`, `darkman` |
| 锁屏 / 休眠 / 防休眠 / 电量 / 充电上限 | [references/power.md](references/power.md) | `loginctl`, `systemctl`, `systemd-inhibit`, `upower`, `tlp` |
| Wi-Fi / DNS / 网速 / 诊断 | [references/network.md](references/network.md) | `nmcli`, `iw`, `resolvectl`, `rfkill`, `speedtest-cli` |
| 蓝牙 | [references/network.md](references/network.md) | `bluetoothctl`, `rfkill` |
| 窗口管理 / 多显示器布局 / 模拟键鼠 | [references/window-management.md](references/window-management.md) | `wmctrl`, `xdotool`, `swaymsg`, `hyprctl`, `xrandr`, `wlr-randr`, `ydotool` |
| 系统通知 / 勿扰 | [references/notifications.md](references/notifications.md) | `notify-send`, `dunstctl`, `makoctl` |
| App 启动 / 退出 / 默认应用 / 自启 | [references/apps.md](references/apps.md) | `xdg-open`, `gtk-launch`, `pkill`, `xdg-mime` |
| 截图 / 录屏 | [references/screenshot.md](references/screenshot.md) | `grim`+`slurp`, `gnome-screenshot`, `spectacle`, `maim`, `flameshot`, `wf-recorder` |
| 文件搜索 / 元数据 / 全文索引 | [references/file-search.md](references/file-search.md) | `plocate`, `fd`, `rg`, `tracker3`, `exiftool` |
| 磁盘 / 挂载 / 镜像 / SMART / 备份 | [references/disk.md](references/disk.md) | `lsblk`, `udisksctl`, `parted`, `dd`, `smartctl`, `borg`, `timeshift` |
| 防火墙 / LUKS / 密钥环 / AppArmor | [references/security.md](references/security.md) | `ufw`, `nft`, `cryptsetup`, `secret-tool`, `aa-status` |
| 服务 / 定时任务 / 时区 / 系统更新 | [references/services.md](references/services.md) | `systemctl`, `journalctl`, `timedatectl`, `apt`（内置） |
| 性能监控（CPU/内存/IO/温度/功耗/GPU） | [references/performance.md](references/performance.md) | `btop`, `iostat`, `sensors`, `powertop`, `nvtop` |
| 系统信息（硬件/版本/总线） | [references/system-info.md](references/system-info.md) | `lscpu`, `lspci`, `lsusb`, `dmidecode`, `inxi`（多内置） |
| 万能入口（没有专用 CLI 的功能） | [references/dbus.md](references/dbus.md) | `busctl`, `gdbus`, `playerctl` |

## 快速决策

```
需要控制什么？
├── 声音
│   ├── 调音量 / 静音           → audio.md（PipeWire 用 wpctl，其余 pactl/amixer）
│   ├── TTS / 播放音频          → audio.md §TTS
│   └── 切换输出/输入设备        → audio-routing.md
├── 屏幕
│   ├── 内屏亮度                → brightness.md §brightnessctl
│   ├── 外接显示器亮度           → brightness.md §ddcutil
│   ├── 键盘背光                → brightness.md §键盘背光
│   ├── 夜览 / 色温             → brightness.md §夜览
│   └── 多显示器排列 / 分辨率    → window-management.md §多显示器布局
├── 外观 / 暗黑模式 / 壁纸       → appearance.md
├── 电源
│   ├── 锁屏 / 关显示器 / 睡眠   → power.md
│   ├── 防止休眠                → power.md §systemd-inhibit
│   ├── 电量 / 电源策略          → power.md §电量信息
│   └── 充电上限                → power.md §充电上限
├── 网络
│   ├── Wi-Fi                   → network.md §nmcli
│   ├── 蓝牙                    → network.md §bluetoothctl
│   ├── DNS                     → network.md §DNS
│   └── 网速测试 / 诊断          → network.md §网速测试
├── 窗口
│   ├── X11 窗口控制            → window-management.md §wmctrl
│   ├── sway / Hyprland         → window-management.md §Wayland
│   ├── GNOME Wayland（受限）   → window-management.md §GNOME
│   └── 模拟键鼠                → window-management.md §ydotool
├── 通知 / 勿扰                 → notifications.md
├── App 管理 / 默认应用 / 自启   → apps.md
├── 截图 / 录屏                 → screenshot.md（先分 Wayland / X11）
├── 文件搜索
│   ├── 按文件名                → file-search.md §plocate / fd
│   ├── 按内容                  → file-search.md §ripgrep
│   └── 元数据                  → file-search.md §元数据
├── 磁盘
│   ├── 挂载 / 弹出 U 盘        → disk.md §挂载
│   ├── 分区 / 格式化            → disk.md §分区
│   ├── 镜像 / 刻录 ISO          → disk.md §磁盘镜像
│   ├── 健康检查                → disk.md §SMART
│   └── 备份（Time Machine 类）  → disk.md §备份
├── 安全
│   ├── 防火墙                  → security.md §防火墙
│   ├── 磁盘加密（LUKS）         → security.md §磁盘加密
│   ├── 密码 / 密钥环            → security.md §密钥环
│   ├── sudo / 组权限            → security.md §sudo
│   └── AppArmor / SELinux      → security.md §MAC
├── 服务 / 系统
│   ├── 服务启停 / 自启          → services.md §systemctl
│   ├── 用户级后台服务           → services.md §用户级服务
│   ├── 日志                    → services.md §journalctl
│   ├── 定时任务                → services.md §定时任务
│   ├── 主机名 / 时区 / 语言     → services.md §主机名
│   └── 系统更新                → services.md §系统更新
├── 性能 / 功耗 / 温度           → performance.md
├── 硬件信息                    → system-info.md
├── 媒体播放控制                 → dbus.md §MPRIS（playerctl）
└── 没有专用 CLI 的桌面功能      → dbus.md（busctl 探索三步法）
```

## 依赖工具汇总

### 内置（基础系统自带）

`systemctl` · `journalctl` · `loginctl` · `hostnamectl` · `timedatectl` · `localectl` · `busctl` · `systemd-inhibit` · `systemd-run` · `systemd-detect-virt` · `systemd-analyze` · `ip` · `ss` · `rfkill` · `lsblk` · `blkid` · `mount` · `fdisk` · `dd` · `losetup` · `free` · `vmstat` · `uptime` · `ps` · `pkill` · `pgrep` · `lscpu` · `uname` · `stat` · `file` · `dmesg` · `udevadm` · `crontab` · `df` · `du` · `find` · `grep`

桌面发行版一般还带：`nmcli` · `bluetoothctl` · `pactl` · `wpctl` · `gsettings` · `xdg-open` · `xdg-mime` · `upower` · `gdbus` · `notify-send`

### 需要安装（Debian/Ubuntu 包名；其他发行版名称基本一致）

| 工具 | 安装 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| `brightnessctl` | `apt install brightnessctl` | 内屏亮度 / 键盘背光 |
| `ddcutil` | `apt install ddcutil` | 外接显示器亮度（DDC/CI） |
| `wlsunset` / `gammastep` | `apt install wlsunset gammastep` | Wayland 夜间色温 |
| `acpi` | `apt install acpi` | 电池信息一行版 |
| `tlp` | `apt install tlp` | 笔记本省电 / 充电阈值 |
| `speedtest-cli` | `apt install speedtest-cli` | 网速测试 |
| `mtr` | `apt install mtr-tiny` | 路由质量诊断 |
| `wmctrl` / `xdotool` | `apt install wmctrl xdotool` | X11 窗口控制 / 模拟键鼠 |
| `wlr-randr` | `apt install wlr-randr` | wlroots 显示器布局 |
| `ydotool` | `apt install ydotool` | Wayland 模拟键鼠 |
| `dunst` / `mako` | `apt install dunst` | 裸 WM 的通知守护进程 |
| `grim` + `slurp` | `apt install grim slurp` | Wayland 截图 + 框选 |
| `flameshot` | `apt install flameshot` | 交互式截图标注 |
| `maim` | `apt install maim` | X11 截图 |
| `wf-recorder` | `apt install wf-recorder` | wlroots 录屏 |
| `wl-clipboard` / `xclip` | `apt install wl-clipboard xclip` | 剪贴板（wl-copy / xclip） |
| `plocate` | `apt install plocate` | 索引式文件名搜索 |
| `fd` | `apt install fd-find`（命令名 `fdfind`） | 实时文件查找 |
| `rg` | `apt install ripgrep` | 内容搜索 |
| `udisksctl` | `apt install udisks2` | 免 sudo 挂载 U 盘 |
| `smartctl` | `apt install smartmontools` | 磁盘健康 |
| `ncdu` / `duf` | `apt install ncdu duf` | 磁盘用量分析 |
| `borg` / `restic` / `timeshift` | `apt install borgbackup restic timeshift` | 备份 / 系统快照 |
| `ufw` | `apt install ufw` | 防火墙前端 |
| `cryptsetup` | `apt install cryptsetup` | LUKS 磁盘加密 |
| `secret-tool` | `apt install libsecret-tools` | 密钥环读写 |
| `htop` / `btop` | `apt install htop btop` | 系统监控 TUI |
| `sysstat` | `apt install sysstat` | iostat / pidstat / sar |
| `lm-sensors` | `apt install lm-sensors` | 温度 / 风扇 |
| `powertop` | `apt install powertop` | 功耗分析 |
| `nvtop` | `apt install nvtop` | GPU 监控 |
| `inxi` / `fastfetch` | `apt install inxi fastfetch` | 系统信息全览 |
| `playerctl` | `apt install playerctl` | 媒体播放控制（MPRIS） |
| `espeak-ng` | `apt install espeak-ng` | TTS |
| `mpv` | `apt install mpv` | 万能音视频播放 |

name: operate-macos
body:
# 用命令行操作 macOS 系统设置

不打开「系统设置」也能通过 shell / agent 控制所有常见系统参数。按需查阅对应参考文档。

## 导航

| 类别 | 参考文档 | 关键工具 |
|------|---------|---------|
| 音量 / 静音 / TTS | [references/audio.md](references/audio.md) | `osascript`, `say`, `afplay`（内置） |
| 音频设备切换 / 麦克风 | [references/audio-routing.md](references/audio-routing.md) | `SwitchAudioSource`, `select_audio_output`, BlackHole |
| 屏幕亮度 / 键盘背光 | [references/brightness.md](references/brightness.md) | `brightness`, `m1ddc`, `ddcctl`, BetterDisplay, Lunar |
| 外观 / 暗黑模式 / 夜览 | [references/appearance.md](references/appearance.md) | `osascript`, `defaults`（内置） |
| 锁屏 / 休眠 / 防休眠 / 电量 | [references/power.md](references/power.md) | `pmset`, `caffeinate`, `battery`（内置+第三方） |
| Wi-Fi / 网速测试 / 诊断 | [references/network.md](references/network.md) | `networksetup`, `networkQuality`, `wdutil`（内置） |
| 蓝牙 | [references/network.md](references/network.md) | `blueutil` |
| 窗口管理 / 多显示器布局 | [references/window-management.md](references/window-management.md) | `displayplacer`, `yabai`, `skhd`, Hammerspoon, Rectangle |
| 系统通知 | [references/notifications.md](references/notifications.md) | `osascript`, `terminal-notifier` |

| 截图 | [references/screenshot.md](references/screenshot.md) | `screencapture`（内置） |

| 磁盘 / 镜像 / Time Machine | [references/disk.md](references/disk.md) | `diskutil`, `hdiutil`, `tmutil`（内置） |
| 安全 / 隐私 / 权限 | [references/security.md](references/security.md) | `tccutil`, `spctl`, `security`, `xattr`, `fdesetup`（内置） |
| 服务 / launchd / 系统配置 | [references/services.md](references/services.md) | `launchctl`, `scutil`, `nvram`, `softwareupdate`（内置） |
| 性能监控（CPU/GPU/温度/功耗） | [references/performance.md](references/performance.md) | `macmon`, `powermetrics`, `smctemp`, `bottom` |
| 系统信息（硬件/版本/磁盘） | [references/system-info.md](references/system-info.md) | `sysctl`, `sw_vers`, `system_profiler`（内置） |
| 万能入口（不记具体工具时） | [references/m-cli.md](references/m-cli.md) | `m-cli` |

## 快速决策

```
需要控制什么？
├── 声音
│   ├── 调音量 / 静音           → audio.md
│   ├── TTS / 播放音频          → audio.md §内置TTS
│   └── 切换输出/输入设备        → audio-routing.md
├── 屏幕
│   ├── 内屏亮度                → brightness.md §内置屏幕
│   ├── 外接显示器亮度           → brightness.md §外接显示器
│   ├── 键盘背光                → brightness.md §键盘背光
│   └── 多显示器排列 / 分辨率    → window-management.md §displayplacer
├── 外观                        → appearance.md
├── 电源
│   ├── 锁屏 / 关显示器 / 睡眠   → power.md §锁屏与休眠
│   ├── 防止休眠                → power.md §caffeinate
│   ├── 电量 / 电源策略          → power.md §电量信息
│   └── 充电上限（Apple Silicon） → power.md §battery
├── 网络
│   ├── Wi-Fi                   → network.md §Wi-Fi
│   ├── 蓝牙                    → network.md §蓝牙
│   └── 网速测试 / 诊断          → network.md §networkQuality
├── 窗口
│   ├── 平铺 / 键盘驱动          → window-management.md §yabai
│   ├── 快捷键半屏/居中          → window-management.md §Rectangle
│   └── Lua 脚本化自动化         → window-management.md §Hammerspoon
├── 通知                        → notifications.md
├── App 管理                    → apps.md
├── 截图                        → screenshot.md
├── Dock / Finder
│   ├── Dock 项目管理           → finder-dock.md §dockutil
│   └── Finder / defaults       → finder-dock.md
├── 文件搜索 / 元数据            → spotlight.md
├── 磁盘 / Time Machine
│   ├── 磁盘分区 / 格式化        → disk.md §diskutil
│   ├── 磁盘镜像                → disk.md §hdiutil
│   └── Time Machine 备份       → disk.md §tmutil
├── 安全 / 权限
│   ├── App 隐私权限             → security.md §tccutil
│   ├── Gatekeeper / 签名        → security.md §spctl
│   ├── Keychain / Wi-Fi 密码    → security.md §security
│   ├── 隔离标志（quarantine）   → security.md §xattr
│   └── FileVault               → security.md §fdesetup
├── 服务 / 系统
│   ├── 启动守护进程             → services.md §launchctl
│   ├── 系统更新                → services.md §softwareupdate
│   ├── 主机名 / VPN            → services.md §scutil
│   └── NVRAM 固件变量           → services.md §nvram
├── 性能 / 功耗 / 温度           → performance.md
├── 硬件信息                    → system-info.md
└── 不记具体工具                 → m-cli.md
```

## 依赖工具汇总

### 内置（无需安装）
`osascript` · `pmset` · `networksetup` · `networkQuality` · `wdutil` · `screencapture` · `defaults` · `open` · `caffeinate` · `powermetrics` · `nettop` · `iostat` · `vm_stat` · `top` · `sw_vers` · `system_profiler` · `sysctl` · `say` · `afplay` · `diskutil` · `hdiutil` · `tmutil` · `mdfind` · `mdls` · `mdutil` · `mdimport` · `launchctl` · `scutil` · `nvram` · `softwareupdate` · `tccutil` · `spctl` · `security` · `xattr` · `fdesetup` · `purge` · `dot_clean`

### 需要安装

| 工具 | 安装 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| `brightness` | `brew install brightness` | 内屏亮度 |
| `m1ddc` | `brew install m1ddc` | 外屏 DDC（Apple Silicon） |
| `ddcctl` | `brew install ddcctl` | 外屏 DDC（Intel） |
| `mac-brightnessctl` | `brew install rakalex/homebrew-mac-brightnessctl/mac-brightnessctl` | 键盘背光 |
| `blueutil` | `brew install blueutil` | 蓝牙开关 / 设备管理 |
| `SwitchAudioSource` | `brew install switchaudio-osx` | 音频设备切换 |
| `select_audio_output` | `brew install select-audio-output` | 模糊匹配音频设备切换 |
| `terminal-notifier` | `brew install terminal-notifier` | 脚本 / cron 发通知 |
| `displayplacer` | `brew install jakehilborn/jakehilborn/displayplacer` | 多显示器布局 |
| `yabai` | `brew install yabai` | 平铺窗口管理器 |
| `skhd` | `brew install skhd` | 热键守护进程（配合 yabai） |
| `dockutil` | `brew install dockutil` | Dock 项目精细管理 |
| `battery` | `brew install battery` | Apple Silicon 充电上限管理 |
| Hammerspoon | `brew install --cask hammerspoon` | Lua 脚本化 macOS API |
| Rectangle | `brew install --cask rectangle` | 快捷键窗口半屏/居中 |
| MonitorControl | `brew install --cask monitorcontrol` | 外屏亮度 GUI + CLI |
| BetterDisplay | `brew install --cask betterdisplay` | 外屏虚拟分辨率 / XDR 超亮度 |
| BlackHole | `brew install --cask blackhole-2ch` | 虚拟音频路由 |
| `macmon` | `brew install vladkens/tap/macmon` | Apple Silicon 实时功耗（无 sudo） |
| `smctemp` | `brew install smctemp` | CPU/GPU 温度 + 风扇 |
| `bottom` | `brew install bottom` | 跨平台系统监控 TUI |
| `m-cli` | `brew install m-cli` | macOS 操作瑞士军刀 |
| `ncdu` | `brew install ncdu` | 磁盘使用分析 TUI |

name: paper-meta-search
description: Use when the user wants to do semantic search over local papers in ~/papers using paper-meta — searching by natural language query, filtering by year/topic, warming the embedding cache, or combining SQL filtering with semantic ranking. Covers paper-meta search, query + search pipeline, grep with semantic rerank, and cache prefetch via generate-cache.sh.
body:
# Paper Meta Search

Use this skill when the user wants to find papers in `~/papers` by semantic
similarity, filter by year or topic, warm the embedding cache, or combine
structured SQL filters with semantic ranking.

## Preconditions

```bash
which paper-meta
paper-meta search --help
```

The installed binary is at `~/sync/bin_arm64/paper-meta`. If stale, rebuild:

```bash
cd ~/projects/models/paper-ops && just install
```

Embedding config is at `~/.paperset/conf.json`:

```json
{
  "embedding": {
    "provider": "yxt",
    "model": "bge-m3",
    "dimensions": 1024,
    "cache_dir": "~/cache/paper-ops/embeddings"
  }
}
```

Override the backend per-call with `--embedding ollama/bge-m3:latest`.

## Papers Root Layout

```
~/papers/
├── rag/          context-engineering/   skills/
├── LLM/          memory/                workflow/
├── Reasoning/    harness/               Code/
├── deepseek/     o1/                    autoui/
└── 2501-...      2604-...  (flat papers at root)
```

Always pick the **narrowest root** first. Only expand to `~/papers` when the
topic spans multiple subdirs.

## Two Embedding Layers

```
Layer 1 — abstract  : title + abstract_preview per paper
                      used by: paper-meta search
                      cache: warm after first run, ~instant after

Layer 2 — pages     : full page texts per paper
                      used by: paper-meta grep --semantic-rerank
                      cache: lazily built per query, persists after
```

First runs are slow (embedding computed); repeat runs are ~0ms (disk cache).
Cache key = provider + model + exact text. Changing `--embedding` invalidates.

## Core Commands

### Semantic Search (abstract layer)

```bash
# Within a topic dir
paper-meta search \
  --root ~/papers/rag \
  --query "retrieval augmented generation knowledge graph" \
  --limit 15 \
  --format plain \
  --relative

# Full corpus
paper-meta search \
  --root ~/papers \
  --query "agent skill learning internalization fine-tuning" \
  --limit 20 \
  --format plain \
  --relative
```

Flags:
- `--query`   natural language query text (embedded and ranked by cosine similarity)
- `--limit`   top-N results after ranking (does not skip embedding; all papers are embedded)
- `--format`  `plain` = `path\tscore\ttitle`; `preview-jsonl` = JSON with abstract; `paths` = paths only
- `--relative` paths relative to `--root`
- `--embedding` override backend, e.g. `ollama/bge-m3:latest`

### Filter by Year, Then Search

When the topic is year-bounded (e.g. `year >= 2026`), SQL-filter first into
a PaperSet, then rerank within it. Use Bash process substitution `<(...)` to
skip the temp file:

```bash
# 1. SQL filter → PaperSet (one command, no temp file)
paper-meta --json set create \
  --id research:topic-2026 \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets \
  --overwrite \
  --from <(paper-meta query --root ~/papers --format paths \
    "SELECT path FROM papers WHERE year >= 2026
     AND (title ILIKE '%agent%' OR title ILIKE '%skill%')")

# 2. Grep + semantic rerank within the set
paper-meta grep \
  "skill|agent|tuning|reinforcement" \
  --in research:topic-2026 \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets \
  --fields title,abstract \
  --topic "agent skill learning internalization LLM fine-tuning" \
  --semantic-rerank \
  --semantic-limit 20 \
  --id research:topic-2026-ranked \
  --overwrite \
  --max-tokens 4000 --matches-per-paper 2
```

### Composing Set Inputs with `<(...)`

`paper-meta set create --from <FILE>` opens whatever file path is passed.
Bash `<(cmd)` exposes `/dev/fd/N` backed by `cmd`'s stdout, so any producer
that emits one path/DOI/paperId per line can feed a set without a temp file.

| Producer | Emits | Good for |
|----------|-------|----------|
| `paper-meta query --format paths "SQL"` | paths | structured filters (year, title, missing fields) |
| `paper-meta search --format paths` | paths | semantic ranking |
| `paper-meta grep --format paths` | paths | regex match across fields |
| `printf '%s\n' DOI1 DOI2 ...` | DOIs | curated short lists (needs `--root`) |
| `cat curated.txt` | paths/DOIs | external lists |

`--from` is **repeatable**, so multiple `<(...)` producers can compose:

```bash
# Union of an SQL filter + a curated DOI list
paper-meta --json set create \
  --id research:union \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets \
  --root ~/papers \
  --overwrite \
  --from <(paper-meta query --root ~/papers --format paths \
    "SELECT path FROM papers WHERE year >= 2026 AND title ILIKE '%rag%'") \
  --from <(printf '%s\n' 2406.15319 2410.05779 2504.11544)
```

Note: `paper-meta query` does not currently support `--id` directly. The
`<(...)` pattern is the idiomatic way to materialize SQL results as a
PaperSet. `paper-meta search` and `paper-meta grep` already support `--id`
natively, so they don't need `<(...)` for set persistence.

### Grep + Semantic Rerank (page layer)

```bash
paper-meta grep \
  "graph|GraphRAG|hybrid|vector" \
  --in ~/papers/rag \
  --fields title,abstract,pages \
  --topic "hybrid graph vector retrieval augmented generation" \
  --semantic-rerank \
  --semantic-limit 10 \
  --id research:rag-graph \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets
```

`--semantic-limit N`: rerank is skipped when candidate count ≤ N (all returned
as-is). To force embedding on all candidates, set N smaller than expected hits.

## Year Filtering

`year >= 2026` requires the `year` field to be populated in `meta.json`.
If queries return empty results, backfill missing years:

```bash
cd ~/projects/models/papers
uv run papers sync --batch ~/papers --year
```

This derives year from the arXiv DOI prefix (`YYMM.xxxxx → 20YY`) and writes
it into each `meta.json`. Safe to re-run; skips papers that already have year.

## Embedding Cache Warmup

Cache is at `~/cache/paper-ops/embeddings/`. Pre-warm with the bundled script
at `paper-meta-search/generate-cache.sh` (or copy to `~/papers/`):

```bash
# Abstract layer only (fast, ~few minutes for ~9K papers)
bash generate-cache.sh

# Abstract + page layer for every paper (slow first run, resumable)
bash generate-cache.sh --pages

# Custom chunk size (default 500 papers/chunk)
CHUNK_SIZE=200 bash generate-cache.sh --pages
```

Logs to `~/papers/.cache-logs/warmup-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log`.

### How Cache Warming Works

**Layer 1** runs `paper-meta search --limit 999999` with a generic query.
`--limit` only affects output, not embedding: all papers are embedded and cached
regardless of limit.

**Layer 2** runs `paper-meta grep --semantic-rerank --semantic-limit 1` per
chunked PaperSet. `--semantic-limit 1` ensures candidate_count > limit, so
embedding is never skipped. Output is discarded; only the cache side-effect
matters.

### Layer 2 Algorithm (Resumable Chunked)

A single large PaperSet (e.g. 7000+ root papers) OOMs paper-meta because every
paper's full `meta.json` (pages can be 500KB each) loads into memory at once.
Solution: chunk + filesystem state markers.

```
1. /tmp/paper-meta-warmup-sets/all-papers.txt
     missing  → fresh start: find every paper dir under ~/papers
     exists   → previous run was interrupted; resume from existing chunks

2. /tmp/paper-meta-warmup-sets/chunk-NN
     missing  → split all-papers.txt into chunks of CHUNK_SIZE
     exists   → resume the existing slicing

3. For each chunk-NN:
     paper-meta set create --id warmup:chunk-NN --from chunk-NN
     paper-meta grep --in warmup:chunk-NN --semantic-rerank ...
     rm chunk-NN   (delete on success; next run skips this chunk)

4. When all chunk-* are gone:
     rm all-papers.txt   (done marker)
```

If a chunk hits an error or is Ctrl-C'd, re-running the script picks up from
the remaining `chunk-*` files. Already-embedded pages are disk-cache hits, so
even a partially-processed chunk is cheap to re-run.

### Common Pitfalls

- grep patterns use `|` for alternation (Rust regex), not `\|`. Use
  `"model|learning|retrieval"`; `"model\|learning\|retrieval"` matches the
  literal string and hits nothing.
- A single PaperSet >~1000 papers OOMs at the `load_grep_paper_from_dir` step.
  Chunking to 500 papers/set is the safe default.
- `paper-meta grep --in <set-id>` requires `--store <STORE>` matching where the
  set was saved.

## Operational Rules

- Do not run abstract search and page grep in the same pipeline expecting the
  same cache. They embed different texts.
- `paper-meta search` already batches all candidate texts in one `embed()` call
  (since paper-ops d9ac324). No per-paper loop overhead.
- After changing `--embedding` backend, the previous cache is not invalidated
  but will be miss-on-read for new backend entries. Old entries remain intact.
- Use `/tmp/paper-meta-research-sets` for draft PaperSets; use the default
  `~/.paperset/db` only for persistent named collections.
- First run on a large root with pages is expected to be slow (tens of seconds
  per topic dir). Second run is near-instant.

## Format Quick Reference

| Flag | `paper-meta search` output |
|------|---------------------------|
| `--format plain` | `path\tscore\ttitle` per line |
| `--format preview-jsonl` | JSON with rank, score, path, title, abstract_preview |
| `--format jsonl` | JSON with rank, score, path, title |
| `--format paths` | one path per line |

## Example: Full Workflow for a New Topic

```bash
# 1. Broad semantic search in relevant subdir
paper-meta search --root ~/papers/rag \
  --query "chunking text splitting strategy" \
  --limit 15 --format plain --relative

# 2. Expand to full corpus if needed
paper-meta search --root ~/papers \
  --query "chunking text splitting strategy" \
  --limit 15 --format plain --relative

# 3. Scope to year >= 2026 if wanted
paper-meta query --root ~/papers --format paths \
  "SELECT path FROM papers WHERE year >= 2026
   AND title ~* 'chunk|split|segment'" > /tmp/chunk-2026.txt

paper-meta --json set create \
  --id research:chunk-2026 \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets \
  --overwrite --from /tmp/chunk-2026.txt

paper-meta grep "chunk|split|segment" \
  --in research:chunk-2026 \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets \
  --fields title,abstract \
  --topic "text chunking splitting strategy for RAG" \
  --semantic-rerank --semantic-limit 10 \
  --id research:chunk-2026-final \
  --store /tmp/paper-meta-research-sets --overwrite
```

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/paper-ops` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/paper-ops/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/paper-ops/justfile` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/paper-ops/README.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/papers` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/papers/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/papers/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/papers/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/papers/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/paper-meta-search/SKILL.md` |

name: parse-document
description: Use when the user wants to parse one local document (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, EPUB, image, scanned PDF) into Markdown or text — for RAG ingest, LLM input, translation prep, summarization, or OCR. Wraps the local `docparse` CLI. Triggers include "parse this PDF", "convert DOCX to markdown", "OCR this scan", "fast PDF to markdown for RAG", "highest-quality PDF parsing", "which parser handles XLSX". For HTML (file or URL) prefer the specialized `html-to-markdown-cli` — it auto-fetches URLs with a browser-like User-Agent and is documented inline below. For editing documents in place or generating new docs, use `edit-office-document` instead.
body:
# Parse Document

Convert one document to Markdown/text using `docparse`. The CLI auto-picks the best parser by file extension and quality tier, with a fallback chain when a parser fails.

> **HTML shortcut:** for HTML input — local file, stdin, or remote URL — use [`html-to-markdown-cli`](#html-prefer-html-to-markdown-cli) instead of `docparse`. It's purpose-built (JohannesKaufmann/html-to-markdown v2 + tables), auto-detects file vs URL vs stdin, and ships a browser User-Agent so anti-bot pages (WeChat, Cloudflare) return real HTML.

## Core rules

- One input file per invocation. For batches, loop in a shell.
- Always pass `-o <path>` for non-trivial output. The default is stdout, which gets unwieldy for long PDFs.
- Don't overwrite the source file. Write to a new path.
- After every parse, verify the output exists, is non-empty, and looks plausible (`head`, `wc -l`, `grep` for headings). Empty output means the parser ran but extracted nothing — switch tiers.
- Quote paths. Paper directories often contain spaces and Unicode.
- For long-running parses (`--quality` on a multi-page paper, scanned PDFs), warn the user and consider running in the background.

## Quick start

```bash
# Default tier (balanced; mineru-pipeline for PDF, docling for DOCX/PPTX)
docparse input.pdf -o out.md

# Fast tier (no LLM, seconds; lower fidelity for math/tables)
docparse --fast input.pdf -o out.md

# Quality tier (mineru-vlm for PDF; minutes/page, best for scans + equations)
docparse --quality input.pdf -o out.md

# Pick a specific parser
docparse --parser docling input.docx -o out.md
docparse --parser mineru-vlm input.pdf -o out.md

# JSON output (text + image paths + parser metadata)
docparse --json input.pdf -o out.json

# Skip extracting inline base64 images (faster, smaller)
docparse --no-image-extract input.pdf -o out.md

# Show parser stderr/progress
docparse -v input.pdf -o out.md

# List parsers and live availability on this machine
docparse --list
```

## Tier semantics

| Tier | Speed | Best for | PDF parser | Other formats |
|------|-------|----------|------------|---------------|
| `--fast` | seconds | quick extract, RAG ingest at scale | pymupdf4llm | markitdown |
| (default) | tens of seconds | balanced; LaTeX formulas, tables | mineru-pipeline | docling |
| `--quality` | minutes per page | scanned PDFs, complex layouts, equations | mineru-vlm | docling |

## Format coverage (current registry)

```
PDF:        markitdown · pymupdf4llm · docling · marker · mineru-pipeline · mineru-vlm · unstructured
DOCX:       markitdown · docling · mammoth · unstructured
PPTX:       markitdown · docling · unstructured
HTML:       html-to-markdown-cli ← preferred (file / URL / stdin)
            · markitdown · unstructured (fallback only)
XLSX, EPUB, markitdown · unstructured
etc.
```

`docparse --list` shows the live matrix. If a parser shows `AVAILABLE no`, its env is missing — run `just install-<parser>` in `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/`.

## How to pick a parser

Default to `docparse <file> -o out.md` (no flags) and look at the output. Escalate from there:

- **Scanned / image-only PDF** → `--quality` (mineru-vlm does OCR). Fast/default tiers will produce empty text.
- **Math-heavy paper** → `--quality` for best LaTeX recovery; default (`mineru-pipeline`) is usually close.
- **DOCX with complex layout** → default picks `docling`. If table cells leak or styles drop, try `--parser mammoth` (semantic-preserving DOCX → HTML → md).
- **PPTX** → default picks `docling`. Slide-by-slide preservation is best in docling; for raw text only, `--parser markitdown` is faster.
- **HTML (file, URL, or stdin)** → use `html-to-markdown-cli` instead — see the dedicated section below. Only fall back to `docparse --parser markitdown` if that tool's output is somehow inadequate.
- **XLSX, EPUB, RTF, eml, etc.** → `--fast` uses `markitdown`, which has the broadest format coverage at the cost of fidelity.
- **Batch / RAG ingestion / text-only PDFs** → `--fast` is fine; pymupdf4llm is fast and decent on text PDFs.
- **Comparing parsers** on the same file → run each with `--parser <name> -o out-<name>.md` and diff.

## HTML: prefer `html-to-markdown-cli`

For any HTML input, reach for the standalone tool first. It lives at
`~/projects/golang/html-to-markdown-cli/` and installs to
`~/sync/bin_<arch>/html-to-markdown-cli`.

Why it beats routing HTML through `docparse`:

- Wraps `github.com/JohannesKaufmann/html-to-markdown/v2` with the CommonMark
  and table plugins — clean fenced code, lists, blockquotes, pipe tables, and
  resolved image/link URLs.
- One positional argument covers all three input modes:

  | INPUT                | meaning                          |
  | -------------------- | -------------------------------- |
  | *omitted* or `-`     | read HTML from **stdin**         |
  | `path/to/file.html`  | read from a **local file**       |
  | `https://…`          | **fetch** over HTTP(S)           |

- URL mode sends a desktop-Chrome User-Agent (`-A` to override) plus `Accept`
  and `Accept-Language` headers, so pages that anti-bot Go's default UA
  (WeChat `mp.weixin.qq.com` returns a 211-byte "环境异常" placeholder
  otherwise) come back as real article HTML.
- In URL mode, `--domain` auto-defaults to the URL's origin, so relative
  links like `/about` resolve naturally.

```bash
# Three input shapes
cat page.html | html-to-markdown-cli                    # stdin
html-to-markdown-cli page.html -o page.md               # local file
html-to-markdown-cli https://example.com -o example.md  # remote URL

# Customize the UA for a stubborn site, or to identify a bot politely
html-to-markdown-cli https://example.com -A "MyBot/1.0 (+contact)"

# Override the base used to resolve relative links
html-to-markdown-cli page.html --domain https://example.com
```

Flags worth knowing: `-o/--output FILE`, `--domain URL`, `--timeout DURATION`
(default `30s`), `-A/--user-agent STRING`, `-v/--version`.

Exit codes: `0` on success, `1` on fetch/IO error (e.g. HTTP 4xx, file not
found), `2` on misuse (more than one positional).

Fall back to `docparse --parser markitdown` only if `html-to-markdown-cli`
produces something unexpectedly bad on a specific page. Common non-issues:

- WeChat / many CMS pages embed UI chrome (modal buttons, "微信扫一扫",
  share bars). The converter renders them faithfully because they are in
  the HTML — that is content noise, not a converter bug. Stripping requires
  a content-selector step that is out of scope here.
- Author choices like "use `<h1>` for every section divider" or "literal
  bullets inside `<li>`" pass through verbatim. The converter is honest.

## Verify the output

```bash
wc -l out.md                   # not empty
head -40 out.md                # title / abstract / section heading on top
grep -cE '^#+ ' out.md         # ≥1 heading for most structured docs
ls images/ 2>/dev/null         # if image extraction was on
```

Common failure modes:

| Symptom | Likely cause | Action |
|---------|--------------|--------|
| Empty `out.md` | scanned PDF parsed with text-only tier | Re-run with `--quality`. |
| `ParseError: <name> failed; tried [...]` | every parser in the tier failed | Re-run with `-v` to see the underlying stderr; check `docparse --list`. |
| Garbled equations | wrong tier for math fidelity | Swap `--quality` ↔ default. |
| Missing tables | low-fidelity parser chosen | Use `--parser docling` or `--parser mineru-pipeline`. |
| `mineru` startup hangs ~30s on first run | model download from HuggingFace | Wait once; cached for subsequent runs. |
| Long absolute paths leak into output | parser embedded a tmp path | File a bug — `ParseResult.source` should be the user-supplied path; do NOT silently rewrite. |

## Long-running parses

`mineru-vlm` (`--quality`) on a 16-page paper can take 10–30 minutes on Apple Silicon, dominated by per-page VLM inference. For multi-page papers:

```bash
# Background, with a sentinel file to signal completion
docparse --quality "paper.pdf" -o out.md -v > parse.log 2>&1 &
echo $! > parse.pid
```

If iterating on a single page, use `mineru` directly with `-s/-e`:

```bash
PYTHONPATH="" mineru -p paper.pdf -o /tmp/page0 -b vlm-auto-engine -s 0 -e 0
```

## When NOT to use this skill

- **Editing a doc in place** (rewrite a paragraph, change styles, set a footer, recompute formulas) → `edit-office-document`.
- **Generating a doc from scratch** (Markdown → DOCX/PDF/PPTX) → `pandoc`, `pptxgenjs`, or `edit-office-document`.
- **Pure OCR of a single image (PNG/JPG, no PDF)** → the `ocr` skill / `tesseract`. (`docparse` doesn't accept image files directly; use `mineru -p img.jpg -o ...` or convert to a 1-page PDF.)
- **Diffing or merging documents** → `edit-office-document`.

## Internals (reference)

`docparse` is the CLI of the `docparser` package at `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/`. Engine pipeline (see `src/docparser/docparser/engine.py`):

1. Pick parser by `(ext, tier)` from the registry, or by `--parser <name>`.
2. Check `available()` — env exists + binary in PATH.
3. If parser doesn't support the format, fall back to `markitdown`.
4. Run `parser.run()`. On `ParseError`, retry with the next parser in the same tier (deduped via `tried` list to prevent loops).
5. Optionally extract inline base64 images to a sibling `images/` directory.
6. Return `ParseResult { text, images, metadata }`.

Adding a new parser, fixing a broken env, or upgrading mineru: see `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/CLAUDE.md`.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/doc-parsers` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/doc-parsers/README.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/golang/html-to-markdown-cli` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/golang/html-to-markdown-cli/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/golang/html-to-markdown-cli/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/golang/html-to-markdown-cli/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/parse-document/SKILL.md` |

name: prd-to-issues
description: Use when the user has a PRD and wants it broken into independently-grabbable GitHub issues — each a vertical slice or "tracer bullet" — for handing off to multiple workers or AFK agents. Distinct from `prd-to-plan`, which writes a local Markdown plan instead of GitHub issues.
body:
# PRD to Issues

Break a PRD into independently-grabbable GitHub issues using vertical slices (tracer bullets).

## Process

### 1. Locate the PRD

Ask the user for the PRD GitHub issue number (or URL).

If the PRD is not already in your context window, fetch it with `gh issue view <number>` (with comments).

### 2. Explore the codebase (optional)

If you have not already explored the codebase, do so to understand the current state of the code.

### 3. Draft vertical slices

Break the PRD into **tracer bullet** issues. Each issue is a thin vertical slice that cuts through ALL integration layers end-to-end, NOT a horizontal slice of one layer.

Slices may be 'HITL' or 'AFK'. HITL slices require human interaction, such as an architectural decision or a design review. AFK slices can be implemented and merged without human interaction. Prefer AFK over HITL where possible.

<vertical-slice-rules>
- Each slice delivers a narrow but COMPLETE path through every layer (schema, API, UI, tests)
- A completed slice is demoable or verifiable on its own
- Prefer many thin slices over few thick ones
</vertical-slice-rules>

### 4. Quiz the user

Present the proposed breakdown as a numbered list. For each slice, show:

- **Title**: short descriptive name
- **Type**: HITL / AFK
- **Blocked by**: which other slices (if any) must complete first
- **User stories covered**: which user stories from the PRD this addresses

Ask the user:

- Does the granularity feel right? (too coarse / too fine)
- Are the dependency relationships correct?
- Should any slices be merged or split further?
- Are the correct slices marked as HITL and AFK?

Iterate until the user approves the breakdown.

### 5. Create the GitHub issues

For each approved slice, create a GitHub issue using `gh issue create`. Use the issue body template below.

Create issues in dependency order (blockers first) so you can reference real issue numbers in the "Blocked by" field.

<issue-template>
## Parent PRD

#<prd-issue-number>

## What to build

A concise description of this vertical slice. Describe the end-to-end behavior, not layer-by-layer implementation. Reference specific sections of the parent PRD rather than duplicating content.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
- [ ] Criterion 3

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #<issue-number> (if any)

Or "None - can start immediately" if no blockers.

## User stories addressed

Reference by number from the parent PRD:

- User story 3
- User story 7

</issue-template>

Do NOT close or modify the parent PRD issue.

name: prd-to-plan
description: Use when the user has a PRD and wants a multi-phase local implementation plan saved as a Markdown file under `./plans/`, mentions tracer bullets, vertical slices, or a phased rollout. Distinct from `prd-to-issues`, which files GitHub issues instead of writing a local plan.
body:
# PRD to Plan

Break a PRD into a phased implementation plan using vertical slices (tracer bullets). Output is a Markdown file in `./plans/`.

## Process

### 1. Confirm the PRD is in context

The PRD should already be in the conversation. If it isn't, ask the user to paste it or point you to the file.

### 2. Explore the codebase

If you have not already explored the codebase, do so to understand the current architecture, existing patterns, and integration layers.

### 3. Identify durable architectural decisions

Before slicing, identify high-level decisions that are unlikely to change throughout implementation:

- Route structures / URL patterns
- Database schema shape
- Key data models
- Authentication / authorization approach
- Third-party service boundaries

These go in the plan header so every phase can reference them.

### 4. Draft vertical slices

Break the PRD into **tracer bullet** phases. Each phase is a thin vertical slice that cuts through ALL integration layers end-to-end, NOT a horizontal slice of one layer.

<vertical-slice-rules>
- Each slice delivers a narrow but COMPLETE path through every layer (schema, API, UI, tests)
- A completed slice is demoable or verifiable on its own
- Prefer many thin slices over few thick ones
- Do NOT include specific file names, function names, or implementation details that are likely to change as later phases are built
- DO include durable decisions: route paths, schema shapes, data model names
</vertical-slice-rules>

### 5. Quiz the user

Present the proposed breakdown as a numbered list. For each phase show:

- **Title**: short descriptive name
- **User stories covered**: which user stories from the PRD this addresses

Ask the user:

- Does the granularity feel right? (too coarse / too fine)
- Should any phases be merged or split further?

Iterate until the user approves the breakdown.

### 6. Write the plan file

Create `./plans/` if it doesn't exist. Write the plan as a Markdown file named after the feature (e.g. `./plans/user-onboarding.md`). Use the template below.

<plan-template>
# Plan: <Feature Name>

> Source PRD: <brief identifier or link>

## Architectural decisions

Durable decisions that apply across all phases:

- **Routes**: ...
- **Schema**: ...
- **Key models**: ...
- (add/remove sections as appropriate)

---

## Phase 1: <Title>

**User stories**: <list from PRD>

### What to build

A concise description of this vertical slice. Describe the end-to-end behavior, not layer-by-layer implementation.

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
- [ ] Criterion 3

---

## Phase 2: <Title>

**User stories**: <list from PRD>

### What to build

...

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ...

<!-- Repeat for each phase -->
</plan-template>

name: process-task-queue
description: Use when the user wants to pick up the next item from a file-based task queue with `tasks/` → `in-processing/` → `done/` subdirectories — claiming, working, and moving one task per invocation. Triggers include "process task", "run next task", "pick up a task", "check the task queue", "处理任务", "执行下一个任务", "查看任务队列", "领取任务", or any reference to that queue layout.
body:
# 任务处理器

你负责管理一个基于文件的任务队列。任务在当前工作目录下的三个子目录间流转：

| 目录              | 用途                         |
|-------------------|------------------------------|
| `tasks/`          | 待处理 — 新提交的任务文件      |
| `in-processing/`  | 执行中 — 当前正在处理的任务    |
| `done/`           | 归档 — 已完成（成功或失败）    |

每个任务是一个 Markdown 文件。每次调用只处理**一个任务**，处理完毕后退出。

> **前置检查：** 开始前先确认当前目录下存在 `tasks/`、`in-processing/`、`done/` 三个子目录。
> 如果缺少任何一个，提示用户并停止，不要自行创建。

> **同步感知：** 此队列可能通过 Dropbox 或类似云同步服务在多台机器间共享。
> 文件可能出现部分同步的情况，两台机器可能同时抢占同一个任务。
> 以下规则专门为安全处理这些场景而设计。

---

## 阶段一 — 领取任务

1. 用 `Glob` 列出当前目录下 `tasks/*.md`。
2. **忽略冲突文件** — 跳过匹配 `* (*.md` 模式的文件（如 Dropbox 的"conflicted copy"文件），这些是同步产物，不是真正的任务。
3. **没有文件？** 输出 `NO TASK FOUND` 并立即停止，不做其他操作。
4. **多个文件？** 按修改时间选择最早的一个。
5. 读取文件内容，理解任务要求。
6. **检查文件内容是否稳定** — 文件很小（< 10 字节）可能正在同步中。等待 2 秒后重新读取并比较。如果内容发生了变化，再等 2 秒重新读取一次。若仍在变化，跳过该文件并警告"文件似乎仍在同步中"，然后尝试下一个。
7. **声明任务所有权** — 用主机名标记后移动文件：
   ```bash
   HOSTNAME=$(hostname -s)
   mv "tasks/<filename>" "in-processing/<filename>.${HOSTNAME}.lock"
   sleep 2
   # 检查是否出现了冲突副本 — 如果有，说明另一台机器也在抢占
   ls in-processing/<filename>.*.lock 2>/dev/null | wc -l
   ```
   - 如果只有**一个** `.lock` 文件（你的）：你拥有该任务。重命名为正式文件名：
     ```bash
     mv "in-processing/<filename>.${HOSTNAME}.lock" "in-processing/<filename>"
     ```
   - 如果存在**多个** `.lock` 文件：另一个 agent 也声明了此任务。主机名按字母序排列**最前**的机器获胜。如果是你，删除其他 lock 文件并继续；否则删除你的 lock 文件，尝试下一个任务。

**安全边界：**
- 不要触碰 `in-processing/` 中不带你的 `.lock` 后缀的文件 — 那属于其他 agent。
- 如果文件为空或没有可执行的内容，直接跳到阶段三，标记为 `FAILED`，原因为"任务文件为空或不包含可执行指令"。

---

## 阶段二 — 执行任务

### 2a. 记录开始

在 `in-processing/` 中的文件末尾追加执行头：

```markdown

---
## 执行日志

**开始时间:** YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
**执行主机:** <hostname>
**状态:** 执行中
```

用 `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` 获取当前 UTC 时间戳，用 `hostname -s` 获取主机名。

### 2b. 执行工作

仔细阅读任务描述，使用所有可用工具（Bash、Read、Write、Edit、Glob、Grep、Agent）完成任务。

- 如果任务描述模糊，按你的最佳判断执行，并在日志中记录你的假设。
- 对可并行的子任务，适当使用 `Agent` 工具分发子 agent。
- 专注于任务要求的内容 — 不要过度设计或添加未要求的功能。

### 2c. 记录进度

工作过程中，在执行日志末尾追加简短的单行记录：

```markdown
- [HH:MM:SS] 完成了 X 步骤
- [HH:MM:SS] 发现问题 Y — 正在应用修复 Z
```

保持简洁。每个重要操作或决策一行。不要记录每次工具调用 — 只记录结果和关键转折点。

---

## 阶段三 — 收尾

当任务完成或不可恢复地失败时：

1. 确定结果：`SUCCESS` 或 `FAILED`。

2. 追加结果块：

   ```markdown

   ---
   **结束时间:** YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
   **状态:** SUCCESS | FAILED
   **摘要:** <一句话描述完成了什么或失败的原因>
   ```

3. 移到 done：
   ```bash
   mv "in-processing/<filename>" "done/<filename>"
   ```

4. 输出最终状态行：
   ```
   TASK COMPLETE: <STATUS> — <filename>
   ```

---

## 代理输出会被自动追加

如果是通过 `task-manager run` 触发的（绝大多数场景），监控进程会
pipe 你的 stdout/stderr，等你退出后**自动在 done 文件末尾追加**一个
`## 代理输出` 段落（必要时还会有 `## 代理输出 (stderr)`），用四个
反引号围起来。所以：

- **不要再把你的回答内容手抄一份贴进文件里** —— stdout 本身就会被
  落盘。你只需要保留正常的 `执行日志` + 状态 footer + `mv` 三件套。
- 你的 stdout 用人类可读的方式写就行（不需要刻意 JSON 化），最终
  内容会原样放在 `## 代理输出` 段里。
- 输出超过 64 KiB 时会从前部截断（保留尾部，前面加一行
  `[…前部已截断…]`），所以最关键的总结放在 stdout 的最后。

如果你是通过纯手动的方式（没经过 `task-manager run`）在跑这条
queue 协议，那就没有这一步 —— 完全按上面的阶段一/二/三操作即可。

---

## 规则

1. **每次只处理一个任务。** 将结果移动到 `done/` 后立即停止。
2. **永远不要遗弃文件。** 即使执行中出了问题，也必须完成收尾（标记 FAILED）并移动到 `done/`。`in-processing/` 中的文件不能没有最终状态。
3. **尊重其他 agent。** 只从 `tasks/` 领取任务。不要碰 `in-processing/` 中的文件（除非是解决你参与的 lock 冲突）。
4. **所有路径为相对路径**，基于当前工作目录。
5. **时间戳使用 UTC**，ISO 8601 格式。
6. **忽略同步产物。** 不要处理 Dropbox/Syncthing 的"conflicted copy"文件或 `.dropbox` 元数据。如果在 `tasks/` 中发现这类文件，直接删除。
7. **标记执行主机。** 在执行日志头部包含 `hostname`，明确记录哪台机器处理了此任务。

name: propose-idea
description: Use when the user wants to propose a new idea, add a draft to `ideas/`, scratch a `proposed`-status idea file, or follow the I-NNN-slug.md convention. Sibling of `research-idea` (advance), `clarify-idea` (resolve open clarifications), and `execute-idea` (carry out plan). Authored prose defaults to Simplified Chinese unless the user specifies otherwise.
body:
# Propose idea

Author a **new** idea at lifecycle status **`proposed`**. The `idea` CLI does **not** create files; it can only print the next numeric id (`--next-id`). File creation is always a write by editor, template, or agent.

**What this skill produces:** one conforming **IDEA markdown file** per `spec/02` / `spec/03` — not a full RFC thread, not an HTML PRD artefact, and not an implementation plan. Deep comparison of options and evidence-heavy analysis belong in **`research-idea`** (and later `## Plan` when promoted).

**Default stance (unless the user explicitly asks otherwise):** keep **`## Idea`** **directional and conceptual** — problem framing, underlying intent, rough scope boundaries, and honest assumptions. Avoid prescribing detailed implementation, step-by-step execution, or heavy proof: that belongs in **`research-idea`**. If the user wants a spec-heavy first draft anyway, follow their instructions and still keep the file conformant.

**Stop unnecessary specificity:** do **not** lock **technical execution forks** here (libraries, APIs, migration mechanics beyond “might touch X subsystem”). **`Compared directions`** = **strategic / conceptual** (whether / which initiative-level fork); **`research-idea`** **`Candidate approaches`** = **technical paths**.

**Typical clarification mode:** This skill is **user-triggered** — use interactive prompts (multi-choice in chat / ask-user tools), then persist **`### 澄清记录`** with **`[x]`** after answers. **`research-idea`** scheduled without a human in the loop does the opposite for pending forks: **only** records unchecked options in **`## Research`** / **`## Notes`** and defers **`[x]`** to a later pass (**Interactive vs non-interactive runs** in **`research-idea`**).

## Clarifying the proposal (before writing)

**Language (authored prose):** Unless the user **explicitly** asks for another language, write **`title`** (frontmatter) and **everything inside `## Idea`** — paragraphs and bullets — in **Simplified Chinese (简体中文)**. Keep canonical Markdown headings exactly **`## Idea`** / optional **`## Research`** etc. per spec §02 §4 (those headings stay English). **Inline identifiers** (file paths, crate names, CLI flags, RFC2119 keywords if quoting normative text) may stay ASCII; add brief Chinese gloss when helpful.

Clarification prompts to the user: prefer **简体中文**.

A **`proposed`** idea is **pre-research** by design: depth belongs in **`research-idea`**, not in endless open-ended Q&A while drafting. When the user’s pitch is vague, oversized, or internally inconsistent, **slow down** and tighten intent **before** you touch the filesystem.

Use the same spirit as the **`grill-me`** skill: walk the decision tree **one question at a time**, resolve branches in dependency order, and state your **recommended option** when it helps.

**Default question shape: multi-choice.** Most prompts to the user should look like:

- Labelled options **A / B / C** (add **D** only when needed).
- One line per option: what it implies for scope, risk, or dependencies.
- Always include **Other — reply in your own words** so nothing is forced.

Prefer presenting choices using **blockquote + GFM task lists** — same portable Markdown shape you will paste back into the idea file (**Recording user choices** below).

Open-ended questions are fine for follow-up **after** they pick an option (or Other). Wide-open “what are you trying to do?” as the *first* move tends to waste turns when the idea still needs structure.

### Recording user choices (canonical Markdown)

After the user answers, **persist what they chose** in the idea file so **`research-idea`** and later readers see both **the prompt** and **the outcome**.

**Rules:**

- Use **only portable Markdown**: **blockquote (`>`) + checklist (`- [ ]` / `- [x]`)**. Do **not** use `::: info` / admonition containers — many renderers treat them as plain text.
- **In chat:** show unchecked boxes so they can reply (“A+C”, “1 and 3”, or tick metaphorically).
- **In the file:** turn chosen rows into **`[x]`**, leave unchosen as **`[ ]`** (or drop unused rows only if the team prefers — default **keep** for audit trail).
- Prompt lines inside the quote stay **简体中文** when prose defaults to Chinese (“可多选” / “单选” as applicable).
- **Selection gate (blocking rounds):** any clarification that **must** be resolved before you continue MUST leave **`≥ 1`** checklist row as **`[x]`** in the eventual **`### 澄清记录`**. **Single-select** ⇒ **exactly one** `[x]` among the listed options **unless** the user commits via **`其他`** — then **`[x] 其他`** plus their verbatim text counts as the selection. **Multi-select** ⇒ **at least one** `[x]` (often several). If they answer with **zero** selections, vagueness, or “跳过” without an explicit **minimal-stub / waive** agreement on record — **do not** create the **`proposed`** file or hand off to **`research-idea`** for that thread; re-prompt once, then stop or defer per **`### Autonomous clarification guardrails`** (never fabricate `[x]`).

**Prompt shape** (unchecked — mirror this when messaging the user):

```markdown
> **澄清：** 可多选 / 单选（按题干注明）
>
> - [ ] 选项 A — …
> - [ ] 选项 B — …
> - [ ] 其他 — …（自由表述）
```

**Record shape** (after answers — write into the idea file):

```markdown
### 澄清记录

> **澄清：** 可多选 / 单选
>
> - [x] 选项 A — …
> - [x] 选项 B — …
> - [ ] 选项 C — …
```

**Placement (`proposed`):**

- Default: **`### 澄清记录`** at the **end of `## Idea`**, after the structured bullets（深层意图、方向对比 …），便于逐轮对照审计。
- If many rounds or long verbatim prompts clutter **`## Idea`**, move rounds to **`## Notes`** instead (`### 澄清记录（YYYY-MM-DD 或轮次）` per round).

English prose variant: keep headings **`### Clarification record`** and **`Clarify:`** inside the blockquote when the user asked English-authored ideas — **same checklist mechanics**.

### Surface vs deeper intent (ask when unclear)

You **may and should** ask the user to clarify **underlying intent** during propose — not full research, but enough that **`research-idea`** does not optimize for the wrong goal.

- **Surface:** what they literally asked for (feature text, “change X”, “add skill Y”).
- **Deeper:** durable outcome — reduce risk, save time, improve agent UX, clarify ownership, defer churn, etc.

Use **multi-choice first**: offer **A/B/C** framings of “what ‘done’ would mean for *you*” plus **Other**. Example stem: “If this idea succeeds in six months, which outcome matters most?” After they answer, add **one** short open prompt only if needed (“anything we’d regret optimizing away?”).

Record the resolved **underlying intent** in **`## Idea`** under **`深层意图：`** (see template). If user insists surface = intent, write **`深层意图：与字面提案一致`** — still makes the choice explicit.

### Minimum clarity bar (before creating the file)

Unless the user **explicitly** asks for a tiny stub only, do **not** write the file until these are settled (via dialogue or already present in their brief):

- **深层意图：** 写在 `## Idea` 的 **`深层意图：`** 下（见上），除非用户简报已足够清晰且你在该段逐字引用。
- **假设：** 至少一条明确假设；或写 **`假设：暂无（尚未识别）。`**。
- **决策标准：** 至少两条子弹 — 何种情况显然值得做 vs 显然不该做（各半行即可）。
- **方向对比：** 至少在战略/概念层对比 ≥2 条可行走向（推进 A vs B vs 暂不推进）— **不是** 实现层技术分叉（技术分叉归 **`research-idea`**）。现实可行时包含 **`暂不推进`**。
- **影响量级：** HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW（一轮多选题）；便于研究前对齐干系人预期。

Fold the resolved points into the **`## Idea`** section as short prose or bullets (see template below).

### Cheap repo signals (when you have a checkout)

If the workspace is a git repo, spend **a few minutes** grounding the proposal before writing:

- Skim **`README.md`** and, if present, **`docs/`** for overlap, contradictions, or duplicate themes.
- Optionally run **`idea -q`** (e.g. keyword or status filters) to spot **near-duplicate** ideas already on disk.
- If you find nothing: **say so in `## Idea`** with **`假设：`** — do not fabricate file paths or quotes.

**When to skip this section:** The user already supplied a concrete title, `## Idea` body, and target `ideas/` directory; or they explicitly ask for a minimal stub only.

### Autonomous clarification guardrails

When **no human is in the loop**, bound clarification churn:

- **Cap:** at most **3** user-visible multi-choice rounds **after** the initial pitch unless the user asks to continue.
- **If still blocked:** file anyway with **`假设：澄清延后待补充`** plus **`待澄清：`** bullets capturing unresolved forks — **or** stop and ask for explicit human input; **never** infinite ping-pong.
- **Resume mechanism:** files left with unresolved **`待澄清：`** bullets — especially once `research-idea` later authors a `### 澄清记录` blockquote-plus-GFM-checklist with all rows `[ ]` and demotes to `suspended` — surface to **`idea --clarify`** (TUI; `spec/08-clarify.md`) and to the **`clarify-idea`** skill (chat). Both auto-discover that exact shape and let the human flip the chosen `[x]` byte-precisely, without needing `research-idea` or this skill to re-do work. The **propose** stage doesn't run `--clarify` itself (no `suspended` state at `proposed`), but the **same checkbox shape** authored here is what those resume tools later operate on — so write the prompt + choices conformantly.

Human-led sessions may exceed the cap when useful.

## Before writing

1. **Find the target `ideas/` directory** — final path component MUST be exactly `ideas` (lowercase). There may be several in a repo; pick the one in scope for this work.
2. **Confirm filename rules** — `I-<id>-<kebab-slug>.md`:
   - `id`: decimal digits only, zero-padded, **minimum width 3**, integer **≥ 1** (not `000`).
   - Within one directory, **all files use the same padding width**; next id is `max(existing) + 1` unless the author intentionally leaves a gap.
   - `slug`: one or more segments of **lowercase `a-z` and digits `0-9`**, segments joined by `-`. No uppercase in the slug.
3. **Next id** — If the `idea` binary is available:
   ```bash
   idea --next-id --dir /path/to/ideas
   ```
   Stdout is one line: the padded numeric id plus a newline (e.g. `008\n`). Strip the newline when interpolating into a filename.

   If you scan from a repo root instead: `idea --next-id --root /path/to/repo` — this only succeeds when **exactly one** `ideas/` directory exists under that root; otherwise the CLI exits `2` and tells you to pass `--dir` for disambiguation.

   **Race hint:** `--next-id` is advisory (§06 §9.4); two parallel authors can receive the same id. Optionally **`idea -q`** check whether `ideas/I-<id>-*.md` already exists before finalizing filename.

   **CLI discovery**: `idea` with no flags prints full help on stdout (exit `0`). Shell completions: `idea completion bash` (or `zsh`, `fish`, …) prints a script to stdout.

## Minimal conforming document

- **Encoding**: UTF-8, **no BOM**. First line is `---`.
- After the closing frontmatter `---`, **one blank line**, then body.
- **Frontmatter** (required keys, each once): `title` (**简体中文** one-line title by default — unless user chose English), `date` (`YYYY-MM-DD` calendar — “birthday” of the idea, not last modified), `status: proposed` (lowercase ASCII — schema keyword).
- **Do not** put idea id or short-title slug in YAML; they live **only** in the filename.
- **Body**: First canonical section MUST be **`## Idea`** (exact heading). For `proposed`, **Research / Plan / Notes are optional**. The **`## Idea` body MUST be non-empty** after trim (at least one sentence is enough).

Inside **`## Idea`**, optional **lightweight structure** (all pre-research — keep short). Use **Chinese labels** below when writing Chinese prose. When using labeled bullets, put **`深层意图：`** **first** immediately after the `## Idea` heading — canonical anchor for **`research-idea`** / **`execute-idea`**.

- **`问题：`** — 一段话说明谁受影响、痛点是什么、为何现在值得讨论。
- **`非目标：`** — 2–4 条，写明相邻但不纳入本期范围的工作。
- **`方向对比：`** — ≥2 条 **战略/概念级** 分叉（含「暂不推进」若有）。不写低级实现选型。
- **`成功信号（进入研究前）：`** — 一句话：什么情况就认为值得交给 **`research-idea`** 深挖。
- **`决策标准：`** — 至少两条子弹：如何判断值得做 vs 不值得。
- **`假设：`** — 明确列出；若没有则写 **`假设：暂无（尚未识别）。`**
- **`证据 / 假设：`** — 1–3 条仓库证据（路径、`idea -q` 结论摘录），缺失则写 **`假设：`** 并说明未发现冲突证据。

English mirror labels (**`Underlying intent:`**, …) are acceptable **only** when the user explicitly asked for English prose.

Template:

```markdown
---
title: <简体中文一行标题>
date: YYYY-MM-DD
status: proposed
---

## Idea

**深层意图：**
<长期结果导向：给谁带来什么收益，而非仅复述 title。若与字面诉求一致则写 **与字面提案一致**。>

**问题：**
<若干句：痛点、场景、为何现在值得立项。>

**非目标：**
- ...
- ...

**方向对比：**
- 方向 A — …
- 方向 B — …
- （暂不推进 — …）

**决策标准：**
- …
- …

**假设：**
- …

**成功信号（进入研究前）：**
<一句话，可观察或可验证。>

**证据 / 假设：**
<路径 / `idea -q` 摘录；或 **假设：**…>

<收尾：倾向性建议或仍开放的疑问（简体）。>

### 澄清记录

> **澄清：** <可多选 / 单选>
>
> - [x] <用户已选>
> - [ ] <未选>
```

Optional sections after Idea (still at `proposed`): `## Research`, `## Plan`, `## Notes` — only if useful; none are required for conformance. Omit **`### 澄清记录`** entirely when no checklist-style clarification occurred; use **`## Notes`** for bulky multi-round logs instead of stuffing **`## Idea`**.

## Checklist before done

- [ ] **`深层意图：`** 已写入 `## Idea` — 或用户明确只要极简 stub。
- [ ] Path ends with `/ideas/` and directory name is not capitalised variant.
- [ ] Filename matches `I-<digits>-<kebab-slug>.md` and id matches directory padding convention.
- [ ] Frontmatter three required fields present; `status` is exactly `proposed`.
- [ ] `## Idea` exists first among canonical sections and has non-trivial content.
- [ ] **Minimum clarity bar** satisfied — or user explicitly chose a **minimal stub** (then keep `## Idea` honestly thin and skip forced criteria).
- [ ] **Repo skim or explicit assumption:** evidence bullets in `## Idea`, or clear **`假设：`** where no evidence was found.
- [ ] **简体中文** prose inside `## Idea` / `title` — unless user explicitly chose English.
- [ ] **Multi-choice / checklist clarifications** copied into the file as **`### 澄清记录`** under **`## Idea`** (blockquote + **`[x]`** / **`[ ]`**) — or under **`## Notes`** when bulky / multi-round; **blocking** rounds show **`≥ 1`** **`[x]`** (single-select: exactly one primary **`[x]`** or **`[x]`** on **其他** + text).
- [ ] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## Optional verification

If `idea` is installed and the file path is known, **use query mode** (`--get` is not implemented yet):

```bash
idea -q "SELECT path, status, title FROM ideas WHERE path = '/absolute/path/to/ideas/I-NNN-slug.md'"
```

Use the same path spelling `idea -q 'SELECT path FROM ideas'` would print for that file (relative vs absolute must match the stored row).

Non-conforming files are excluded from `idea -q` results (stderr may list diagnostics); fix structure rather than waiting on `--set`/`--force` write escapes — those modes are not shipped yet.

## Reference
