uses it — never drop the flag.

Other interaction modes for reference (don't use these in the loop):
- `batchmode` — also non-interactive but suppresses most stdout; harder
  to grep for `^!` live.
- `scrollmode` — stops less often than default but can still block.
- `errorstopmode` (default) — blocks on every error. Never use from
  scripts.

If you ever see xelatex appear to hang with no new output, you almost
certainly forgot `-interaction=nonstopmode`. Kill it (`Ctrl-C` or `kill`)
and re-run with the flag.

### Font setup used in practice

Use the exact font setup that worked in the paper under
`/Users/junix/papers/.../zh/main.tex`, not just a bare font-family name.

1. Install **思源宋体 static OTF files** under
   `~/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/`. Prefer the static SC OTF release from
   Adobe Source Han Serif. Do not rely on a variable-font file alone if you
   want deterministic `Path=...` loading from XeLaTeX.
2. Verify the font is visible before touching the paper:

   ```bash
   fc-list | rg -i "Source Han Serif SC|思源宋体"
   fc-match "Source Han Serif SC"
   ```

   If nothing shows up, refresh the cache and re-check:

   ```bash
   fc-cache -fv ~/.fonts
   ```

3. On macOS, `\setCJKmainfont{Source Han Serif SC}` may still resolve to the
   wrong face or silently fall back, even when `fc-list` sees the font. Prefer
   a **path-first** branch that points XeLaTeX at the exact OTF file:

   ```latex
   \usepackage{ifxetex}
   \ifxetex
     \usepackage{xeCJK}
     \IfFileExists{/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf}{
       \setCJKmainfont[
         AutoFakeBold=false,
         AutoFakeSlant=false,
         BoldFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
         ItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
         BoldItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
         SmallCapsFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
         Path=/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/,
         Extension=.otf,
       ]{SourceHanSerifSC-Regular}
     }{
       \setCJKmainfont{Source Han Serif SC}
     }
   \fi
   ```

4. If the generated PDF makes Chinese look too heavy, `AutoFakeBold=false`
   alone is not always enough. Explicitly map `BoldFont` back to
   `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular`. Only use `SemiBold`/`Bold` if the paper really
   needs visible Chinese bold text.
5. If consistency matters more than style purity, it is acceptable to point
   `\setCJKmainfont`, `\setCJKsansfont`, and `\setCJKmonofont` to the same
   思源宋体 regular face. Reliability is more important than mixing several CJK
   families in this workflow.
6. Fallback order:
   local `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf` path →
   `Source Han Serif SC` family name →
   `Noto Serif CJK SC` if installed →
   last-resort system fallback. Do **not** switch to `Songti SC` just because
   it exists; it is not the project default.

---

## Step 1 — Find the Main TeX File

**What it is**: the `.tex` file with `\begin{document}`. Only ONE file in
the paper directory has it. That is the entry point you will compile.

**How to find it**:

```bash
ls *.tex                                   # list tex files
grep -l '\\begin{document}' *.tex          # the one with \begin{document}
```

**Priority order when multiple candidates**:
1. Check for a manifest: `00README.json`, `latexmkrc`, `.vscode/settings.json`
   may name it (look for a `toplevel` or `main_file` key).
2. `main.tex` > `paper.tex` > `<conference>_<year>.tex` (e.g. `neurips_2026.tex`)
3. Shortest filename containing `\begin{document}`.

**Real example** from `/Users/junix/papers/2604-KV Packet .../zh/`:
- `ls *.tex` showed 7 `.tex` files including `01_abstract.tex`, …,
  `06_conclusion.tex`, and `neurips_2026.tex`.
- `grep -l '\\begin{document}' *.tex` pointed to `neurips_2026.tex`.
- `00README.json` confirmed `"usage": "toplevel"` → `"neurips_2026.tex"`.

**Baseline compile — do this BEFORE translating anything.**

```bash
cd "<paper-dir>"
# NEVER delete *.bbl — arXiv-style papers ship a pre-built .bbl with no .bib
# source; removing it strands every \cite forever.
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out *.blg *.bcf *.run.xml *.toc
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex 2>&1 | grep -E "^!|^l\."
```

- No `^!` lines = clean baseline → proceed.
- Errors here = pre-existing problems. Fix them FIRST (missing packages,
  font issues, etc.). Translating on a broken baseline just confuses you
  later about what you broke.

While the baseline is still pristine, snapshot the source's control
sequence set so Step 7.3 has something to diff against later:

```bash
grep -ohrIE '\\[a-zA-Z]+' . --include='*.tex' | sort -u > /tmp/cmds.before
```

If you skip this snapshot, Step 7.3 falls back to `git diff` — workable
only if the directory is under version control with the pre-translation
state committed.

---

## Step 2 — Decide Whether to Flatten

Look at the main file:

```bash
grep -n '\\input\|\\include' <main>.tex
```

**Case A — no `\input`/`\include` in main**: content is inline. Skip
`flatten-paper`. Translate directly in the main file, section by section.
(This is what happened in the KV Packet example.)

**Case B — main uses `\input{secs/foo}` etc.**: flatten each input into
standalone sections and translate those. The main file then points at the
flattened parts.

### Flattening — when Case B applies

```bash
mkdir -p zh/parts
flatten-paper -f secs/intro.tex       -o zh/parts/intro.tex
flatten-paper -f secs/method.tex      -o zh/parts/method.tex
# ... one per section
```

- `flatten-paper -f IN -o OUT` inlines any nested `\input`s and strips
  `%`-comments. Always use `-o` — without it, it overwrites the input.
- Then repoint main to the new parts: change `\input{secs/intro}` →
  `\input{zh/parts/intro}`.

**Warning**: if you find pre-existing split files (e.g. `01_abstract.tex`)
that main does NOT `\input`, they may be stale snapshots. Do not translate
them — they will not show up in the PDF. Translate where `\input` actually
points, or fall back to Case A (inline main).

---

## Step 3 — Add xeCJK to the Preamble (CRITICAL)

Without this, Chinese characters render as blank boxes (tofu) or throw
"Missing character" errors. Do this BEFORE any translation.

Add right before `\begin{document}` in the main `.tex`:

```latex
\usepackage{ifxetex}
\ifxetex
  \usepackage{xeCJK}
  \IfFileExists{/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf}{
    \setCJKmainfont[
      AutoFakeBold=false,
      AutoFakeSlant=false,
      BoldFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      ItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      BoldItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      SmallCapsFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      Path=/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/,
      Extension=.otf,
    ]{SourceHanSerifSC-Regular}
    \setCJKsansfont[
      AutoFakeBold=false,
      AutoFakeSlant=false,
      BoldFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      ItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      BoldItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      SmallCapsFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      Path=/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/,
      Extension=.otf,
    ]{SourceHanSerifSC-Regular}
    \setCJKmonofont[
      AutoFakeBold=false,
      AutoFakeSlant=false,
      BoldFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      ItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      BoldItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      SmallCapsFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      Path=/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/,
      Extension=.otf,
    ]{SourceHanSerifSC-Regular}
  }{
    \setCJKmainfont{Source Han Serif SC}
    \setCJKsansfont{Source Han Serif SC}
    \setCJKmonofont{Source Han Serif SC}
  }
\fi
```

### Font choice (biggest source of errors for weak agents)

**Use 思源宋体 (Source Han Serif SC)** as the paper body font — it's the
project default and gives the expected serif/宋体 aesthetic for academic
papers. Source Han is open-source, ships with consistent weights, and is
widely available (macOS via Font Book install, Linux via
`fonts-noto-cjk`).

Font discovery is flaky across TeX Live versions and macOS. Many docs
recommend "Songti SC" — on real systems, xelatex often cannot find it
even though `fc-list | grep Songti` shows it. This is because TeX's
kpathsea and fontconfig don't always agree about `/System/Library/Fonts/
Supplemental/`.

**Reliable (tested 2026-04):**
- Local OTF path:
  `/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf`
  — most deterministic choice on this machine.
- `Source Han Serif SC` — the preferred family-name fallback once `fc-list`
  can see the font.
- `Noto Serif CJK SC` — same glyph family under the Google/Noto alias;
  acceptable fallback if the Adobe naming is unavailable.

**Often fails:**
- `Songti SC` — `fontspec Error: The font "Songti SC" cannot be found.`
- macOS family-name lookup without a local path — may silently resolve to the
  wrong face or a fallback font.
- `Hiragino Mincho ProN` — Japanese font, works but wrong aesthetic.
- `PingFang SC` — sans-serif; wrong aesthetic for paper body. Acceptable
  only as a last-resort fallback if Source Han/Noto are both unavailable.

**Sanity test before patching the paper** (5 seconds — save 10 minutes of
debugging the big doc):

```bash
cat > /tmp/ftest.tex <<'EOF'
\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{ifxetex}
\ifxetex
  \usepackage{xeCJK}
  \IfFileExists{/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf}{
    \setCJKmainfont[
      AutoFakeBold=false,
      AutoFakeSlant=false,
      BoldFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      ItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      BoldItalicFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      SmallCapsFont={SourceHanSerifSC-Regular},
      Path=/Users/junix/.fonts/SourceHanSerif/static/,
      Extension=.otf,
    ]{SourceHanSerifSC-Regular}
  }{
    \setCJKmainfont{Source Han Serif SC}
  }
\fi
\begin{document}
你好 Hello
\end{document}
EOF
cd /tmp && rm -f ftest.aux ftest.log ftest.pdf \
  && xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode ftest.tex 2>&1 | tail -5
```

If "Output written on ftest.pdf" appears with no `^!` errors — font works.
If `fontspec Error: The font "X" cannot be found` — try the next font.

NOTE: the Write tool may not produce valid heredoc via echo. Use the
Write tool directly to create `/tmp/ftest.tex`, then run `xelatex`.

### pdfTeX-only packages (wrap in `\ifPDFTeX…\fi`)

Some papers preload packages that only work under pdfLaTeX. Under xelatex
they may fail silently (e.g. broken fonts) or loudly. If baseline was
clean these are fine; only touch if you see related errors.

Packages to guard if they appear AND cause errors:
- `\RequirePackage{XCharter}` — loads its own `.fontspec` that fights xeCJK
- `\RequirePackage[T1]{fontenc}`, `\RequirePackage{cmap}`,
  `\RequirePackage[utf8]{inputenc}` — ignored under xelatex but noisy
- `\RequirePackage[tracking=smallcaps]{microtype}` — `tracking` is pdfTeX-only
- `\DisableLigatures`, `\pdfmapfile`, `\pdfmapline`

Wrap pattern (inside the class file or preamble):

```latex
\RequirePackage{iftex}
\ifPDFTeX
  \RequirePackage[T1]{fontenc}
  \RequirePackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\fi
```

### Verify: recompile after xeCJK addition

```bash
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex 2>&1 | grep -E "^!|^l\."
```

No `^!` lines = new baseline. You are ready to translate.

---

## Step 3.5 — Localize Template-Hardcoded English Labels

Conference and journal class files (`.cls`/`.sty`) ship visible English
strings that the body never re-emits — `Figure`, `Table`, `Abstract`,
`References`, `Equal contribution`, `Correspondence to:`, `Under review`,
`Preprint`, `Proceedings of`. Translate every paragraph and the PDF still
prints "Figure 1" instead of "图 1". Audit before translation begins:

```bash
grep -rnE \
  '(\\figurename|\\tablename|\\refname|\\contentsname|\\bibname|\\appendixname|\\abstractname|\\keywordsname|\\listfigurename|\\listtablename|Equal contribution|Correspondence to|Under review|Preprint|Proceedings of)' \
  . --include='*.sty' --include='*.cls' --include='*.tex'
```

Override in the preamble (after the xeCJK block) for the standard names:

```latex
\renewcommand{\figurename}{图}
\renewcommand{\tablename}{表}
\renewcommand{\refname}{参考文献}      % article class
\renewcommand{\bibname}{参考文献}      % book/report class
\renewcommand{\contentsname}{目录}
\renewcommand{\appendixname}{附录}
\renewcommand{\abstractname}{摘要}
```

For template-specific commands (e.g. `\icmlEqualContribution`,
`\icmlCorrespondingAuthor`), prefer `\renewcommand` first; if no
overridable command exists, edit the local copy of the `.sty`/`.cls` in
the working directory directly. Do not modify a system-installed class
file.

Recompile after this step. The PDF should now show Chinese figure/table
prefixes and section headings without any prose changes yet.

## Step 4 — Build a Terminology Table (read first, translate second)

Before writing any translation, read the abstract + first page and collect
domain terms. Without this, every section translation drifts — the same
English word becomes 3 different Chinese words across the paper.

**Rules for the table:**
1. First occurrence in a section: Chinese term + English in parentheses.
   Example: "大语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）". Then just "LLM".
2. Proper nouns (model/dataset/method names): keep English, never translate.
   Examples: `Llama-3.1`, `Qwen2.5`, `HotpotQA`, `KV Packet`, `CacheBlend`.
3. Abbreviations (FLOPs, TTFT, RoPE, RAG, KV): keep as-is; introduce once
   with the full form.
4. Technical terms: agreed Chinese word, same across sections.

**Example table from KV Packet paper (reuse pattern for other ML papers):**

| English | Chinese |
|---------|---------|
| Large Language Model (LLM) | 大语言模型 |
| Key-Value (KV) cache | 键值（KV）缓存 |
| Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) | 检索增强生成（RAG） |
| Time-to-First-Token (TTFT) | 首 token 生成延迟（TTFT） |
| prefill / decoding | 预填充 / 解码 |
| recomputation | 重新计算 |
| context-dependent / -independent | 上下文相关的 / 上下文无关的 |
| soft-token adapter | 软 token 适配器 |
| self-supervised distillation | 自监督蒸馏 |
| attention sink | 注意力汇聚（点） |
| boundary artifact | 边界伪影 |
| Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE) | 旋转位置编码（RoPE） |
| chunk / document block | 文档块 |
| fine-tuning / prompt tuning | 微调 / 提示调优 |
| plug-and-play | 即插即用 |

Keep the table in a note and apply consistently.

### Quick decision rule (use when the table below doesn't cover a case)

If the content is **machine-executable** (code, formulas, command outputs,
identifiers, file paths, URLs), **do not translate**. Only translate
narrative content meant for human readers (prose, headings, captions,
bullet items). Use this when in doubt about `\verb|…|`, `\lstlisting`,
`\minted`, error dumps, JSON literals, or anything that looks "evidential"
rather than "narrative".

### General translation rules (apply to every section)

| Category | Rule |
|----------|------|
| LaTeX commands | Never translate: `\cite`, `\citep`, `\ref`, `\label`, `\includegraphics`, `\textbf`, `\emph`, `\textit`, `\eqref`, environment names |
| Math | Never translate: `$…$`, `$$…$$`, `\begin{equation}…\end{equation}`, `\begin{align}…\end{align}` etc. |
| Labels | `\label{sec:foo}`, `\label{fig:bar}`, `\label{eq:baz}` stay UNCHANGED. Translating a label breaks every `\ref` to it. |
| Section headings | `\section{Introduction}` → `\section{引言}` is fine. But keep `\label{sec:introduction}` exactly as is. |
| Figure/table captions | Translate the *prose* inside `\caption{…}`. Keep subfig labels like `(a)`, `(b)` intact. |
| Proper nouns | Model/dataset/method/institution names: English. Authors: English. |
| Numbers + units | Keep Arabic numerals and English units: `8B`, `80~GB`, `$5\times 10^{-4}$` |
| Table content | Translate header row and first-column labels. Keep method/dataset/metric names (e.g. `No Recompute`, `Biography`, `F1`) as English. |
| Punctuation | In prose: Chinese punctuation `。，；：""『』（）`. Inside LaTeX args: English punctuation. `Fig.~\ref{...}` → `图~\ref{...}` (keep the `~`). |
| Abbreviations | First use: full Chinese + English abbr in parentheses. After: just the abbr. |
| Tilde `~` | Non-breaking space in LaTeX. Keep it: `Section~\ref{...}` → `第~\ref{...} 节`. |
| Quotes | ``text'' renders as smart quotes in LaTeX. When translating keep the same structure if the author used them for emphasis; or replace with `「…」`. Don't just type `"…"` — pdfLaTeX/xelatex treat it differently. |
| Bibliography | `.bib` entries: do NOT translate. Run `bibtex` as normal. |
| Comments | Lines starting with `%` in source: leave alone or translate in place (they don't render). |
| Author block | Don't translate. Names, affiliations, emails stay English. |

---

## Step 5 — Translate Piece by Piece (the core loop)

**Piece = one section.** Split by `\section{…}`. Inside each section there
may be `\subsection`, figures, tables, equations — translate around them,
never through them.

### For each section:

**5a. Read the section.** Use the Read tool to see its exact content.
Count the lines so you know the range for Edit.

**5b. Translate in your head** (you are the translator — don't invoke
another agent). Apply the terminology table + general rules.

**5c. Edit in place.** Use the Edit tool. Prefer **one Edit per `\section`
block** (or per paragraph if the section is huge). Replace ONLY the English
prose; leave math/LaTeX/citations byte-identical. The `old_string` must
match the source exactly (whitespace, punctuation).

**5d. Compile.**

```bash
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex 2>&1 | grep -E "^!|^l\."
```

- No `^!` lines → move to next section.
- Any `^!` line → go to 5e, fix, recompile, only then move on.

**5e. Fix errors.** See the error table below.

### Worked example — abstract

Original:

```latex
\begin{abstract}
Large Language Models (LLMs) rely heavily on Key-Value (KV) caching to
minimize inference latency. However, standard KV caches are
context-dependent: reusing a cached document ...
\end{abstract}
```

Translated (one Edit replaces the inside of `\begin{abstract}…\end{abstract}`):

```latex
\begin{abstract}
大语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）严重依赖键值（Key-Value, KV）缓存
以最小化推理延迟。然而，标准的 KV 缓存是上下文相关的：在新的上下文中复用
已缓存的文档时，需要重新计算 KV 状态以应对注意力分布的变化。...
\end{abstract}
```

Note: `\begin{abstract}` and `\end{abstract}` are OUTSIDE the Edit's
`old_string`/`new_string`. Only the prose inside changes.

### Worked example — section with math and figure

Original:

```latex
\section{Methods}
\label{sec:methods}

When concatenating precomputed KV caches with RoPE realignment, ...
the packet representation for a single document is:

\begin{equation}\label{eq:packet_const}
\mathcal{P}(D;\phi) = [\mathbf{h}_1, \dots, \mathbf{h}_{N_h}, \mathbf{e}_1, \dots, \mathbf{e}_L, \mathbf{t}_1, \dots, \mathbf{t}_{N_t}]
\end{equation}

At inference time, each document is independently wrapped ...
```

Translated (math block UNCHANGED):

```latex
\section{方法}
\label{sec:methods}

在经过 RoPE 重新对齐的前提下拼接预计算的 KV 缓存时，...
单篇文档的数据包表示为：

\begin{equation}\label{eq:packet_const}
\mathcal{P}(D;\phi) = [\mathbf{h}_1, \dots, \mathbf{h}_{N_h}, \mathbf{e}_1, \dots, \mathbf{e}_L, \mathbf{t}_1, \dots, \mathbf{t}_{N_t}]
\end{equation}

在推理时，每篇文档按式~\eqref{eq:packet_const} 独立地包装成一个数据包，...
```

Observe: `\label{sec:methods}`, `\label{eq:packet_const}`, the equation
body, and `\eqref{eq:packet_const}` all stay exactly the same. Only prose
changes.

### Order to translate the pieces

1. `\begin{abstract}…\end{abstract}` (small, good warm-up)
2. Each `\section{…}` in order, top to bottom.

After each Edit, compile. You should have the same number of compiles as
sections + 1 (the final check).

---

## Step 6 — Error Table (translation-induced LaTeX errors)

These are the errors you'll actually see. Learn them.

| Compile error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `Package fontspec Error: The font "X" cannot be found` | xelatex can't find the CJK font | Try local `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf` path first, then `Source Han Serif SC`, then `Noto Serif CJK SC`; re-run sanity test from Step 3 |
| `Missing character: There is no 中 (U+4E2D) in font …` | xeCJK not loaded yet | Add the xeCJK block (Step 3). If already added, the block may be AFTER a package that set a non-CJK default — move it to the end of the preamble. |
| `! There's no line here to end` | Stray `\\` at start of a paragraph you translated | Remove the extra `\\` |
| `! Extra }, or forgotten \endgroup` | Brace mismatch — usually you deleted a `}` when translating | Re-read your Edit, count braces |
| `! Missing $ inserted` | You removed a `$` around inline math | Restore `$…$` |
| `! Undefined control sequence \xxx` | You translated a macro name by accident (e.g. `\ref` → `\参考`) | Restore the original macro |
| `LaTeX Warning: Reference `sec:foo' undefined` | You changed a `\label` or `\ref` target | Restore. Labels stay English. |
| `LaTeX Warning: Citation `bar' undefined` | Bibliography not built yet (benign on first run) or you changed a `\cite` key | If benign, run `bibtex <main>` then `xelatex` twice. Else restore the key. |
| `! File ended while scanning use of \…` | Corrupted `.aux` / partial edit left unbalanced env | `rm *.aux` and recompile; if persists, read the log for line number and check the env around it |
| Output looks OK but ??? appears where refs/cites should be | Not enough xelatex passes | Run xelatex → bibtex → xelatex → xelatex |
| `Package fontspec Warning: Font "X" does not contain requested Script "CJK"` | A requested CJK face lacks the metadata xelatex wants | Warning only, ignore, or point sans/mono to the same 思源宋体 regular face |

### Locate errors fast

The compile log prints `l.<N>` for each error. Read the main file around
that line:

```bash
# print lines N-3 to N+3 around the error
sed -n '120,128p' <main>.tex
```

Fix in-place with Edit, recompile.

---

## Step 7 — Verification (run all four checks, fix, recompile)

After all sections are translated, run this verification block. Each check
catches a different silent failure mode that the per-section compile loop
does **not** catch on its own.

### 7.1 Missed-English prose scan

```bash
LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 perl -CSDL -nle \
  'print "$.: $_" if /[a-zA-Z]{4,}/ && !/\p{Han}/ && !/^\s*\\/ && !/^\s*%/ && !/^\s*$/' \
  <main>.tex | head -40
```

Important flags (without these the scan reports false positives):
- `LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8` — ensures UTF-8 locale.
- `-CSDL` — perl reads/writes UTF-8.
- `\p{Han}` — matches any Chinese character.

**Allowed to remain English**: author block (names, universities, emails);
table cells with method/dataset/metric names (`No Recompute`, `HotpotQA`,
`F1`); inline model/dataset names (`Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct`, `Qwen-3-4B`);
bibliography entries; figure legends that match what the figure literally
contains.

**Must be translated if still English**: prose paragraphs; section /
subsection titles; figure/table captions (prose part); items in
`\begin{itemize}` / `\begin{enumerate}`; `\textbf{…}` labels that
introduce concepts.

### 7.2 CJK catcode scan (silent failure: `\macro<CJK>` parses as one undefined command)

xeCJK sets every CJK character to catcode 11 (letter), so `\xmax概率`
parses as one undefined control sequence `\xmax概率`, **not** `\xmax` +
`概率`. The compile may still succeed (xelatex turns the undefined token
into a warning under `nonstopmode`), but the macro silently swallows the
adjacent Chinese text in the PDF.

```bash
grep -rnE '\\[a-zA-Z]+[一-龥]' . --include='*.tex'
```

Each match needs `{}` between the macro and the CJK characters:
`\xmax概率` → `\xmax{}概率`. The `{}` is an empty group that terminates
the control sequence without changing layout.

### 7.3 LaTeX command-spelling diff (catches typos introduced during Edit)

Editing inside `\caption{…}`, theorem statements, or footnotes can
mis-spell a control sequence (e.g. `\footnotetext` → `\footnotext`).
xelatex may not flag the typo if the misspelled name happens to be valid
in some other package or is hidden inside a group. Diff the set of
control sequences against the pre-translation baseline:

```bash
# Before translating: snapshot the original command set.
grep -ohrIE '\\[a-zA-Z]+' . --include='*.tex' | sort -u > /tmp/cmds.before
```

```bash
# After translating: compare and inspect right-side-only entries.
grep -ohrIE '\\[a-zA-Z]+' . --include='*.tex' | sort -u > /tmp/cmds.after
diff /tmp/cmds.before /tmp/cmds.after | grep '^>'
```

Right-side-only commands are suspicious. Each one is either a typo (fix
it) or an intentional addition (e.g. `\figurename`, `\setCJKmainfont`,
`\renewcommand` from Step 3.5 — leave alone). If you didn't snapshot
beforehand and the directory is under git, `git diff -- '*.tex' | grep -E
'^[-+]\\\\[a-zA-Z]+'` is a workable substitute.

### 7.4 Image-language reminder (figures with embedded English text)

Figures rendered as PDF/PNG (axis labels, legends, framework names) keep
their source-language text after translation. The translator cannot fix
these unless figure source files (`.svg`, `.tex`, `.py`, `.tikz`) are
available. List every embedded image so the user can spot-check:

```bash
grep -rohE '\\includegraphics(\[[^]]*\])?\{[^}]+\}' . --include='*.tex' \
  | sed 's/.*{\([^}]*\)}/\1/' | sort -u
```

In the final report, note that any of these may still contain visible
English needing separate image-level localization (out of scope for this
skill).

### Fix loop

For each miss in 7.1, each match in 7.2, and each suspicious command in
7.3: Edit → recompile (`xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex 2>&1
| grep -E "^!|^l\."`). Item 7.4 is informational — record it in the
final report, do not auto-edit images.

---

## Step 8 — Final Verification

```bash
cd "<paper-dir>"
# NEVER delete *.bbl — arXiv-style papers ship a pre-built .bbl with no .bib
# source; removing it strands every \cite. bibtex/biber will overwrite the
# .bbl on its own when it actually runs.
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out *.blg *.bcf *.run.xml *.toc
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex > /dev/null 2>&1   # pass 1: writes .aux with \citation entries
bibtex <main>           > /dev/null 2>&1                        # if bibliography exists — builds .bbl from .bib
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex > /dev/null 2>&1   # pass 2: pulls in .bbl, numbers citations
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode <main>.tex 2>&1 | tail -6     # pass 3: resolves \ref/\pageref to final numbers
```

If the paper has a `.bbl` but no `.bib` (typical for arXiv submissions),
skip the `bibtex <main>` step — running it with no database errors out
and clobbers the shipped `.bbl`. The dispatchers
(`translate-next-paper`, `translate-next-paper-random`) auto-detect this
and fall back to the shipped `.bbl`.

### Why three xelatex passes when a bibliography exists

LaTeX is a two-pass system (it writes `.aux` on one run and reads it on
the next). Bibliographies add a third pass:

| Run | What happens | What's still wrong after |
|-----|--------------|--------------------------|
| xelatex (pass 1) | Writes `\citation{key}` into `.aux`. Cites show as `[?]`. `\ref` shows `??`. | No bibliography, no numbers |
| bibtex | Reads `.aux`, looks up keys in `.bib`, produces `.bbl` | — |
| xelatex (pass 2) | Pulls `.bbl` into the document, numbers the citations, updates `.aux` with new label positions | `\ref`/`\pageref` may still be off because label numbers just changed |
| xelatex (pass 3) | Reads the updated `.aux`; all `\ref`/`\pageref`/`\cite` now resolve | Done |

- **No `\bibliography{}` / `\printbibliography` at all?** Two xelatex runs
  are enough (pass 1 writes `.aux`, pass 2 resolves `\ref`).
- **Has a bibliography?** Always run the full `xelatex → bibtex → xelatex
  → xelatex` cycle. Skipping the third pass leaves `??` where numbered
  cross-references should be, even though citations themselves look fine.
- If the final PDF shows any `??` or `[?]`, you didn't run enough passes
  — just run xelatex once more.

The last line should say `Output written on <main>.pdf (N pages)` with no
`! ` errors above it. Warnings like "Some font shapes were not available,
defaults substituted" are cosmetic.

### Open the PDF and visually check

- Section headings display in Chinese.
- Body text is Chinese (no tofu boxes ▯).
- Math and citations render (numbers, not `??`).
- Figures and tables are present, captions in Chinese.
- No broken lines or overfull boxes in obvious places (minor warnings are
  expected for CJK + Latin mixed text).

---

## Worked full-paper recipe (condensed)

Ran on `2604-KV Packet.../zh/` on 2026-04-17, 239-line source, produced
10-page Chinese PDF with zero compile errors:

```bash
cd "<paper-dir>"
# 1. Find main
grep -l '\\begin{document}' *.tex          # → neurips_2026.tex

# 2. Baseline
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode neurips_2026.tex 2>&1 | grep "^!"   # clean

# 3. Flatten not needed (main has no \input) — skip.

# 4. Add xeCJK block before \title{}, then test
#    (use Edit tool, not echo/heredoc — LaTeX content needs exact bytes)
rm -f *.aux && xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode neurips_2026.tex 2>&1 | grep "^!"   # clean

# 5. Build terminology table in notes (see Step 4).

# 6. Translate piece by piece (compile after EACH):
#    - abstract                    → compile → clean
#    - \section{Introduction}      → compile → clean
#    - \section{Background...}     → compile → clean
#    - \section{Methods}           → compile → clean
#    - \section{Experiments}       → compile → clean
#    - \section{Conclusion}        → compile → clean

# 7. Scan for missed English (see Step 7) — only author block + table
#    cells with method names remained, which is correct.

# 8. Final pass with bibliography
# NEVER delete *.bbl — arXiv-style papers ship a pre-built .bbl with no .bib.
rm -f *.aux *.log *.out *.blg *.bcf *.run.xml *.toc
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode neurips_2026.tex > /dev/null 2>&1
bibtex neurips_2026
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode neurips_2026.tex > /dev/null 2>&1
xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode neurips_2026.tex 2>&1 | tail -3
# → "Output written on neurips_2026.pdf (10 pages)."
```

---

## Markdown Workflow (skipping LaTeX parts)

Markdown papers are simpler: no `\input`, no compile step. The translation
principle is the same — piece by piece via Read + Edit.

1. Read the `.md` file; split by H1/H2 headings.
2. For each section, translate the prose. **Do not touch**:
   - Code fences ```` ``` ```` and their contents
   - Inline code `` ` ``
   - Link URLs: `[text](url)` → translate `text`, leave `url`
   - Math blocks (`$…$`, `$$…$$`)
   - Image refs `![alt](path)`, HTML tags
3. Edit in place.
4. Scan for remaining English:
   ```bash
   LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 perl -CSDL -nle \
     'print "$.: $_" if /[a-zA-Z]{4,}/ && !/\p{Han}/ && !/^\s*[#\-\*\[]/' \
     paper.md | head -40
   ```
5. Manual review — there is no compiler. Open the rendered preview (VS
   Code, Typora, etc.), spot-check structure, links, and code fences.

---

## Tips and Common Mistakes

- **Never translate before baseline-compiling the original.** If it doesn't
  compile in English, your Chinese version won't either, and you'll waste
  time blaming your edits.
- **Never batch 5 sections into one Edit.** Compile after each. The error
  location tells you which section to debug; that signal is gone if you
  stacked changes.
- **Never skip the font sanity test.** Local-path Source Han setup vs.
  family-name fallback costs more debugging time than any other issue.
- **Never translate `\label{...}`.** It's the ID the `\ref` side points
  at. Translating it turns every cross-reference into `??`.
- **Never translate inside `$…$` or `\begin{equation}…\end{equation}`.**
  Math variables are not English words.
- **Never translate bib keys.** `\citep{llama3}` stays `\citep{llama3}`.
- **Always keep `~` tildes.** They are non-breaking spaces. `Section~\ref`
  → `第~\ref{...}~节` is fine.
- **Table columns that appear in both the text and in figure legends**:
  keep English (e.g. `No Recompute`, `Full Recompute`) so text and figure
  stay in sync.
- **Author names**: do not translate, do not add Chinese. Keep the
  original name block intact.
- **The first compile after adding xeCJK may take longer** than the
  baseline — font loading. Don't mistake slowness for hang.
- **Multi-language quotes**: in Chinese prose use `「」` or `""`; do not
  use straight `"`. Inside LaTeX arguments keep English punctuation.
- **If a compile produces 100 errors**, don't panic — usually they
  cascade from a single brace mismatch near the top. Find the FIRST
  `^!` and fix that; recompile; most will vanish.
- **Keep the .orig backup.** `cp main.tex main.tex.orig` before you start
  is a one-line safety net; you can diff against it to verify you only
  changed prose.

---

## Known Limitations

| Limitation | Impact | Workaround |
|------------|--------|------------|
| macOS system fonts not always visible to xelatex | Family name resolves poorly or font cannot be found | Prefer local `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf` path, then `Source Han Serif SC`, then `Noto Serif CJK SC` |
| Chinese text looks too bold after adding xeCJK | Wrong weight selected for CJK bold shape | Keep `AutoFakeBold=false` and map `BoldFont` to `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular` |
| Pre-split section files may not be referenced by main | You translate them, nothing changes in PDF | Always check `grep \\input main.tex` — translate where main actually reads |
| `inputenc` warns under xelatex | Noisy log | Harmless; ignore or guard with `\ifPDFTeX` |
| Perl `\p{Han}` scan needs UTF-8 locale | False positives on Chinese lines | Use `LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 perl -CSDL` |
| CJK italic fonts are usually missing | Warning "font shape undefined" | Cosmetic; xeCJK falls back |

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/paper-translate` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/paper-translate/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/paper-translate/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/paper-translate/CLAUDE.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/translate-paper/SKILL.md` |

name: triage-issue
description: Use when the user reports a bug, says "triage this", asks to investigate a problem and file it, or wants the agent to find root cause and write up a GitHub issue with a TDD fix plan — mostly autonomous, minimal questions back to the user.
body:
# Triage Issue

Investigate a reported problem, find its root cause, and create a GitHub issue with a TDD fix plan. This is a mostly hands-off workflow - minimize questions to the user.

## Process

### 1. Capture the problem

Get a brief description of the issue from the user. If they haven't provided one, ask ONE question: "What's the problem you're seeing?"

Do NOT ask follow-up questions yet. Start investigating immediately.

### 2. Explore and diagnose

Use the Agent tool with subagent_type=Explore to deeply investigate the codebase. Your goal is to find:

- **Where** the bug manifests (entry points, UI, API responses)
- **What** code path is involved (trace the flow)
- **Why** it fails (the root cause, not just the symptom)
- **What** related code exists (similar patterns, tests, adjacent modules)

Look at:
- Related source files and their dependencies
- Existing tests (what's tested, what's missing)
- Recent changes to affected files (`git log` on relevant files)
- Error handling in the code path
- Similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase that work correctly

### 3. Identify the fix approach

Based on your investigation, determine:

- The minimal change needed to fix the root cause
- Which modules/interfaces are affected
- What behaviors need to be verified via tests
- Whether this is a regression, missing feature, or design flaw

### 4. Design TDD fix plan

Create a concrete, ordered list of RED-GREEN cycles. Each cycle is one vertical slice:

- **RED**: Describe a specific test that captures the broken/missing behavior
- **GREEN**: Describe the minimal code change to make that test pass

Rules:
- Tests verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details
- One test at a time, vertical slices (NOT all tests first, then all code)
- Each test should survive internal refactors
- Include a final refactor step if needed
- **Durability**: Only suggest fixes that would survive radical codebase changes. Describe behaviors and contracts, not internal structure. Tests assert on observable outcomes (API responses, UI state, user-visible effects), not internal state. A good suggestion reads like a spec; a bad one reads like a diff.

### 5. Create the GitHub issue

Create a GitHub issue using `gh issue create` with the template below. Do NOT ask the user to review before creating - just create it and share the URL.

<issue-template>

## Problem

A clear description of the bug or issue, including:
- What happens (actual behavior)
- What should happen (expected behavior)
- How to reproduce (if applicable)

## Root Cause Analysis

Describe what you found during investigation:
- The code path involved
- Why the current code fails
- Any contributing factors

Do NOT include specific file paths, line numbers, or implementation details that couple to current code layout. Describe modules, behaviors, and contracts instead. The issue should remain useful even after major refactors.

## TDD Fix Plan

A numbered list of RED-GREEN cycles:

1. **RED**: Write a test that [describes expected behavior]
   **GREEN**: [Minimal change to make it pass]

2. **RED**: Write a test that [describes next behavior]
   **GREEN**: [Minimal change to make it pass]

...

**REFACTOR**: [Any cleanup needed after all tests pass]

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
- [ ] All new tests pass
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

</issue-template>

After creating the issue, print the issue URL and a one-line summary of the root cause.

name: ubiquitous-language
body:
# Ubiquitous Language

Extract and formalize domain terminology from the current conversation into a consistent glossary, saved to a local file.

## Process

1. **Scan the conversation** for domain-relevant nouns, verbs, and concepts
2. **Identify problems**:
   - Same word used for different concepts (ambiguity)
   - Different words used for the same concept (synonyms)
   - Vague or overloaded terms
3. **Propose a canonical glossary** with opinionated term choices
4. **Write to `UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md`** in the working directory using the format below
5. **Output a summary** inline in the conversation

## Output Format

Write a `UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md` file with this structure:

```md
# Ubiquitous Language

## Order lifecycle

| Term        | Definition                                              | Aliases to avoid      |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| **Order**   | A customer's request to purchase one or more items      | Purchase, transaction |
| **Invoice** | A request for payment sent to a customer after delivery | Bill, payment request |

## People

| Term         | Definition                                  | Aliases to avoid       |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------- |
| **Customer** | A person or organization that places orders | Client, buyer, account |
| **User**     | An authentication identity in the system    | Login, account         |

## Relationships

- An **Invoice** belongs to exactly one **Customer**
- An **Order** produces one or more **Invoices**

## Example dialogue

> **Dev:** "When a **Customer** places an **Order**, do we create the **Invoice** immediately?"
> **Domain expert:** "No — an **Invoice** is only generated once a **Fulfillment** is confirmed. A single **Order** can produce multiple **Invoices** if items ship in separate **Shipments**."
> **Dev:** "So if a **Shipment** is cancelled before dispatch, no **Invoice** exists for it?"
> **Domain expert:** "Exactly. The **Invoice** lifecycle is tied to the **Fulfillment**, not the **Order**."

## Flagged ambiguities

- "account" was used to mean both **Customer** and **User** — these are distinct concepts: a **Customer** places orders, while a **User** is an authentication identity that may or may not represent a **Customer**.
```

## Rules

- **Be opinionated.** When multiple words exist for the same concept, pick the best one and list the others as aliases to avoid.
- **Flag conflicts explicitly.** If a term is used ambiguously in the conversation, call it out in the "Flagged ambiguities" section with a clear recommendation.
- **Only include terms relevant for domain experts.** Skip the names of modules or classes unless they have meaning in the domain language.
- **Keep definitions tight.** One sentence max. Define what it IS, not what it does.
- **Show relationships.** Use bold term names and express cardinality where obvious.
- **Only include domain terms.** Skip generic programming concepts (array, function, endpoint) unless they have domain-specific meaning.
- **Group terms into multiple tables** when natural clusters emerge (e.g. by subdomain, lifecycle, or actor). Each group gets its own heading and table. If all terms belong to a single cohesive domain, one table is fine — don't force groupings.
- **Write an example dialogue.** A short conversation (3-5 exchanges) between a dev and a domain expert that demonstrates how the terms interact naturally. The dialogue should clarify boundaries between related concepts and show terms being used precisely.

<example>

## Example dialogue

> **Dev:** "How do I test the **sync service** without Docker?"

> **Domain expert:** "Provide the **filesystem layer** instead of the **Docker layer**. It implements the same **Sandbox service** interface but uses a local directory as the **sandbox**."

> **Dev:** "So **sync-in** still creates a **bundle** and unpacks it?"

> **Domain expert:** "Exactly. The **sync service** doesn't know which layer it's talking to. It calls `exec` and `copyIn` — the **filesystem layer** just runs those as local shell commands."

</example>

## Re-running

When invoked again in the same conversation:

1. Read the existing `UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md`
2. Incorporate any new terms from subsequent discussion
3. Update definitions if understanding has evolved
4. Re-flag any new ambiguities
5. Rewrite the example dialogue to incorporate new terms

name: upgrade-rust-crates
body:
# upgrade-rust-crates

Batch-refresh `github.com/junix/*` git-dependency pins and rebuild every standalone
crate under `~/projects/crates`. Each crate is an **independent git repo** with its
own `Cargo.toml` + `Cargo.lock` (there is **no** root workspace `Cargo.toml`).

## Why this works (read before running)

- `cargo update -p <pkg>` re-pins a git dep to the **latest commit on its GitHub
  remote** — so an upstream bugfix must already be *pushed*. The local working copy
  of the upstream is irrelevant.
- Downstream crates ignore an upstream's lockfile; only each crate's own
  `Cargo.lock` matters. So crates can be upgraded **independently**.
- We still process in **topological order** (pure-upstream first) so that when we
  commit+push a lock bump, downstream crates processed later re-pin to the final
  commit in a single pass. `plan.py` computes this order.
- A dep's **package name** (`cargo update -p stream_md`) differs from its **repo
  name** (`github.com/junix/stream-md-rs`). The scripts handle both.

## Per-crate steps (scripts/upgrade-one.sh)

1. `cargo update -p <pkg>` for every junix git dep → may change `Cargo.lock`.
2. `cargo build --release` (verify compile; produce CLI binaries) using a **shared**
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=~/projects/models/target`.
3. **CLI crates** (cargo metadata reports `bin` targets): install each binary to
   `~/sync/bin_<arch>/` (with `xattr -c` + `codesign -f -s -`), run `cargo test
   --release`, then smoke-test each installed binary with `--help`.
   **Lib crates**: compile-verify only — no install, no tests.
4. If `Cargo.lock` changed **and** the build succeeded: commit **only** `Cargo.lock`
   and `git push`. A failed build leaves the lock modified but uncommitted.

## Quick start

```bash
S=~/projects/skills/upgrade-rust-crates/scripts

# Full pass over all crates in dependency order (multi-hour — run in background):
bash "$S/upgrade-all.sh"

# Fast incremental sweep — only crates that actually have junix git deps, and only
# rebuild/install when their Cargo.lock actually changed:
CHANGED_ONLY=1 bash "$S/upgrade-all.sh"

# Just a few crates (dir names), e.g. after an upstream fix to stream_md-rs:
bash "$S/upgrade-all.sh" stream_md-rs xchat-rs

# Resume an interrupted run (skips crates already done in the latest report dir):
RESUME=1 bash "$S/upgrade-all.sh"
```

A full pass builds 300+ crates sequentially. **Run it in the background** (Bash
`run_in_background: true`) and poll the report, or scope to `CHANGED_ONLY=1` /
explicit crate names. Recommend `CHANGED_ONLY=1` for routine maintenance and the
full pass only when a true full rebuild is wanted.

## How to drive it as the agent

1. Confirm scope with the user if unclear: full pass vs `CHANGED_ONLY=1` vs a named
   subset. Warn that a full pass is multi-hour and pushes commits.
2. Launch `upgrade-all.sh` in the background. It prints the report dir at startup:
   `~/projects/models/.upgrade-report/<timestamp>/`.
3. Monitor progress with `wc -l <report>/done.txt` and tail `<report>/results.jsonl`
   (do not spawn sub-agents to poll — use `wc -l`).
4. When done, read `<report>/summary.md`. Investigate each entry under **Build
   failures**, **Test failures**, **Smoke non-zero**, and **Push failures** using
   the matching `<report>/logs/<crate>.log`. Fix or report them — do not silently
   pass over failures.

## Flags (env vars)

| var | effect |
|-----|--------|
| `CHANGED_ONLY=1` | skip crates with no junix git deps; only build/test/install when the lock changed |
| `NO_PUSH=1` | commit `Cargo.lock` but don't push |
| `NO_COMMIT=1` | stage `Cargo.lock` but don't commit (implies no push) — for review-first runs |
| `RUN_LIB_TESTS=1` | also run `cargo test --release` for library crates |
| `RESUME=1` | continue the latest report dir, skipping completed crates |
| `CRATES_ROOT` | override `~/projects/crates` |
| `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` | override shared target (default `$CRATES_ROOT/target`) |

See [REFERENCE.md](REFERENCE.md) for output format, failure handling, and the
relationship to the per-crate `update-junix-git` justfile recipe.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/target` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/target/CLAUDE.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/upgrade-rust-crates/SKILL.md` |

name: use-asr
body:
# Use the `asr-rs` CLI (speech-to-text)

`asr-rs` is a local Rust ASR CLI + warm Unix-socket service. Engine default
**SenseVoice** (sherpa-onnx, fast, Mandarin-strong), with optional **MiMo**
cloud ASR (`mimo-v2.5-asr`, `MIMO_API_KEY`). Binary:
`~/sync/bin_arm64/asr-rs` (Apple Silicon) or `~/sync/bin_x86/asr-rs` (Intel).
Source: `~/projects/models/asr-rs/`.

Two ways to recognize: a **warm shared `serve`** over a Unix socket (one model
loaded for all clients) or a **one-shot `run`** per file (cold-loads the model
each call). Prefer serve.

> **Sibling — Python `asr-cli`** (`~/sync/bin/asr-cli`, `~/projects/models/asr-cli/`):
> not just legacy. It now has the **same `serve` / `config` / `launchd` contract**
> as `asr-rs` (same socket, same label) plus extra engines including ones
> `asr-rs` lacks (FunASR, Whisper-MLX, **Qwen3-ASR**). It also supports
> **MiMo ASR**. For plain SenseVoice on
> macOS, prefer `asr-rs` (no Python startup). Reach for `asr-cli` when you need a
> different engine. See the **`asr-cli`** section below. ⚠️ Both fight over the
> **same socket + launchd label** — run only one shared service at a time.

## Prerequisites

```bash
which asr-rs || (cd ~/projects/models/asr-rs && just install)
asr-rs download              # one-time: fetch SenseVoice into shared cache
```

Model cache (shared with Python stack):

```text
~/.cache/speech-onnx/sherpa-onnx-sense-voice-zh-en-ja-ko-yue-2024-07-17/
```

## Pick a mode

| Situation | Use |
|-----------|-----|
| Shared serve running (typical after `launchd install`) | **`asr-rs config` → socket → JSON request** (warm, ~80–100 ms on short clips) |
| One-off / no daemon | `asr-rs run -f <file.wav>` (or `--json`) |
| Pipe audio from another tool | `cat clip.wav \| asr-rs run -f -` |
| Need socket path / liveness | **`asr-rs config`** (JSON: `serve_socket`, `serve_status`) |
| Install the shared service | `asr-rs launchd install` (login autostart) |
| Validate only | `asr-rs run --dryrun -f clip.wav` |

## ⭐ Agent workflow: `config` (discover) + serve JSON (transcribe)

**Preferred** when the shared `serve` is up. Discover the socket from
`asr-rs config`, then send one NDJSON line. A **file path** is the simplest
request (no base64); the server reads & resamples it.

```bash
# 1) Discover socket + liveness (stdout is JSON by default)
asr-rs config
# → {"ok":true,"config":{"serve_socket":"/Users/…/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock",
#                        "serve_status":"running","serve_engine":"sensevoice", …}}

# 2) Transcribe over the socket (file path — server does the I/O)
python3 <<'PY'
import json, socket, subprocess
eff = json.loads(subprocess.check_output(["asr-rs", "config"]))["config"]
if eff["serve_status"] != "running":
    raise SystemExit("asr-rs serve not running — `asr-rs launchd start` or use one-shot run")
req = json.dumps({"path": "/absolute/path/audio.wav"}) + "\n"
s = socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
s.settimeout(30); s.connect(eff["serve_socket"])
s.sendall(req.encode())
print(s.makefile("rb").readline().decode())   # {"text":"…","raw":"…","engine":"…","ms":42}
PY
```

Quick `nc` probe / transcribe (newline-terminated, one line back):

```bash
SOCK=$(asr-rs config | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["config"]["serve_socket"])')
printf '{"ping":true}\n'              | nc -U "$SOCK"   # → {"ok":true,"engine":"sensevoice"}
printf '{"path":"/abs/audio.wav"}\n'  | nc -U "$SOCK"   # → {"text":"…","raw":"…","engine":"…","ms":…}
```

### Serve request / response (NDJSON, one line each)

| request | response |
|---------|----------|
| `{"ping":true}` | `{"ok":true,"engine":"…"}` (does **not** load the model) |
| `{"path":"/abs/file.wav"}` | `{"text":"…","raw":"…","engine":"…","ms":N}` |
| `{"pcm":"<base64>","format":"s16le","sample_rate":16000}` | same as above |
| invalid | `{"error":"…"}` |

- `pcm` = base64 of raw little-endian samples. `format`: `s16le` (int16) or
  `f32le`; `sample_rate` any (auto-resampled to 16 kHz). Mono.
- Persistent connection: multiple requests/responses per connect are allowed.
- Single engine: fixed when `serve` starts (config or flags); not per request.

## ⭐ `asr-rs config` — discover the socket & service state

```bash
asr-rs config            # stdout: {"ok":true,"config":{…}}  (JSON by default)
asr-rs config --plain    # human-readable + nc connect example
```

Fields under `.config`:

| field | meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `serve_socket` | absolute socket path (`--socket` > config `socket_path` > default `~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock`) |
| `serve_socket_exists` | whether the socket **file** is present (may be stale) |
| `serve_status` | **`running` \| `stopped`** — decided by a real `{"ping":true}` |
| `serve_engine` | engine the live service reports |
| `default_engine` / `language` / `fp32` / `threads` / `idle_unload_secs` | effective config |
| `config_file` / `config_file_exists` | `~/.asr-cli/config.json` |
| `launchd_label` | `com.junix.asr-cli` |

```bash
asr-rs config | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["config"]["serve_status"])'
```

Bad config → JSON `{"ok":false,"error":{…},"fix":…}` on stdout and **exit 3**.

## One-shot CLI (`run` subcommand)

```bash
asr-rs run -f recording.wav                    # transcript to stdout
asr-rs run --json -f recording.wav             # {"ok":true,"file":…,"text":…,"raw":…,"engine":…}
asr-rs run -l zh -f recording.wav              # language hint
asr-rs run --engine mimo -l zh -f recording.wav --json  # MiMo V2.5 ASR cloud
asr-rs run -f a.wav -f b.wav                     # multi-file: "<path>\t<text>" per line
cat clip.wav | asr-rs run -f -                 # stdin (`-` = read WAV/ffmpeg-decodable bytes)
ffmpeg -i talk.m4a -f wav - | asr-rs run -f -  # decode then transcribe
asr-rs run --dryrun -f clip.wav                # validate model paths, no recognizer load
```

- stdout = transcript(s) or JSON; stderr = logs, dryrun summary, `Error:` / `Fix:`.
- `-f, --file` selects input (`-` = stdin). Repeat for multiple files.
- `-e sensevoice|mimo`; SenseVoice accepts `-l auto|zh|en|ja|ko|yue`; MiMo accepts `-l auto|zh|en`.
- MiMo flags: `--api-key` (or `MIMO_API_KEY`), `--base-url`, `--mimo-model`.
- SenseVoice flags: `--fp32`; `--no-itn`; `--threads N`.
- Model utils: `asr-rs download [--force]`, `asr-rs model-path [--fp32]`.
- Compat aliases at top level: `--download-model`, `--model-path`.

### MiMo vs local speed reference

Recent local check on `/tmp/python-tts-cli-mimo.wav` (3.84s Mandarin WAV,
24 kHz, 184 KB), 7 `hyperfine` runs:

| mode | mean wall time | practical read |
|------|---------------:|----------------|
| SenseVoice warm socket | 93 ms | fastest for repeated local use |
| MiMo cloud ASR | 671 ms | faster than cold local here, but network variance |
| SenseVoice cold `asr-rs run` | 794 ms | model reload dominates one-shot CLI |

Accuracy on that clip favored MiMo for embedded English tokens: MiMo preserved
`Python TTS DLI` and `Mimo`; SenseVoice produced `pythonTTSDLI` and `密目`.

## Resident `serve` (warm model)

```bash
asr-rs serve --socket ~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock
asr-rs serve --socket … -l zh --idle-unload-secs 0   # never unload
```

- Loads the model once at startup (fail fast), reuses it per request.
- `--idle-unload-secs` (default 300; `0`=never): drops model after idle, reloads on next request.
- Bare `asr-rs serve` requires `--socket` unless `socket_path` is in `~/.asr-cli/config.json`.

## macOS LaunchAgent (login autostart)

```bash
cd ~/projects/models/asr-rs && just launchd-install
# or: just install && asr-rs launchd install

asr-rs launchd status
asr-rs launchd start
asr-rs launchd stop
asr-rs launchd uninstall
```

| item | value |
|------|-------|
| label | `com.junix.asr-cli` |
| plist | `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.junix.asr-cli.plist` |
| program | installed `asr-rs serve --socket ~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock` |
| logs | `~/.asr-cli/serve.out.log`, `serve.err.log` |

## Config file `~/.asr-cli/config.json`

```json
{
  "default_engine": "sensevoice",
  "language": "auto",
  "fp32": false,
  "threads": 2,
  "socket_path": "~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock",
  "idle_unload_secs": 300
}
```

Precedence: **CLI flag > config > built-in default**. Unknown keys fail loudly.

## Exit codes

| code | meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | success (incl. `--help`, bare `asr-rs`, `--dryrun`) |
| 1 | transcription / runtime error |
| 2 | clap usage / argument error |
| 3 | config or input validation error |

`--json` errors use envelope `{"ok":false,"error":{"code":"…","message":"…"},"fix":"…"}`.

## Gotchas

- **Warm vs cold**: `asr-rs run` reloads the model every call (seconds). For repeated use, prefer `serve` + socket.
- **Single engine on shared service**: change via config, then `asr-rs launchd start` — not per request.
- **Stale socket**: `serve_socket_exists:true` but `serve_status:stopped` → leftover socket file; restart serve.
- **16 kHz mono** target; inline `pcm` must be raw little-endian, no header.
- **Piping JSON to `head` is safe** (SIGPIPE handled).
- Dictation flow (mic → text): `dictabar listen` reuses the same socket.

## Python `asr-cli` — multi-engine sibling (funasr / whispermlx / qwen3)

Binary `~/sync/bin/asr-cli`; source `~/projects/models/asr-cli`; runs via `uv`.
Same shared-serve contract as `asr-rs` (socket `~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock`,
config `~/.asr-cli/config.json`, launchd label `com.junix.asr-cli`) — including
the **same `config` / `serve` / `launchd` subcommands and NDJSON socket protocol
documented above**. The differences worth knowing:

**Engines** (`-e`, default `sensevoice`):

| engine | install | notes |
|--------|---------|-------|
| `sensevoice` | base (`just build`) | sherpa-onnx, fast, Mandarin-strong (same model as `asr-rs`) |
| `funasr` | `uv sync --group funasr` | Paraformer (ModelScope); ~2.5× slower; English tech words sometimes better |
| `whispermlx` | `uv sync --group whispermlx` | MLX Whisper, **Apple Silicon only**; `--whisper-model mlx-community/whisper-*` |
| `qwen3` | `uv sync --group qwen3` | Qwen3-ASR-1.7B (~3.4GB), 30 langs + dialects, strong punctuation/书面化; `--qwen3-device auto/cuda:0/mps/cpu` |
| `mimo` | base | Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 ASR cloud; `MIMO_API_KEY`; `--base-url`, `--mimo-model`; languages `auto|zh|en` |

**CLI surface differs from `asr-rs`** — one-shot uses a **positional file**, not `run -f`:

```bash
asr-cli a.wav                          # one-shot (positional; NOT `run -f`)
asr-cli --json a.wav b.wav             # JSONL per file: {"file","text","raw","engine"}
asr-cli -e funasr long.wav --funasr-vad
asr-cli -e whispermlx a.wav            # default mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo
asr-cli -e qwen3 -l zh a.wav           # Qwen3-ASR, force Chinese
asr-cli -e mimo -l zh a.wav --json     # MiMo V2.5 ASR cloud
asr-cli --download-model               # SenseVoice model (sensevoice only)
asr-cli config                         # same JSON envelope as asr-rs config
asr-cli serve --socket ~/.cache/asr-cli/asr.sock --idle-unload-secs 0
asr-cli launchd install                # shares label com.junix.asr-cli
uv run asr-bench ~/mic.wav --runs 5 --json   # inference-only benchmark (load/warmup excluded)
```

- `qwen3` HF download honors `HF_ENDPOINT`; if a mirror fails, prefix
  `HF_ENDPOINT=https://huggingface.co`.
- `funasr`/`whispermlx`/`qwen3` weights auto-download on first use; only `sensevoice`
  uses `asr-cli --download-model`.
- Engine choice on the shared serve is fixed at startup (config/flags), same as `asr-rs`.

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/asr-rs` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/asr-rs/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/asr-rs/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/asr-rs/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/asr-rs/README.md` |
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/asr-cli` |
| 实现 | `~/projects/models/asr-cli/src/` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/asr-cli/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/asr-cli/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/asr-cli/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/use-asr/SKILL.md` |

name: use-awesome-commands
body:
# Use awesome commands (agent-friendly modern CLI)

Prefer these over classic Unix tools: they are faster, have sane defaults, and —
most importantly for an agent — emit **structured (JSON) output** you can pipe to
`jq` instead of parsing fragile text. Check availability with `command -v <tool>`;
if missing, fall back to the classic tool or `brew install <tool>`.

## The rule

**When output will be parsed or piped, reach for the tool with `--json` first.**
Brittle `awk`/`cut`/regex on human-formatted text is the main cause of agent
shell errors. JSON → `jq` is deterministic.

## Highest-value commands

| Task | Use (not the classic) | Agent tip |
|------|----------------------|-----------|
| Search code | `rg` (ripgrep) not `grep` | `rg --json PATTERN \| jq` for matches with line/col; respects `.gitignore` |
| Find files | `fd` not `find` | `fd -e py -0` → null-delimited for safe xargs |
| Read file | `bat` not `cat` | `bat -pp` for plain piped output; syntax + git gutter for humans |
| Parse JSON | `jq` | the backbone — extract/reshape every other tool's `--json` |
| Parse YAML/TOML | `yq` | `yq -o=json` to bridge into `jq` |
| List dir | `eza` not `ls` | `eza --tree --git`; for machine use prefer `fd` |
| Disk usage | `dust` not `du` | visual; for thresholds parse `du -k` or `dust -j` |
| Replace text | `sd` not `sed` | `sd 'old' 'new' file` — literal-friendly regex |
| Processes | `procs --json` not `ps` | structured process list |
| Benchmark | `hyperfine --export-json out.json CMD` | reliable timing, no `time` parsing |
| Count code | `tokei -o json` not `wc -l` | per-language LOC as JSON |
| HTTP | `xh` / `http` (HTTPie) not `curl` | JSON body by default; `xh` is the fast Rust one |
| Git diff view | `delta` / `difft` (difftastic) | syntax/structural diff for review |
| Fan out over dirs/inputs | `par` or GNU `parallel` | bounded concurrency; `par --json` for structured runs |
| Fuzzy filter | `fzf` | scriptable: `fzf -f QUERY` for non-interactive filtering |
| Jump to dir | `zoxide` (`z`) not `cd` | learns frecent dirs |

## Pipeline examples

```bash
# Every TODO with file:line, as JSON the agent can act on
rg --json 'TODO' | jq -r 'select(.type=="match") | "\(.data.path.text):\(.data.line_number)"'

# Flatten JSON so it's greppable, then restore (when no jq filter fits)
gron config.json | rg 'apiKey' ; gron -u <edited>     # if gron installed

# Benchmark two approaches, compare programmatically
hyperfine --export-json /tmp/b.json 'rg foo' 'grep -r foo .' && jq '.results[].mean' /tmp/b.json

# Largest source files by LOC
tokei -o json | jq -r '.[] | select(.children) | .children[].name' 2>/dev/null
```

## Reference lists (when a tool for the task isn't above)

Curated GitHub "awesome" lists — browse for a category-specific tool:

- `agarrharr/awesome-cli-apps` — broadest, organized by domain
- `spinov001-art/awesome-cli-tools-2026` — 50+ modern Rust/Go/Python tools
- `thegdsks/awesome-modern-cli` — modern replacements for classic Unix tools
- `alebcay/awesome-shell` — shell frameworks, toolkits, gizmos

For driving terminal code agents, see the `use-code-agents` skill; for fanning a
command across many directories, see `use-par`.

name: use-chatgpt-app
description: Use when the user wants to drive the official ChatGPT macOS desktop app (com.openai.chat) — activating the app, sending a regular or temporary chat, generating images, producing project infographics, saving the original image, copying response text, searching chat history, or reading basic settings. 在 macOS 上自动化操作官方 ChatGPT 桌面端时触发。Requires the desktop UI; not for web or API access.
body:
# Drive the ChatGPT macOS app

通过 `macbot` 把 ChatGPT.app（`com.openai.chat`）当作黑盒接口调用。每个具体动作都拆到 `references/` 下；这份 SKILL.md 只写心智模型、整套流程图、和最常见的踩坑速查。

## 设计原则

1. **所有操作必须幂等**：重复执行不破坏状态。
2. **每一步必须可验证**：截图 / 剪贴板 / 文件系统 / AX 读回。
3. **优先级心法**：菜单路径 / 快捷键 > AX 语义查询 > OCR 文字点击 > ground > 固定坐标。固定坐标只能从「当前窗口几何」或「刚截图 / AX 树」推导，不要跨会话缓存。
4. **失败必须可恢复**：所有错误场景有明确修复路径，不盲目重试。

## 关键事实（2026-05-19 实测）

- **顶层菜单**：`Apple / ChatGPT / 文件 / 编辑 / 显示 / Chats / 窗口 / 帮助`。
- **底部输入框**是 `AXTextArea`：`ui set` 直接生效，可 `return` 发送。
- **侧栏搜索框**是 `AXTextField`：**`ui set` 静默失败**（Electron 吞合成 value），必须走 `shift+cmd+f` 聚焦 + 剪贴板粘贴。
- **模型按钮**是底部 `AXButton`，`value` 形如 `5.5 Thinking` / `5.5 Pro` / `5.5 Instant` / `Auto`。
- **生成图片**不暴露为 `AXImage`：右键定位靠 `ground` 或 `grid`；菜单深路径 / 保存面板路径详见 `image-save.md`。
- **回复操作按钮**（复制 / 点赞 / 重生成）惰性渲染：**未滚到回复末尾就不在 DOM 里**——`ground` 找不到、OCR 搜不到「复制」二字。
- **`ui query --locator`** 在 ≥2026-05-19 的 macbot 二进制上稳定可用；旧二进制有 `MainActor.assumeIsolated` SIGTRAP 的 bug（exit 133，零字节输出）。`just install` 升级即可。
- **`macbot menu click`** 是规范子命令；`macbot menu-click` 仍作 deprecated 别名，新脚本一律用 `menu click`。
- **`macbot clipboard paste-text` / `paste --name <X>`** 是 ≥2026-05-19 的新粘贴原语：内部 `snapshot 当前 pasteboard → 写入内容 → ⌘V → 还原 snapshot`，**不污染用户原本复制的内容**，并把 set→⌘V 之间的窗口从秒级压到毫秒级。**所有把 prompt 送进 ChatGPT 输入框的脚本都应优先用这个**，旧的 `pbcopy + cmd+v` 留作 macbot 不可用时的回退。详见 `send-text.md` 路径 A / 路径 C。
- **JSON 信封**：所有命令输出 `{ok, ts, command, schemaVersion:"2", data, error}`。`ui set` 回读在 `data.verification.matched` + `data.verification.readBack`；`wait window-appear` 在 `data.lastObserved.pathHint` 给出稳定锚点；`clipboard paste` / `paste-text` 在 `data.outcome` 给出 `clobberedBeforeFire` / `retried` 等诊断。

## 前置条件

1. ChatGPT Desktop App 已安装并登录（未登录 UI 完全不同）。
2. macbot 已授权：辅助功能 + 屏幕录制权限。
3. 逐字键入建议英文输入法；中文 / 长文本 / 多行 / 代码块 / xpack 打包结果**一律走剪贴板粘贴**。

---

## 导航（按你想做什么找）

| 我要…… | 看 | 一句话摘要 |
|---|---|---|
| 把 ChatGPT 提到前台 | [`references/activation.md`](references/activation.md) | `count windows` → `open -b` → `app activate` → `assert frontmost`；**长流程每段键盘事件前都要重做**，焦点会漂走 |
| 开普通 / 临时新聊天 | [`references/new-chat.md`](references/new-chat.md) | `cmd+n` vs `shift+cmd+n`；输入框残留必须 `cmd+a` 后再粘贴 |
| 切模型（Pro / Thinking / Instant / 旧模型） | [`references/model-selection.md`](references/model-selection.md) | locator `containsAny` 读 / 打开弹层 → OCR 点击 → `suffix` 读回 |
| 发提示词（剪贴板 / `ui set`） | [`references/send-text.md`](references/send-text.md) | `ui set` 适用矩阵；何时回退剪贴板 |
| 取回复正文 | [`references/copy-response.md`](references/copy-response.md) | 用 `mouse scroll --dy` 滚到末尾（**Cmd+Down 不工作**）→ triple-click + Cmd+A + Cmd+C 拿全文；ground 复制按钮是备用 |
| 生成图片 | [`references/image-generate.md`](references/image-generate.md) | 必须用普通新聊天；项目信息图工作流 |
| 保存生成图片到磁盘 | [`references/image-save.md`](references/image-save.md) | 右键图片中心 → `将图片另存为…` → 保存面板 → 替换 / 验证 |
| 找历史聊天 | [`references/history-search.md`](references/history-search.md) | `shift+cmd+f` + 剪贴板 + OCR 点击；侧栏 `ui set` 静默失败 |
| 用菜单 / 快捷键操作 | [`references/menu-and-shortcuts.md`](references/menu-and-shortcuts.md) | 顶层菜单清单 + 全局快捷键速查；`menu click` 规范子命令 |
| 大区域点击不想调 `ground` / 复核 `ground` 结果 | [`references/grid-and-mark.md`](references/grid-and-mark.md) | grid 旁路（无候选）vs mark 自验证（有候选） |
| 用 ChatGPT 做代码评审（xpack） | [`references/code-review-via-xpack.md`](references/code-review-via-xpack.md) | xpack 形态速查 + 9 步评审流程 + 多 feature 串行的激活纪律 |
| 看完整错误恢复表 | [`references/error-recovery.md`](references/error-recovery.md) | 全部症状 → 根因 → 处置矩阵 |

---

## 快速路径选择

| 任务 | 首选路径 | 必须验证 |
|---|---|---|
| 文本问答 / 探测 | 临时聊天 (`shift+cmd+n`) + `clipboard paste-text` 安全粘贴 | 最新回复出现，需要原文时走 `copy-response.md` |
| 图片生成 | 普通新聊天 (`cmd+n`) + `clipboard paste-text` 安全粘贴 | 截图看到「图片已创建」或图片本体 |
| 保存生成图片 | 右键图片本体 → `将图片另存为…` | 保存 sheet 关闭，`file` 显示 PNG |
| 复制回复文本 | 先滚到回复末尾，再点回复下方复制按钮 | `pbpaste` 内容匹配预期 |
| 查找历史聊天 | `shift+cmd+f` + `clipboard paste-text` + OCR 点击 | 截图确认打开目标聊天 |
| 代码评审 | `xpack ... \| macbot clipboard set --name review --stdin` → 普通聊天 + 切 Pro + `clipboard paste --name review` | 详见 `code-review-via-xpack.md` 第 9 步 |

---

## Top 7 踩坑速查

完整表见 [`references/error-recovery.md`](references/error-recovery.md)。

1. **长流程里焦点漂走，键盘事件落到错的 App** → 整套 review 持续几十分钟到几小时，你自己跑的 bash 命令、`swift build`、`git commit`、`just install` 都会把焦点抢去 Terminal/Ghostty。`cmd+v` 会把 30KB xpack 内容粘到 shell 当命令执行。**每段会触发键盘事件 / 合成 `Cmd+V` / 鼠标点击 ChatGPT 内部的代码前都要 `macbot app activate --bundle com.openai.chat && macbot assert app --frontmost`**。`screenshot` 不依赖 frontmost，所以「截图能拍到 ChatGPT」≠「键盘事件会进 ChatGPT」。详见 [`references/activation.md`](references/activation.md) 的「焦点会漂走」节。
2. **`Cmd+Down` / `PageDown` / `End` 不滚动 ChatGPT** → Electron WebView 吞掉这些按键，焦点元素变了但视口不动。**必须用合成 wheel 事件**：`macbot mouse scroll --dy <px> -- <x> <y>`。`--dy 100000` 单次直达底部，`--dy=-100000`（等号形式）直达顶部。
3. **`macbot mouse scroll --down 20 --lines -- <x> <y>` 实际滚 0 像素** → `--down` / `--up` / `--lines` 在有位置参数（坐标）时绑不到 dy，命令成功但 `dy: 0`。统一用 `--dy <pixels>`。**所有 flag 必须在 `--` 之前，坐标在 `--` 之后**。
4. **`ground` / OCR 找不到「复制」按钮** → 回复正文没滚到末尾，操作按钮没渲染到 DOM。先用上面 #2 的 wheel 事件滚到底；取响应**优先用 triple-click + `Cmd+A` + `Cmd+C`**（实测最稳），ground 复制按钮经常返回错坐标。详见 [`references/copy-response.md`](references/copy-response.md) 方案 A。
5. **`macbot app activate` 后没窗口可截图** → 早先 `cmd+w` 关掉了所有窗口。先 `osascript ... count windows` 看是不是 0，是就 `open -b com.openai.chat && sleep 1` 再 activate。
6. **`macbot mouse click -1440 515` 报 `Unknown option '-1440'`** → 副屏 ChatGPT 窗口 x<0，ArgumentParser 把负数当 flag。坐标前加 `--`：`macbot mouse click -- -1440 515`。同理 `mouse scroll`、`mouse move`、`mouse drag`。
7. **`ui query --locator` 静默 exit 133 + 零字节输出** → 旧 macbot 二进制 `MainActor.assumeIsolated` SIGTRAP。`cd ~/projects/models/macbot && just install` 升级到 ≥2026-05-19。

---

## 注意事项

1. **所有交互操作前重做 `app activate` + `assert app --frontmost` 三连击**。短流程合并到一条 bash 行里没问题；**长流程（多次等回复、多 feature 串行评审）每次发键盘事件前都要重新激活**——别的 App 会在等待期间抢焦点。`screenshot` 不要求 frontmost；`keyboard hotkey / tap / type`、`clipboard paste`、`osascript keystroke` 全部要求 frontmost。
2. **滚动 ChatGPT 必须用合成 wheel 事件** (`macbot mouse scroll --dy <px>`)，键盘快捷键 `Cmd+Down` / `PageDown` 不会触发滚动。`--down` / `--up` / `--lines` 在有位置参数时绑定失败；统一用 `--dy <pixels>`，正向下、负向上（负值要 `--dy=-N` 等号形式）。
3. **Retina 屏不需要手动转换坐标**，`ground --screen` 返回的 `screen_logical` 可以直接喂 `mouse click`。
4. **保存生成图片**最终交付走右键 → `将图片另存为…`；区域截图只用作预览。
5. **保存面板按 Return** 不一定等价于保存；焦点可能在文件名输入框，或有同名确认，必须显式点 `保存` 或 `替换` 并验证面板关闭。
6. **图片默认 PNG**；需要其他格式后续 `convert` / `sips`。
7. **图片生成必须用普通新聊天** `cmd+n`；临时聊天不允许调图片工具。
8. **「Thinking」卡住直接新建聊天重试**，不要重发污染上下文。
9. **中文 / 长文本 / 多行提示词走 `macbot clipboard paste-text` 安全粘贴**（不污染用户剪贴板，无 set→⌘V 竞态），同一段 prompt 多轮复用走 `clipboard set --name X` + `clipboard paste --name X`。`macbot keyboard type` 不要作为主路径；旧 `pbcopy + cmd+v` 仅作 macbot 不可用时的回退。
10. **xpack 默认 token 上限可能不够**——多文件横切评审建议 `--max-tokens 100000` 起步，看到 `[Output truncated due to global budget limit]` 才缩 glob。注意源码里也可能出现 "truncated" 字面量，所以 grep 验证截断时要看完整 sentinel 串，不只是单词。

## References

改 CLI、契约或实现时，先改下列 `~/projects/<lang>/<repo>/` 路径，再同步本 skill 正文。

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| 仓库根 | `~/projects/models/macbot` |
| 构建 / 测试 | `~/projects/models/macbot/justfile` |
| 项目 Agent 说明 | `~/projects/models/macbot/CLAUDE.md` |
| 人类文档 | `~/projects/models/macbot/README.md` |
| 本 skill | `~/projects/skills/use-chatgpt-app/SKILL.md` |

name: use-code-agents
description: Use when the user wants to run, compare, or script terminal code agents — GitHub Copilot CLI (`copilot`), Cursor Agent (`cursor-agent`), Claude Code wrappers (`glmcc` for Zhipu/GLM, `arkcc` for Volcano Engine/Doubao, `deepseekcc` for DeepSeek, or stock `claude`), OpenAI Codex CLI (`codex`), or `opencode`; asks how to choose one; needs the right flags for model selection, working directory, approvals, tool permissions, sandbox/worktree, non-interactive scripting, headless Agent Skills triggering (`/skillname` for Claude Code wrappers, `$mention` for Codex), `/goal` long-horizon autonomous loops, `--mode autopilot` self-judged loops, or Claude Code plugins; or wants to capture the agent's execution trace (tool calls, reasoning, intermediate decisions) rather than just the final answer.
body:
# Using Code Agents

Pick one CLI first. Then give a concrete command with the correct model, directory, and approval flags. For deep details (every flag, every gotcha, every event field), follow the index to the per-CLI reference file.

## Router

Pick by **the strongest reason** to use this one rather than any other:

| Pick | when you want |
|---|---|
| `copilot` | the strongest explicit allow/deny controls per tool / path / URL, or a GitHub-native workflow |
| `cursor-agent` | git-worktree isolation (`-w`), read-only `plan` / `ask` modes, or Cursor-specific models like `composer-2.5-fast` |
| a Claude Code wrapper (`glmcc` / `arkcc` / `deepseekcc` / stock `claude`) | the richest flag surface and the richest trace output (hooks, partial messages, structured `result`, JSON-schema-constrained answers); plus Claude Code plugins, custom sub-agents, and MCP-heavy workflows. Pick the wrapper by vendor — they share 100% of the flag surface and only differ in default model and API endpoint. |
| `opencode` | TUI/server/web modes, provider-routed model IDs (`provider/model`), or a session you can share/attach from multiple clients |

If multiple apply, prefer the one whose **failure mode** you can debug fastest — for example `copilot` for compliance-sensitive runs (clearest permission errors), a Claude Code wrapper for tracing-heavy benchmarking (richest event stream).

### Picking by speed vs. task complexity

Independent of the router above, performance on real work tends to fall into three buckets:

| You need | Pick |
|---|---|
| **Fastest iteration** — many small tasks, quick feedback loops | `cursor-agent --model composer-2.5-fast` — fastest of any agent covered here |
| **Hardest tasks** — complex bugs, multi-file refactors, deep reasoning where correctness beats speed | OpenAI **Codex CLI** (`codex exec ...`) — strongest on complex / open-ended problems. See `references/codex.md` for full flag surface, sandbox modes, skill triggering, and `/goal` setup. |
| **Middle ground** — capable across most tasks, no standout extreme | `copilot`, Claude Code wrappers (`glmcc` / `arkcc` / `deepseekcc`), `opencode` |

When in doubt: start with the middle-ground option whose router row best matches the task; switch up to Codex if it gets stuck on something genuinely hard, or down to `composer-2.5-fast` if latency dominates correctness.

### Picking by autonomous / long-horizon need

Three CLIs can "set it and walk away," but with different stop semantics — pick by **how strict you need the finish line to be**.

| Stop semantics | Pick |
|---|---|
| **Externally judged** — separate evaluator model reads the transcript every turn against a user-written verifiable predicate; agent stops when the condition holds | **Codex CLI** `/goal <objective>` (experimental — enable `features.goals = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml` or via `/experimental`; subcommands: `/goal` to view, `/goal pause`, `/goal resume`, `/goal clear`) — **or** a Claude Code wrapper (`glmcc` / `arkcc` / `deepseekcc`) `/goal <condition>` (GA in Claude Code 2.1.139+; default evaluator is a Haiku-class model; stop with `/goal clear` or aliases `stop` / `off` / `reset` / `none` / `cancel`) |
| **Self-judged** — the acting agent decides itself when the initial task is complete; capped by a continuation counter | **`copilot --mode autopilot`** (shorthand `--autopilot`). Cap loop length with `--max-autopilot-continues <N>` (default `5`). No separate finish-condition predicate — quality is whatever the agent thinks "done" means. |

Externally-judged `/goal` is harder to fool — the evaluator only judges what's in the transcript, so write conditions bound to a verifiable command output (e.g. "`pytest` exits 0 with no uncommitted changes", "`CHANGELOG.md` lists every merged PR this week"). Bad fit for open-ended research, calendar-driven waits, or anything where the agent might "succeed silently" without printing evidence.

Self-judged autopilot is fine for short, well-scoped tasks where you trust the agent's own completion sense and just want it to not stop for approval on every tool call. Always set `--max-autopilot-continues` in CI / unattended contexts.

`cursor-agent` and `opencode` have no equivalent at time of writing — `cursor-agent --force` and `opencode --dangerously-skip-permissions` only auto-approve tool calls within a single turn; they don't drive a multi-turn loop toward a finish line.

### Brainstorming with multiple agents in parallel

When the answer isn't known up front — exploring approaches, gathering multiple perspectives, evaluating competing hypotheses, or grading the same task from independent angles — fan out across CLIs (or across multiple instances of one CLI) in parallel and synthesize the outputs.

```bash
# Pattern A — same prompt, different agents → triangulate by model
PROMPT="research approaches to X and recommend one with tradeoffs"
REPO=.

(copilot   -p "$PROMPT" --model gpt-5.5         -C "$REPO" --allow-all-tools --mode plan      > /tmp/out.copilot  2>&1) &
(cursor-agent -p        --model composer-2.5-fast --workspace "$REPO" --mode ask --trust "$PROMPT" > /tmp/out.cursor   2>&1) &
(cd "$REPO" && deepseekcc -p "$PROMPT"          --permission-mode plan                          > /tmp/out.deepseek 2>&1) &
(codex exec "$PROMPT"   -C "$REPO" -s read-only --skip-git-repo-check                           > /tmp/out.codex    2>&1) &
wait
```

```bash
# Pattern B — same agent, different ideas → parallel hypothesis testing
for idea in idea1 idea2 idea3; do
  (codex exec "deeply research: $idea" -s read-only --skip-git-repo-check > "/tmp/out.$idea" 2>&1) &
done
wait
```

After `wait` returns, synthesize: read each output, weight findings by how many agents independently arrived at them, flag disagreements as decision points, and prefer the agent with the most relevant strengths (Codex for hardest analyses, `composer-2.5-fast` for fast scanning, `copilot` for compliance-shaped checks, Claude Code wrappers for richest reasoning trace).

Best when:
- You want perspective diversity — different models have different biases, so consensus across them is a stronger signal than any one
- The problem is open-ended (no canonical answer)
- Outputs are cheap to cross-check

Avoid when:
- One agent is clearly best for the task (use the pickers above)
- Parallel runs need to coordinate — they can't see each other's work
- Cost matters more than wall-clock time (N× single-run cost; wall-clock is the slowest agent)

Tip: capture each agent's exit code separately (`wait $PID_A; STATUS_A=$?`) so one agent's failure doesn't poison the synthesis.

### Parallel editing — prefer Git worktrees

The patterns above are safe for **read-only / research** runs — no files change, no conflicts possible. For runs that **edit code**, analyze the task first: if the parallel agents may touch overlapping files, isolate each one with `git worktree` so they can't clobber each other's changes.

1. Create one worktree per agent on its own branch:
   ```bash
   git worktree add ../work-A -b agent-A
   git worktree add ../work-B -b agent-B
   ```
2. Point each agent at its own worktree, using that CLI's cwd flag:
   - `copilot -C ../work-A …`
   - `cursor-agent --workspace ../work-A …` (or `cursor-agent -w` for its built-in worktree mode)
   - `(cd ../work-A && glmcc -p "…")` for Claude Code wrappers
   - `codex exec "…" -C ../work-A`
3. Review the resulting branches and merge / cherry-pick the winner(s); drop the worktrees with `git worktree remove`.

This is advisory, not a hard rule — skip the worktree dance for read-only or research tasks where it would just be overhead.

## Where to look

| If you want to… | Read |
|---|---|
| Run `copilot` — flags, permissions, modes (plan / interactive / autopilot), MCP toolsets, trace flags, jq paths, gotchas | `references/copilot.md` |
| Run `cursor-agent` — workspace/worktree, sandbox, sessions, trace flags, jq paths, gotchas | `references/cursor-agent.md` |
| Run a Claude Code wrapper (`glmcc` / `arkcc` / `deepseekcc` / stock `claude`) — permission modes, tool allow/disallow, Claude-Code session flags, trace flags incl. hook events, **Agent Skills + `/skillname` headless trigger**, **Claude Code plugins**, jq paths, gotchas. **Plus**: vendor comparison table (default model, API env var, endpoint), how to write your own wrapper for a new vendor. | `references/claude-code-wrappers.md` |
| Run `codex` (OpenAI Codex CLI) — `codex exec` flag surface, sandbox modes, **Agent Skills + `$mention` trigger**, **`/goal` long-horizon loops with `features.goals`**, trace capture, common gotchas | `references/codex.md` |
| Run `opencode` — provider/model id format, subcommands, session export, trace flags, jq paths, gotchas | `references/opencode.md` |
| Capture an execution trace from any of the four — flag matrix, live tail pattern, common jq analyses, normalize-and-compare recipe, which channel to capture (stdout JSONL vs disk log vs session export) | `references/tracing.md` |

## Common error use cases (cross-CLI)

These are the things that actually break headless runs. Recognize the symptom, jump to the named reference.

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Command hangs forever on first tool call | Missing the agent's "approve everything" flag | copilot: `--allow-all-tools`. cursor-agent: `--force` / `--yolo`.
